---
name: generic-ux-designer
description: Professional UX expertise for any project type. Covers user research, design thinking, interaction patterns, accessibility, and design critique. Use when designing new features, improving UX, conducting design reviews, or evaluating user flows.
---

# Generic UX Designer

Professional UX expertise that adapts to any project's constraints and user needs.

## Design Thinking Process

### 1. Empathize
- Understand user needs
- Identify pain points
- Map user journeys
- Research competitors

### 2. Define
- Problem statements
- User personas
- Success metrics
- Constraints

### 3. Ideate
- Brainstorm solutions
- Explore alternatives
- Consider edge cases
- Sketch concepts

### 4. Prototype
- Low-fidelity first
- Test assumptions
- Iterate quickly
- Validate with users

### 5. Test
- Usability testing
- A/B testing
- Analytics review
- Iterate based on data

## User Psychology Principles

### Cognitive Load
- Reduce choices (Hick's Law)
- Chunk information (Miller's Law)
- Use progressive disclosure
- Minimize user memory load

### Visual Hierarchy
- Size indicates importance
- Contrast draws attention
- Whitespace groups elements
- F-pattern/Z-pattern reading

### Behavioral Patterns
- Users scan, not read
- Familiarity breeds trust
- Immediate feedback essential
- Error prevention > recovery

### Emotional Design
- Delight in micro-interactions
- Personality through copy
- Trust through transparency
- Satisfaction through completion

## Interaction Patterns

### Navigation

| Pattern | Use Case |
|---------|----------|
| Top nav | Simple sites, < 7 items |
| Side nav | Complex apps, many sections |
| Bottom nav | Mobile apps, < 5 items |
| Hamburger | Mobile, space-constrained |
| Tabs | Related content sections |
| Breadcrumbs | Deep hierarchies |

### Forms

| Pattern | Benefit |
|---------|---------|
| Single column | Faster completion |
| Inline validation | Immediate feedback |
| Field grouping | Logical flow |
| Smart defaults | Reduced effort |
| Progress indicator | Clear expectations |
| Error summary | Easy correction |

### Data Display

| Pattern | Use Case |
|---------|----------|
| Cards | Browsable content |
| Tables | Comparable data |
| Lists | Sequential items |
| Timeline | Chronological events |
| Dashboard | At-a-glance metrics |
| Search + filters | Large datasets |

### Feedback

| Pattern | Use Case |
|---------|----------|
| Toast | Non-blocking info |
| Modal | Blocking/important |
| Inline | Contextual feedback |
| Banner | Global notifications |
| Progress | Long operations |
| Skeleton | Loading states |

## Mobile UX Considerations

### Touch Targets
- Minimum 44x44px
- 8px spacing between targets
- Thumb-friendly placement
- Bottom sheet for actions

### Gestures
- Tap: Primary action
- Swipe: Navigate/reveal
- Long press: Secondary actions
- Pinch: Zoom (media)

### Mobile Patterns
- Bottom navigation
- Pull to refresh
- Infinite scroll (carefully)
- Full-screen modals
- Native keyboard handling

## Accessibility (UX Perspective)

### Inclusive Design
- Design for extremes, works for all
- Consider temporary disabilities
- Support different input methods
- Test with real users

### Key Considerations

| Aspect | UX Approach |
|--------|-------------|
| Vision | High contrast, scalable text, alt text |
| Motor | Large targets, keyboard nav, no timing |
| Cognitive | Simple language, clear structure |
| Hearing | Captions, visual indicators |

### Testing
- Screen reader walkthrough
- Keyboard-only navigation
- Color blindness simulation
- Reduced motion mode

## Design Critique Framework

### Criteria

| Aspect | Questions |
|--------|-----------|
| Usability | Can users complete tasks? |
| Clarity | Is purpose immediately clear? |
| Consistency | Follows established patterns? |
| Hierarchy | Most important stands out? |
| Feedback | Users know system state? |
| Error handling | Recoverable mistakes? |
| Accessibility | Works for everyone? |

### Constructive Feedback

**Instead of:** "This is confusing"
**Say:** "Users might not understand X because Y. Consider Z."

**Instead of:** "I don't like this"
**Say:** "This pattern may cause issues because... Alternative approach..."

## Common UX Patterns

### Onboarding
1. Progressive disclosure
2. Contextual tips
3. Interactive tutorials
4. Skip option always
5. Revisitable help

### Empty States
1. Explain what goes here
2. Guide to first action
3. Show example content
4. Make it delightful

### Loading States
1. Skeleton screens > spinners
2. Progress indicators for long ops
3. Optimistic UI when safe
4. Background loading when possible

### Error States
1. Explain what happened
2. Suggest how to fix
3. Provide recovery action
4. Don't blame user

### Success States
1. Confirm action completed
2. Show next steps
3. Celebrate achievements
4. Enable sharing if appropriate

## Content Strategy

### Microcopy
- Be concise
- Use active voice
- Avoid jargon
- Be human
- Guide action

### Labels & Instructions
- Clear and specific
- Explain why (if needed)
- Example formats
- Required vs optional

### Error Messages
- Specific problem
- How to fix
- No technical jargon
- Apologetic when appropriate

## Metrics That Matter

### Engagement
- Time on task
- Completion rate
- Return visits
- Feature adoption

### Satisfaction
- NPS score
- CSAT surveys
- Support tickets
- User feedback

### Performance
- Task success rate
- Error rate
- Time to first action
- Abandonment points

## Design Review Checklist

### Functionality
- [ ] Primary task achievable
- [ ] Edge cases handled
- [ ] Error states designed
- [ ] Loading states designed

### Usability
- [ ] Clear visual hierarchy
- [ ] Obvious next actions
- [ ] Consistent with patterns
- [ ] Minimal cognitive load

### Accessibility
- [ ] Keyboard navigable
- [ ] Screen reader tested
- [ ] Color contrast sufficient
- [ ] Touch targets sized

### Polish
- [ ] Micro-interactions smooth
- [ ] Copy clear and helpful
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints
- [ ] Performance acceptable

## See Also

- Project-specific UX skills for detailed requirements
- `.claude/skills/generic-design-system/` - Visual design
- `CLAUDE.md` - Project constraints

1→---
     2→name: generic-code-reviewer
     3→description: Review code for bugs, security vulnerabilities, performance issues, accessibility gaps, and CLAUDE.md workflow compliance. Supports any tech stack - HTML/CSS/JS, React, TypeScript, Node.js, Python, NestJS, Next.js, and more. Use when completing features, before commits, or reviewing pull requests.
     4→---
     5→
     6→# Generic Code Reviewer
     7→
     8→Review code against development principles, CLAUDE.md workflow, and production quality standards. Adapts to any project's tech stack.
     9→
    10→## CLAUDE.md Workflow Compliance
    11→
    12→**Always check the project's CLAUDE.md file for specific rules.**
    13→
    14→**Before ANY Commit:**
    15→- Task file exists in `tasks/[feature-name].md`
    16→- Task file has comprehensive review section
    17→- All tests passing
    18→- Type checking passing (if TypeScript)
    19→- No console errors/warnings
    20→- Bundle size within limits
    21→
    22→## Tech Stack Detection
    23→
    24→Automatically adapt checks based on project:
    25→
    26→| Detection | Tech Stack | Key Checks |
    27→|-----------|------------|------------|
    28→| `package.json` with React | React/TypeScript | Components, hooks, state |
    29→| `package.json` with Next.js | Next.js | SSR, API routes, routing |
    30→| `package.json` with NestJS | NestJS | Guards, DTOs, services |
    31→| `.html` files, no build | Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS | Semantic HTML, minimal JS |
    32→| `requirements.txt` or `.py` | Python | Type hints, validation |
    33→
    34→## Security Checks
    35→
    36→### Frontend
    37→- Sanitize all user input (use textContent, not innerHTML)
    38→- Use `unknown` type for external data, validate before using
    39→- No dynamic code execution (eval, Function constructor)
    40→- No exposed API keys in client code
    41→- HTTPS for all external requests
    42→
    43→### Backend
    44→- Input validation on all endpoints
    45→- Authentication on protected routes
    46→- No raw SQL queries (use ORM/parameterized)
    47→- Secrets in environment variables
    48→- Rate limiting on public endpoints
    49→
    50→### General
    51→- No tracking without user consent
    52→- Privacy-first data handling
    53→- WebCrypto API for encryption (no custom crypto)
    54→
    55→## Performance Requirements
    56→
    57→### Bundle/Page Size
    58→| Project Type | Target |
    59→|--------------|--------|
    60→| Static site | < 50KB (excluding media) |
    61→| SPA/React | < 100KB gzipped initial |
    62→| Full-stack app | < 200KB gzipped initial |
    63→
    64→### Animation Performance
    65→- GPU-accelerated only (transform, opacity)
    66→- 60fps target
    67→- Avoid layout thrashing
    68→- Use requestAnimationFrame
    69→
    70→### Loading
    71→- Lazy load non-essential code/images
    72→- Code splitting for large features
    73→- Optimize images (WebP, responsive)
    74→
    75→## Accessibility (WCAG AA)
    76→
    77→### Required
    78→- Focus indicators on all interactive elements
    79→- Keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter, Escape, Arrows)
    80→- Color contrast >= 4.5:1 for text
    81→- ARIA labels on icon-only buttons
    82→- Alt text for all meaningful images
    83→- Semantic HTML structure
    84→
    85→### Interactive Elements
    86→- Focus trapped in modals
    87→- Escape key closes dialogs
    88→- Skip links for navigation
    89→- Form error messages announced
    90→
    91→## Code Quality
    92→
    93→### General Principles
    94→- DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself)
    95→- Single Responsibility
    96→- Explicit over implicit
    97→- No magic numbers/strings
    98→- Self-documenting code
    99→
   100→### HTML
   101→- Semantic tags (`<nav>`, `<main>`, `<article>`, not just `<div>`)
   102→- Proper heading hierarchy
   103→- Self-closing tags for void elements
   104→- Meaningful class names
   105→
   106→### CSS
   107→- Mobile-first approach
   108→- Avoid `!important`
   109→- CSS custom properties for reusable values
   110→- Consistent naming convention (BEM or similar)
   111→- Comment complex animations
   112→
   113→### JavaScript/TypeScript
   114→- ES6+ syntax (const, let, arrow functions)
   115→- TypeScript strict mode when available

file.content:
---
name: generic-code-reviewer
description: Review code for bugs, security vulnerabilities, performance issues, accessibility gaps, and CLAUDE.md workflow compliance. Supports any tech stack - HTML/CSS/JS, React, TypeScript, Node.js, Python, NestJS, Next.js, and more. Use when completing features, before commits, or reviewing pull requests.
---

# Generic Code Reviewer

Review code against development principles, CLAUDE.md workflow, and production quality standards. Adapts to any project's tech stack.

## CLAUDE.md Workflow Compliance

**Always check the project's CLAUDE.md file for specific rules.**

**Before ANY Commit:**
- Task file exists in `tasks/[feature-name].md`
- Task file has comprehensive review section
- All tests passing
- Type checking passing (if TypeScript)
- No console errors/warnings
- Bundle size within limits

## Tech Stack Detection

Automatically adapt checks based on project:

| Detection | Tech Stack | Key Checks |
|-----------|------------|------------|
| `package.json` with React | React/TypeScript | Components, hooks, state |
| `package.json` with Next.js | Next.js | SSR, API routes, routing |
| `package.json` with NestJS | NestJS | Guards, DTOs, services |
| `.html` files, no build | Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS | Semantic HTML, minimal JS |
| `requirements.txt` or `.py` | Python | Type hints, validation |

## Security Checks

### Frontend
- Sanitize all user input (use textContent, not innerHTML)
- Use `unknown` type for external data, validate before using
- No dynamic code execution (eval, Function constructor)
- No exposed API keys in client code
- HTTPS for all external requests

### Backend
- Input validation on all endpoints
- Authentication on protected routes
- No raw SQL queries (use ORM/parameterized)
- Secrets in environment variables
- Rate limiting on public endpoints

### General
- No tracking without user consent
- Privacy-first data handling
- WebCrypto API for encryption (no custom crypto)

## Performance Requirements

### Bundle/Page Size
| Project Type | Target |
|--------------|--------|
| Static site | < 50KB (excluding media) |
| SPA/React | < 100KB gzipped initial |
| Full-stack app | < 200KB gzipped initial |

### Animation Performance
- GPU-accelerated only (transform, opacity)
- 60fps target
- Avoid layout thrashing
- Use requestAnimationFrame

### Loading
- Lazy load non-essential code/images
- Code splitting for large features
- Optimize images (WebP, responsive)

## Accessibility (WCAG AA)

### Required
- Focus indicators on all interactive elements
- Keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter, Escape, Arrows)
- Color contrast >= 4.5:1 for text
- ARIA labels on icon-only buttons
- Alt text for all meaningful images
- Semantic HTML structure

### Interactive Elements
- Focus trapped in modals
- Escape key closes dialogs
- Skip links for navigation
- Form error messages announced

## Code Quality

### General Principles
- DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself)
- Single Responsibility
- Explicit over implicit
- No magic numbers/strings
- Self-documenting code

### HTML
- Semantic tags (`<nav>`, `<main>`, `<article>`, not just `<div>`)
- Proper heading hierarchy
- Self-closing tags for void elements
- Meaningful class names

### CSS
- Mobile-first approach
- Avoid `!important`
- CSS custom properties for reusable values
- Consistent naming convention (BEM or similar)
- Comment complex animations

### JavaScript/TypeScript
- ES6+ syntax (const, let, arrow functions)
- TypeScript strict mode when available
- No `any` types for user data
- Async/await over raw promises
- Event delegation where appropriate
- Defensive programming (null checks)

### Python
- Type hints on functions
- Docstrings for public functions
- PEP 8 style compliance
- Context managers for resources
- Proper exception handling

## Design System Compliance

Check project's design system for:
- Color palette adherence
- Typography consistency
- Spacing system (4px, 8px base)
- Component patterns
- Dark/light mode support

## Testing Requirements

### Minimum Coverage
- Critical paths tested
- Edge cases covered
- Error states handled
- Accessibility tested

### Test Types
| Type | When |
|------|------|
| Unit | Business logic, utilities |
| Integration | API endpoints, data flow |
| E2E | Critical user journeys |

## Surgical Simplicity

Changes should impact minimal code relevant to task only.

- Every line intentional
- No commented-out code
- No dead code
- Clean, documented, maintainable
- Follow existing patterns

## Review Checklist

### Pre-Commit
- [ ] Task file updated
- [ ] Tests pass
- [ ] Type check passes (if TS)
- [ ] Lint passes
- [ ] Build succeeds
- [ ] No console errors

### Security
- [ ] Input sanitized
- [ ] Auth on protected routes
- [ ] No exposed secrets
- [ ] Privacy respected

### Performance
- [ ] Bundle size acceptable
- [ ] No layout thrashing
- [ ] Images optimized
- [ ] Lazy loading used

### Accessibility
- [ ] Keyboard navigable
- [ ] Focus indicators
- [ ] Contrast sufficient
- [ ] ARIA labels present

### Quality
- [ ] Code follows patterns
- [ ] No magic values
- [ ] Proper error handling
- [ ] Self-documenting

## See Also

- `CLAUDE.md` - Project workflow rules
- `.claude/workflow-checklist.md` - Complete checklist
- Project-specific code-reviewer skills for detailed requirements

# Optimize .claude/ Folder - Context Reduction Plan

**Goal:** Reduce context consumption while preserving all functionality
**Constraint:** No deletions - only restructuring and condensing

---

## Executive Summary

### Current State
- **Total SKILL.md content:** ~290 KB across 25 files (~8,600 lines)
- **Largest files:** example-react-app-ux-designer (32KB/1,206 lines), document-skills/pptx (25.5KB), example-react-app-design-system (24KB)
- **Key finding:** ~50-60% redundancy across similar skill types

### Target Outcome
- **Estimated reduction:** 25-35% (~70-100 KB savings)
- **Method:** Extract shared patterns, eliminate redundancy, tighten prose
- **Preserved:** All functionality, all knowledge, all examples

---

## Phase 1: Create Shared Reference Library

Create `C:\Users\tjn\.claude\skills\_shared\` directory with extracted common patterns.

### 1.1 Create CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md (~80 lines)
Extract from 4 code-reviewer skills:
- Security checks (Frontend/Backend/General)
- Performance requirements table
- Accessibility (WCAG AA) requirements
- Review checklist template

**Files affected:**
- `generic-code-reviewer/SKILL.md`
- `example-react-app-code-reviewer/SKILL.md`
- `example-fullstack-code-reviewer/SKILL.md`
- `example-static-site-code-reviewer/SKILL.md`

**Estimated savings:** 300+ lines across 4 files

### 1.2 Create DESIGN_PATTERNS.md (~100 lines)
Extract from design system and UX skills:
- Typography scale table
- Spacing system (4px/8px base)
- Color theory principles
- Visual hierarchy patterns
- Breakpoint table

**Files affected:**
- `generic-design-system/SKILL.md`
- `generic-ux-designer/SKILL.md`
- `frontend-enhancer/SKILL.md`
- All example-*-design-system skills

**Estimated savings:** 200+ lines across 6 files

### 1.3 Create UX_PRINCIPLES.md (~120 lines)
Extract from UX designer skills:
- Hick's Law, Fitts's Law, Jakob's Law
- Cognitive load (Miller's Law)
- Design Thinking framework (5 steps)
- User psychology principles
- Interaction patterns tables

**Files affected:**
- `generic-ux-designer/SKILL.md`
- `example-react-app-ux-designer/SKILL.md`
- `example-fullstack-ux-designer/SKILL.md`
- `example-static-site-ux-designer/SKILL.md`

**Estimated savings:** 400+ lines across 4 files

---

## Phase 2: Condense Verbose Skills

### 2.1 example-react-app-ux-designer.md (1,206 → ~350 lines)
Current state: Contains generic UX frameworks that belong in generic-ux-designer

**Changes:**
- Remove Design Thinking framework (use `@import _shared/UX_PRINCIPLES.md`)
- Remove Visual Design Principles section (generic)
- Remove User Psychology section (use shared reference)
- Remove Accessibility section (use CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md)
- **Keep:** Neuman Brain specific constraints, brand colors, project-specific examples

**Estimated savings:** ~850 lines

### 2.2 tech-debt-analyzer.md (619 → ~400 lines)
Current state: Contains theoretical tool descriptions for non-existent scripts

**Changes:**
- Remove detailed Python script descriptions (tools don't exist yet)
- Condense "Success Metrics" from narrative to bullet list
- Tighten workflow explanations
- Keep practical analysis steps and patterns

**Estimated savings:** ~220 lines

### 2.3 test-specialist.md (566 → ~350 lines)
Current state: Heavy language-specific examples

**Changes:**
- Reduce code examples from 15+ to 5-7 representative ones
- Remove language-specific details (generalize)
- Keep core testing strategies
- Reference tech-specific patterns rather than embedding

**Estimated savings:** ~215 lines

### 2.4 example-react-app-design-system.md (871 → ~400 lines)
Current state: Repeats generic design patterns

**Changes:**
- Reference DESIGN_PATTERNS.md for shared patterns
- Keep only Neuman Brain specific tokens and components
- Reduce code examples to essential patterns

**Estimated savings:** ~470 lines

### 2.5 frontend-enhancer.md (335 → ~200 lines)
Current state: Overlaps with design system skills

**Changes:**
- Reference shared design patterns
- Focus on enhancement workflow, not fundamentals
- Tighten prose

**Estimated savings:** ~135 lines

---

## Phase 3: Standardize Skill Structure

### 3.1 Implement Inheritance Model
Add front matter indicating relationships:

```yaml
---
name: example-react-app-code-reviewer
inherits: generic-code-reviewer
imports:
  - _shared/CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md
description: ...
---
```

Project skills become thin overrides of generic skills.

### 3.2 Standardize "See Also" Sections
Current: Verbose, inconsistent across skills
Target: Single-line format

```markdown
## See Also
- [generic-ux-designer](./../generic-ux-designer/) - Base UX patterns
- [CLAUDE.md](../../CLAUDE.md) - Project rules
```

**Estimated savings:** ~50 lines across all skills

### 3.3 Remove Verbose Introductions
Many skills have 20-30 line introductions explaining "what this skill does."

**Target:** 2-3 lines max. The description field handles discovery.

**Estimated savings:** ~200 lines across all skills

---

## Phase 4: Command File Optimization

### 4.1 start-task.md (162 → ~100 lines)
**Changes:**
- Reduce examples from 6 to 3
- Condense routing logic explanation
- Remove redundant comments

**Estimated savings:** ~60 lines

### 4.2 run.md - No changes needed
Already concise at 44 lines.

---

## Phase 5: Documentation Consolidation

### 5.1 CLAUDE-CODE-RESOURCES.md (793 lines)
This file is a reference guide - optimization opportunities:
- Convert verbose sections to tables
- Remove redundant installation instructions
- Tighten "Why" explanations

**Target:** 793 → ~600 lines
**Estimated savings:** ~190 lines

### 5.2 docs/ folder - Audit for redundancy
Review if any docs duplicate CLAUDE-CODE-RESOURCES.md content.

---

## Implementation Order

| Priority | Task | Savings | Effort |
|----------|------|---------|--------|
| 1 | Create _shared/ folder + 3 reference files | Foundation | Medium |
| 2 | Condense example-react-app-ux-designer | ~850 lines | High |
| 3 | Condense example-react-app-design-system | ~470 lines | Medium |
| 4 | Update generic skills to reference shared | ~300 lines | Medium |
| 5 | Condense tech-debt-analyzer | ~220 lines | Low |
| 6 | Condense test-specialist | ~215 lines | Low |
| 7 | Standardize all See Also + intros | ~250 lines | Low |
| 8 | Optimize start-task.md | ~60 lines | Low |
| 9 | Optimize CLAUDE-CODE-RESOURCES.md | ~190 lines | Medium |

**Total estimated reduction:** ~2,500+ lines (25-30%)

---

## Files to Modify

### New Files
- `C:\Users\tjn\.claude\skills\_shared\CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md`
- `C:\Users\tjn\.claude\skills\_shared\DESIGN_PATTERNS.md`
- `C:\Users\tjn\.claude\skills\_shared\UX_PRINCIPLES.md`

### Primary Modifications (High Impact)
- `skills/example-react-app-ux-designer/SKILL.md`
- `skills/example-react-app-design-system/SKILL.md`
- `skills/generic-code-reviewer/SKILL.md`
- `skills/generic-ux-designer/SKILL.md`
- `skills/generic-design-system/SKILL.md`

### Secondary Modifications (Medium Impact)
- `skills/tech-debt-analyzer/SKILL.md`
- `skills/test-specialist/SKILL.md`
- `skills/frontend-enhancer/SKILL.md`
- `skills/example-fullstack-ux-designer/SKILL.md`
- `skills/example-fullstack-design-system/SKILL.md`
- `commands/start-task.md`
- `CLAUDE-CODE-RESOURCES.md`

### Minor Updates (Add References)
- All remaining `example-*` skills (add import references)

---

## Validation Strategy

After each phase:
1. Verify skill still activates correctly
2. Test that referenced content is accessible
3. Confirm no broken links
4. Spot-check key features still documented

---

## Questions for User

Before proceeding:

1. **Shared Library Approach:** Should the `_shared/` folder use:
   - Option A: Markdown files that Claude reads when referenced
   - Option B: Inline snippets that get copied into skills during optimization

2. **Project-Specific Skills:** The `example-*` skills reference specific projects (Neuman Brain, Lazy Golfing, thedeadrobot). Should these be:
   - Option A: Keep as templates for other users to customize
   - Option B: Rename to project names (neuman-brain-ux-designer, etc.)
   - Option C: Remove project-specific content entirely (pure generic skills)

3. **Preservation Level:** For code examples, prefer:
   - Option A: Keep all examples but compress formatting
   - Option B: Reduce to 2-3 representative examples per concept
   - Option C: Remove inline examples, link to external resources

content.text: I've created a comprehensive optimization plan. Before proceeding, I have a few questions to clarify your preferences:

questions.question: For the shared reference library (_shared/ folder), how should Claude access the extracted patterns?

options.label: Markdown links (Recommended)
options.description: Skills link to _shared/ files. Claude reads them when invoked. Most flexible, enables future updates in one place.

options.label: Inline snippets
options.description: Copy condensed patterns directly into each skill. Faster loading but means updating multiple files for changes.

questions.question: The example-* skills reference specific projects (Neuman Brain, Lazy Golfing, thedeadrobot). How should these be handled?

options.label: Keep as templates (Recommended)
options.description: Preserve as examples showing how to customize generic skills for specific projects. Users copy and adapt for their own projects.

options.label: Remove project content
options.description: Strip all project-specific references, make them purely generic. Loses the "how to customize" examples.

options.label: Rename to project names
options.description: Rename to neuman-brain-*, lazy-golfing-*, etc. Makes it clearer these are your actual project skills, not templates.

questions.question: For code examples within skills, what level of preservation do you prefer?

options.label: Reduce to essentials (Recommended)

# Optimize .claude/ Folder - Context Reduction Plan

**Goal:** Reduce context consumption while preserving all functionality
**Constraint:** No deletions - only restructuring and condensing

---

## Executive Summary

### Current State
- **Total SKILL.md content:** ~290 KB across 25 files (~8,600 lines)
- **Largest files:** example-react-app-ux-designer (32KB/1,206 lines), document-skills/pptx (25.5KB), example-react-app-design-system (24KB)
- **Key finding:** ~50-60% redundancy across similar skill types

### Target Outcome
- **Estimated reduction:** 25-35% (~70-100 KB savings)
- **Method:** Extract shared patterns, eliminate redundancy, tighten prose
- **Preserved:** All functionality, all knowledge, all examples

---

## Phase 1: Create Shared Reference Library

Create `C:\Users\tjn\.claude\skills\_shared\` directory with extracted common patterns.

### 1.1 Create CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md (~80 lines)
Extract from 4 code-reviewer skills:
- Security checks (Frontend/Backend/General)
- Performance requirements table
- Accessibility (WCAG AA) requirements
- Review checklist template

**Files affected:**
- `generic-code-reviewer/SKILL.md`
- `example-react-app-code-reviewer/SKILL.md`
- `example-fullstack-code-reviewer/SKILL.md`
- `example-static-site-code-reviewer/SKILL.md`

**Estimated savings:** 300+ lines across 4 files

### 1.2 Create DESIGN_PATTERNS.md (~100 lines)
Extract from design system and UX skills:
- Typography scale table
- Spacing system (4px/8px base)
- Color theory principles
- Visual hierarchy patterns
- Breakpoint table

**Files affected:**
- `generic-design-system/SKILL.md`
- `generic-ux-designer/SKILL.md`
- `frontend-enhancer/SKILL.md`
- All example-*-design-system skills

**Estimated savings:** 200+ lines across 6 files

### 1.3 Create UX_PRINCIPLES.md (~120 lines)
Extract from UX designer skills:
- Hick's Law, Fitts's Law, Jakob's Law
- Cognitive load (Miller's Law)
- Design Thinking framework (5 steps)
- User psychology principles
- Interaction patterns tables

**Files affected:**
- `generic-ux-designer/SKILL.md`
- `example-react-app-ux-designer/SKILL.md`
- `example-fullstack-ux-designer/SKILL.md`
- `example-static-site-ux-designer/SKILL.md`

**Estimated savings:** 400+ lines across 4 files

---

## Phase 2: Condense Verbose Skills

### 2.1 example-react-app-ux-designer.md (1,206 → ~350 lines)
Current state: Contains generic UX frameworks that belong in generic-ux-designer

**Changes:**
- Remove Design Thinking framework (use `@import _shared/UX_PRINCIPLES.md`)
- Remove Visual Design Principles section (generic)
- Remove User Psychology section (use shared reference)
- Remove Accessibility section (use CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md)
- **Keep:** Neuman Brain specific constraints, brand colors, project-specific examples

**Estimated savings:** ~850 lines

### 2.2 tech-debt-analyzer.md (619 → ~400 lines)
Current state: Contains theoretical tool descriptions for non-existent scripts

**Changes:**
- Remove detailed Python script descriptions (tools don't exist yet)
- Condense "Success Metrics" from narrative to bullet list
- Tighten workflow explanations
- Keep practical analysis steps and patterns

**Estimated savings:** ~220 lines

### 2.3 test-specialist.md (566 → ~350 lines)
Current state: Heavy language-specific examples

**Changes:**
- Reduce code examples from 15+ to 5-7 representative ones
- Remove language-specific details (generalize)
- Keep core testing strategies
- Reference tech-specific patterns rather than embedding

**Estimated savings:** ~215 lines

### 2.4 example-react-app-design-system.md (871 → ~400 lines)
Current state: Repeats generic design patterns

**Changes:**
- Reference DESIGN_PATTERNS.md for shared patterns
- Keep only Neuman Brain specific tokens and components
- Reduce code examples to essential patterns

**Estimated savings:** ~470 lines

### 2.5 frontend-enhancer.md (335 → ~200 lines)
Current state: Overlaps with design system skills

**Changes:**
- Reference shared design patterns
- Focus on enhancement workflow, not fundamentals
- Tighten prose

**Estimated savings:** ~135 lines

---

## Phase 3: Standardize Skill Structure

### 3.1 Implement Inheritance Model
Add front matter indicating relationships:

```yaml
---
name: example-react-app-code-reviewer
inherits: generic-code-reviewer
imports:
  - _shared/CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md
description: ...
---
```

Project skills become thin overrides of generic skills.

### 3.2 Standardize "See Also" Sections
Current: Verbose, inconsistent across skills
Target: Single-line format

```markdown
## See Also
- [generic-ux-designer](./../generic-ux-designer/) - Base UX patterns
- [CLAUDE.md](../../CLAUDE.md) - Project rules
```

**Estimated savings:** ~50 lines across all skills

### 3.3 Remove Verbose Introductions
Many skills have 20-30 line introductions explaining "what this skill does."

**Target:** 2-3 lines max. The description field handles discovery.

**Estimated savings:** ~200 lines across all skills

---

## Phase 4: Command File Optimization

### 4.1 start-task.md (162 → ~100 lines)
**Changes:**
- Reduce examples from 6 to 3
- Condense routing logic explanation
- Remove redundant comments

**Estimated savings:** ~60 lines

### 4.2 run.md - No changes needed
Already concise at 44 lines.

---

## Phase 5: Documentation Consolidation

### 5.1 CLAUDE-CODE-RESOURCES.md (793 lines)
This file is a reference guide - optimization opportunities:
- Convert verbose sections to tables
- Remove redundant installation instructions
- Tighten "Why" explanations

**Target:** 793 → ~600 lines
**Estimated savings:** ~190 lines

### 5.2 docs/ folder - Audit for redundancy
Review if any docs duplicate CLAUDE-CODE-RESOURCES.md content.

---

## Implementation Order

| Priority | Task | Savings | Effort |
|----------|------|---------|--------|
| 1 | Create _shared/ folder + 3 reference files | Foundation | Medium |
| 2 | Condense example-react-app-ux-designer | ~850 lines | High |
| 3 | Condense example-react-app-design-system | ~470 lines | Medium |
| 4 | Update generic skills to reference shared | ~300 lines | Medium |
| 5 | Condense tech-debt-analyzer | ~220 lines | Low |
| 6 | Condense test-specialist | ~215 lines | Low |
| 7 | Standardize all See Also + intros | ~250 lines | Low |
| 8 | Optimize start-task.md | ~60 lines | Low |
| 9 | Optimize CLAUDE-CODE-RESOURCES.md | ~190 lines | Medium |

**Total estimated reduction:** ~2,500+ lines (25-30%)

---

## Files to Modify

### New Files
- `C:\Users\tjn\.claude\skills\_shared\CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md`
- `C:\Users\tjn\.claude\skills\_shared\DESIGN_PATTERNS.md`
- `C:\Users\tjn\.claude\skills\_shared\UX_PRINCIPLES.md`

### Primary Modifications (High Impact)
- `skills/example-react-app-ux-designer/SKILL.md`
- `skills/example-react-app-design-system/SKILL.md`
- `skills/generic-code-reviewer/SKILL.md`
- `skills/generic-ux-designer/SKILL.md`
- `skills/generic-design-system/SKILL.md`

### Secondary Modifications (Medium Impact)
- `skills/tech-debt-analyzer/SKILL.md`
- `skills/test-specialist/SKILL.md`
- `skills/frontend-enhancer/SKILL.md`
- `skills/example-fullstack-ux-designer/SKILL.md`
- `skills/example-fullstack-design-system/SKILL.md`
- `commands/start-task.md`
- `CLAUDE-CODE-RESOURCES.md`

### Minor Updates (Add References)
- All remaining `example-*` skills (add import references)

---

## Validation Strategy

After each phase:
1. Verify skill still activates correctly
2. Test that referenced content is accessible
3. Confirm no broken links
4. Spot-check key features still documented

---

## Questions for User

Before proceeding:

1. **Shared Library Approach:** Should the `_shared/` folder use:
   - Option A: Markdown files that Claude reads when referenced
   - Option B: Inline snippets that get copied into skills during optimization

2. **Project-Specific Skills:** The `example-*` skills reference specific projects (Neuman Brain, Lazy Golfing, thedeadrobot). Should these be:
   - Option A: Keep as templates for other users to customize
   - Option B: Rename to project names (neuman-brain-ux-designer, etc.)
   - Option C: Remove project-specific content entirely (pure generic skills)

3. **Preservation Level:** For code examples, prefer:
   - Option A: Keep all examples but compress formatting
   - Option B: Reduce to 2-3 representative examples per concept
   - Option C: Remove inline examples, link to external resources

lines: +1. **Reference Style:** Markdown links - all paths relative within .claude/ (portable)

- **Key finding:** ~50-60% redundancy across similar skill types

-- **Preserved:** All functionality, all knowledge, all examples

**Constraint:** No deletions - only restructuring and condensing

- `example-fullstack-ux-designer/SKILL.md`
- `example-static-site-ux-designer/SKILL.md`

**Estimated savings:** 400+ lines across 4 files

---

## Phase 2: Genericize Project-Specific Skills

**Goal:** Transform example-* skills into generic variants while preserving ALL rules, guidelines, and instructions.

### 2.1 example-react-app-ux-designer.md → generic-react-ux-designer.md
**Current:** 1,206 lines with Neuman Brain references
**Target:** ~400 lines of generic React UX guidance

**Transformation:**
- Replace "Neuman Brain" → "React applications"
- Remove specific brand colors, keep pattern: "Use project's design tokens"
- Keep ALL UX principles (Hick's Law, Fitts's Law, etc.)
- Keep ALL design thinking frameworks
- Keep ALL accessibility requirements
- Reduce code examples from 20+ to 6-8 representative patterns
- Reference `_shared/UX_PRINCIPLES.md` for foundational concepts

**Estimated result:** ~400 lines (savings: ~800 lines)

### 2.2 example-react-app-design-system.md → generic-react-design-system.md
**Current:** 871 lines with Neuman Brain tokens
**Target:** ~300 lines of generic React design system guidance

**Transformation:**
- Replace specific colors → "Follow project's color palette"
- Keep typography scale principles (not specific sizes)
- Keep spacing system pattern (4px base unit concept)
- Keep component pattern guidance
- Keep animation performance requirements
- Reduce code examples to 3-4 essential patterns
- Reference `_shared/DESIGN_PATTERNS.md`

**Estimated result:** ~300 lines (savings: ~570 lines)

### 2.3 example-fullstack-* skills (4 files)
**Current:** ~1,000 lines total across 4 files
**Target:** ~500 lines total

**Transformation:**
- Same genericization approach
- "Lazy Golfing" → "Full-stack Next.js/NestJS applications"
- Preserve all architectural patterns
- Preserve all testing requirements
- Reduce project-specific examples

### 2.4 example-static-site-* skills (4 files)
**Current:** ~900 lines total across 4 files
**Target:** ~500 lines total

**Transformation:**
- "thedeadrobot" → "Static HTML/CSS/JS sites"
- Preserve all patterns and guidelines
- Keep vanilla JS best practices

---

## Phase 3: Condense Utility Skills

### 3.1 tech-debt-analyzer.md (619 → ~400 lines)
**Changes:**
- Remove theoretical tool descriptions (Python scripts don't exist)
- Condense "Success Metrics" to bullet list
- Keep practical analysis workflow
- Keep code smell patterns

### 3.2 test-specialist.md (566 → ~350 lines)
**Changes:**
- Reduce examples from 15+ to 5-7 essentials
- Generalize language-specific guidance
- Keep core testing strategies

### 3.3 frontend-enhancer.md (335 → ~200 lines)
**Changes:**
- Reference shared design patterns
- Focus on enhancement workflow
- Reduce redundant examples

---

## Phase 3: Standardize Skill Structure

### 3.1 Implement Inheritance Model
Add front matter indicating relationships:

```yaml
---
name: example-react-app-code-reviewer
inherits: generic-code-reviewer
imports:
  - _shared/CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md
description: ...
---
```

Project skills become thin overrides of generic skills.

### 3.2 Standardize "See Also" Sections
Current: Verbose, inconsistent across skills
Target: Single-line format

```markdown
## See Also
- [generic-ux-designer](./../generic-ux-designer/) - Base UX patterns

### 3.3 Remove Verbose Introductions
Many skills have 20-30 line introductions explaining "what this skill does."

**Target:** 2-3 lines max. The description field handles discovery.

**Estimated savings:** ~200 lines across all skills

---

**Changes:**
- Reduce examples from 6 to 3
- Condense routing logic explanation
- Remove redundant comments

**Estimated savings:** ~60 lines

### 4.2 run.md - No changes needed
Already concise at 44 lines.

---

### 5.1 CLAUDE-CODE-RESOURCES.md (793 lines)
This file is a reference guide - optimization opportunities:

- Remove redundant installation instructions
- Tighten "Why" explanations

**Target:** 793 → ~600 lines
**Estimated savings:** ~190 lines

### 5.2 docs/ folder - Audit for redundancy
Review if any docs duplicate CLAUDE-CODE-RESOURCES.md content.

# Optimize .claude/ Folder - Context Reduction Plan

**Goal:** Reduce context consumption while preserving all functionality
**Constraint:** No deletions - only restructuring and condensing

---

## User Preferences (Confirmed)

1. **Reference Style:** Markdown links - all paths relative within .claude/ (portable)
2. **Project Skills:** Make example-* skills fully generic while preserving all rules/guidelines
3. **Code Examples:** Reduce to 2-3 essentials per concept

---

## Executive Summary

### Current State
- **Total SKILL.md content:** ~290 KB across 25 files (~8,600 lines)
- **Largest files:** example-react-app-ux-designer (32KB/1,206 lines), document-skills/pptx (25.5KB), example-react-app-design-system (24KB)
- **Key finding:** ~50-60% redundancy across similar skill types

### Target Outcome
- **Estimated reduction:** 30-40% (~90-120 KB savings)
- **Method:** Extract shared patterns, genericize project skills, reduce examples
- **Preserved:** All functionality, all rules/guidelines, essential examples

---

## Phase 1: Create Shared Reference Library

Create `C:\Users\tjn\.claude\skills\_shared\` directory with extracted common patterns.

### 1.1 Create CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md (~80 lines)
Extract from 4 code-reviewer skills:
- Security checks (Frontend/Backend/General)
- Performance requirements table
- Accessibility (WCAG AA) requirements
- Review checklist template

**Files affected:**
- `generic-code-reviewer/SKILL.md`
- `example-react-app-code-reviewer/SKILL.md`
- `example-fullstack-code-reviewer/SKILL.md`
- `example-static-site-code-reviewer/SKILL.md`

**Estimated savings:** 300+ lines across 4 files

### 1.2 Create DESIGN_PATTERNS.md (~100 lines)
Extract from design system and UX skills:
- Typography scale table
- Spacing system (4px/8px base)
- Color theory principles
- Visual hierarchy patterns
- Breakpoint table

**Files affected:**
- `generic-design-system/SKILL.md`
- `generic-ux-designer/SKILL.md`
- `frontend-enhancer/SKILL.md`
- All example-*-design-system skills

**Estimated savings:** 200+ lines across 6 files

### 1.3 Create UX_PRINCIPLES.md (~120 lines)
Extract from UX designer skills:
- Hick's Law, Fitts's Law, Jakob's Law
- Cognitive load (Miller's Law)
- Design Thinking framework (5 steps)
- User psychology principles
- Interaction patterns tables

**Files affected:**
- `generic-ux-designer/SKILL.md`
- `example-react-app-ux-designer/SKILL.md`
- `example-fullstack-ux-designer/SKILL.md`
- `example-static-site-ux-designer/SKILL.md`

**Estimated savings:** 400+ lines across 4 files

---

## Phase 2: Genericize Project-Specific Skills

**Goal:** Transform example-* skills into generic variants while preserving ALL rules, guidelines, and instructions.

### 2.1 example-react-app-ux-designer.md → generic-react-ux-designer.md
**Current:** 1,206 lines with Neuman Brain references
**Target:** ~400 lines of generic React UX guidance

**Transformation:**
- Replace "Neuman Brain" → "React applications"
- Remove specific brand colors, keep pattern: "Use project's design tokens"
- Keep ALL UX principles (Hick's Law, Fitts's Law, etc.)
- Keep ALL design thinking frameworks
- Keep ALL accessibility requirements
- Reduce code examples from 20+ to 6-8 representative patterns
- Reference `_shared/UX_PRINCIPLES.md` for foundational concepts

**Estimated result:** ~400 lines (savings: ~800 lines)

### 2.2 example-react-app-design-system.md → generic-react-design-system.md
**Current:** 871 lines with Neuman Brain tokens
**Target:** ~300 lines of generic React design system guidance

**Transformation:**
- Replace specific colors → "Follow project's color palette"
- Keep typography scale principles (not specific sizes)
- Keep spacing system pattern (4px base unit concept)
- Keep component pattern guidance
- Keep animation performance requirements
- Reduce code examples to 3-4 essential patterns
- Reference `_shared/DESIGN_PATTERNS.md`

**Estimated result:** ~300 lines (savings: ~570 lines)

### 2.3 example-fullstack-* skills (4 files)
**Current:** ~1,000 lines total across 4 files
**Target:** ~500 lines total

**Transformation:**
- Same genericization approach
- "Lazy Golfing" → "Full-stack Next.js/NestJS applications"
- Preserve all architectural patterns
- Preserve all testing requirements
- Reduce project-specific examples

### 2.4 example-static-site-* skills (4 files)
**Current:** ~900 lines total across 4 files
**Target:** ~500 lines total

**Transformation:**
- "thedeadrobot" → "Static HTML/CSS/JS sites"
- Preserve all patterns and guidelines
- Keep vanilla JS best practices

---

## Phase 3: Condense Utility Skills

### 3.1 tech-debt-analyzer.md (619 → ~400 lines)
**Changes:**
- Remove theoretical tool descriptions (Python scripts don't exist)
- Condense "Success Metrics" to bullet list
- Keep practical analysis workflow
- Keep code smell patterns

### 3.2 test-specialist.md (566 → ~350 lines)
**Changes:**
- Reduce examples from 15+ to 5-7 essentials
- Generalize language-specific guidance
- Keep core testing strategies

### 3.3 frontend-enhancer.md (335 → ~200 lines)
**Changes:**
- Reference shared design patterns
- Focus on enhancement workflow
- Reduce redundant examples

---

## Phase 4: Standardize Skill Structure

### 4.1 Implement Import References
Add front matter with relative imports (portable):

```yaml
---
name: generic-react-ux-designer
imports:
  - ./_shared/UX_PRINCIPLES.md
  - ./_shared/DESIGN_PATTERNS.md
description: ...
---
```

### 4.2 Standardize "See Also" Sections
**Current:** Verbose, inconsistent
**Target:** Single-line relative links

```markdown
## See Also
- [UX Principles](./_shared/UX_PRINCIPLES.md)
- [Design Patterns](./_shared/DESIGN_PATTERNS.md)
```

**Savings:** ~50 lines across all skills

### 4.3 Remove Verbose Introductions
**Current:** 20-30 line intros explaining "what this skill does"
**Target:** 2-3 lines max (description field handles discovery)

**Savings:** ~200 lines across all skills

---

## Phase 5: Command & Documentation Optimization

### 5.1 start-task.md (162 → ~100 lines)
- Reduce examples from 6 to 3
- Condense routing logic
- Keep all functionality

### 5.2 CLAUDE-CODE-RESOURCES.md (793 → ~600 lines)

---

## Implementation Order

| 2 | Genericize + condense example-react-app-* (4 files) | 4 modify | ~1,500 lines |

file.content:
---
name: generic-code-reviewer
description: Review code for bugs, security vulnerabilities, performance issues, accessibility gaps, and CLAUDE.md workflow compliance. Supports any tech stack - HTML/CSS/JS, React, TypeScript, Node.js, Python, NestJS, Next.js, and more. Use when completing features, before commits, or reviewing pull requests.
---

# Generic Code Reviewer

Review code against development principles, CLAUDE.md workflow, and production quality standards. Adapts to any project's tech stack.

## CLAUDE.md Workflow Compliance

**Always check the project's CLAUDE.md file for specific rules.**

**Before ANY Commit:**
- Task file exists in `tasks/[feature-name].md`
- Task file has comprehensive review section
- All tests passing
- Type checking passing (if TypeScript)
- No console errors/warnings
- Bundle size within limits

## Tech Stack Detection

Automatically adapt checks based on project:

| Detection | Tech Stack | Key Checks |
|-----------|------------|------------|
| `package.json` with React | React/TypeScript | Components, hooks, state |
| `package.json` with Next.js | Next.js | SSR, API routes, routing |
| `package.json` with NestJS | NestJS | Guards, DTOs, services |
| `.html` files, no build | Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS | Semantic HTML, minimal JS |
| `requirements.txt` or `.py` | Python | Type hints, validation |

## Security Checks

### Frontend
- Sanitize all user input (use textContent, not innerHTML)
- Use `unknown` type for external data, validate before using
- No dynamic code execution (eval, Function constructor)
- No exposed API keys in client code
- HTTPS for all external requests

### Backend
- Input validation on all endpoints
- Authentication on protected routes
- No raw SQL queries (use ORM/parameterized)
- Secrets in environment variables
- Rate limiting on public endpoints

### General
- No tracking without user consent
- Privacy-first data handling
- WebCrypto API for encryption (no custom crypto)

## Performance Requirements

### Bundle/Page Size
| Project Type | Target |
|--------------|--------|
| Static site | < 50KB (excluding media) |
| SPA/React | < 100KB gzipped initial |
| Full-stack app | < 200KB gzipped initial |

### Animation Performance
- GPU-accelerated only (transform, opacity)
- 60fps target
- Avoid layout thrashing
- Use requestAnimationFrame

### Loading
- Lazy load non-essential code/images
- Code splitting for large features
- Optimize images (WebP, responsive)

## Accessibility (WCAG AA)

### Required
- Focus indicators on all interactive elements
- Keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter, Escape, Arrows)
- Color contrast >= 4.5:1 for text
- ARIA labels on icon-only buttons
- Alt text for all meaningful images
- Semantic HTML structure

### Interactive Elements
- Focus trapped in modals
- Escape key closes dialogs
- Skip links for navigation
- Form error messages announced

## Code Quality

### General Principles
- DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself)
- Single Responsibility
- Explicit over implicit
- No magic numbers/strings
- Self-documenting code

### HTML
- Semantic tags (`<nav>`, `<main>`, `<article>`, not just `<div>`)
- Proper heading hierarchy
- Self-closing tags for void elements
- Meaningful class names

### CSS
- Mobile-first approach
- Avoid `!important`
- CSS custom properties for reusable values
- Consistent naming convention (BEM or similar)
- Comment complex animations

### JavaScript/TypeScript
- ES6+ syntax (const, let, arrow functions)
- TypeScript strict mode when available
- No `any` types for user data
- Async/await over raw promises
- Event delegation where appropriate
- Defensive programming (null checks)

### Python
- Type hints on functions
- Docstrings for public functions
- PEP 8 style compliance
- Context managers for resources
- Proper exception handling

## Design System Compliance

Check project's design system for:
- Color palette adherence
- Typography consistency
- Spacing system (4px, 8px base)
- Component patterns
- Dark/light mode support

## Testing Requirements

### Minimum Coverage
- Critical paths tested
- Edge cases covered
- Error states handled
- Accessibility tested

### Test Types
| Type | When |
|------|------|
| Unit | Business logic, utilities |
| Integration | API endpoints, data flow |
| E2E | Critical user journeys |

## Surgical Simplicity

Changes should impact minimal code relevant to task only.

- Every line intentional
- No commented-out code
- No dead code
- Clean, documented, maintainable
- Follow existing patterns

## Review Checklist

### Pre-Commit
- [ ] Task file updated
- [ ] Tests pass
- [ ] Type check passes (if TS)
- [ ] Lint passes
- [ ] Build succeeds
- [ ] No console errors

### Security
- [ ] Input sanitized
- [ ] Auth on protected routes
- [ ] No exposed secrets
- [ ] Privacy respected

### Performance
- [ ] Bundle size acceptable
- [ ] No layout thrashing
- [ ] Images optimized
- [ ] Lazy loading used

### Accessibility
- [ ] Keyboard navigable
- [ ] Focus indicators
- [ ] Contrast sufficient
- [ ] ARIA labels present

### Quality
- [ ] Code follows patterns
- [ ] No magic values
- [ ] Proper error handling
- [ ] Self-documenting

## See Also

- `CLAUDE.md` - Project workflow rules
- `.claude/workflow-checklist.md` - Complete checklist
- Project-specific code-reviewer skills for detailed requirements

1→---
     2→name: generic-design-system
     3→description: Complete design system reference for any project - colors, typography, spacing, components, animations. Adapts to project theme and tech stack. Use when implementing UI, choosing colors, creating animations, or ensuring brand consistency.
     4→---
     5→
     6→# Generic Design System
     7→
     8→Comprehensive design system reference that adapts to any project's visual language.
     9→
    10→## Color System
    11→
    12→### Defining Project Colors
    13→
    14→Every project should define:
    15→- **Primary**: Main brand color
    16→- **Secondary**: Supporting color
    17→- **Accent**: Highlight/CTA color
    18→- **Background**: Base canvas color
    19→- **Foreground**: Primary text color
    20→- **Muted**: Secondary elements
    21→- **Destructive**: Error/danger states
    22→- **Success**: Positive states
    23→- **Warning**: Caution states
    24→
    25→### Color Mode Support
    26→
    27→| Mode | Considerations |
    28→|------|----------------|
    29→| Light | Higher contrast, softer backgrounds |
    30→| Dark | Reduced blue light, darker backgrounds |
    31→| High Contrast | WCAG AAA compliance |
    32→
    33→### Contrast Requirements
    34→
    35→| Use Case | Minimum Ratio |
    36→|----------|---------------|
    37→| Body text | 4.5:1 (AA) |
    38→| Large text (24px+) | 3:1 (AA) |
    39→| UI components | 3:1 (AA) |
    40→| Enhanced | 7:1 (AAA) |
    41→
    42→## Typography
    43→
    44→### Font Stack
    45→
    46→```css
    47→/* System fonts for performance */
    48→--font-sans: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif;
    49→--font-mono: 'SF Mono', Monaco, 'Courier New', monospace;
    50→
    51→/* Custom fonts loaded via @font-face or Google Fonts */
    52→```
    53→
    54→### Type Scale
    55→
    56→| Name | Size | Line Height | Use |
    57→|------|------|-------------|-----|
    58→| xs | 12px | 16px | Captions, labels |
    59→| sm | 14px | 20px | Secondary text |
    60→| base | 16px | 24px | Body text |
    61→| lg | 18px | 28px | Lead paragraphs |
    62→| xl | 20px | 28px | Section headers |
    63→| 2xl | 24px | 32px | Page headers |
    64→| 3xl | 30px | 36px | Hero text |
    65→| 4xl | 36px | 40px | Display |
    66→
    67→### Font Weights
    68→
    69→| Weight | Value | Use |
    70→|--------|-------|-----|
    71→| Normal | 400 | Body text |
    72→| Medium | 500 | UI elements |
    73→| Semibold | 600 | Subheadings |
    74→| Bold | 700 | Headings |
    75→
    76→## Spacing System
    77→
    78→### Base Unit
    79→

   132→--ease-default: cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1);

   138→

   142→|-------|----------|-----|
   143→| fast | 100ms | Micro-interactions |

   146→| slower | 500ms | Page transitions |
   147→
   148→### GPU-Accelerated Properties
   149→
   150→**DO animate:**

   153→
   154→**AVOID animating:**

   156→- `top`, `left`, `right`, `bottom`
   157→- `margin`, `padding`
   158→- `background-color` (OK for simple)
   159→
   160→## Components
   161→
   162→### Buttons
   163→
   164→| Variant | Use |
   165→|---------|-----|
   166→| Primary | Main actions (Submit, Save) |
   167→| Secondary | Alternative actions |
   168→| Ghost | Subtle actions |
   169→| Outline | Less prominent actions |
   170→| Destructive | Delete, remove actions |
   171→
   172→### Form Inputs
   173→
   174→- Clear focus states
   175→- Error states with messages

   177→- Label always visible
   178→- Required indicator
   179→
   180→### Cards

   182→- Consistent padding
   183→- Optional header/footer
   184→- Hover state for interactive
   185→- Focus state for keyboard
   186→
   187→### Modals/Dialogs

   189→- Focus trapped inside

   193→- Dark overlay
   194→
   195→## Icons

   197→### Size Tokens
   198→
   199→| Token | Size | Use |
   200→|-------|------|-----|

   202→| sm | 16px | Inline with body |
   203→| md | 20px | Buttons, inputs |
   204→| lg | 24px | Headers |
   205→| xl | 32px | Features |
   206→
   207→### Stroke Width
   208→
   209→- 1.5px for light icons
   210→- 2px for standard icons
   211→- 2.5px for bold icons
   212→
   213→## Responsive Breakpoints
   214→
   215→| Token | Min Width | Devices |
   216→|-------|-----------|---------|
   217→| sm | 640px | Large phones |
   218→| md | 768px | Tablets |
   219→| lg | 1024px | Laptops |
   220→| xl | 1280px | Desktops |
   221→| 2xl | 1536px | Large desktops |
   222→
   223→## Dark Mode Strategy
   224→
   225→```css
   226→/* CSS Variables approach */

   228→  --background: #ffffff;
   229→  --foreground: #000000;
   230→}

   245→

   263→

---
name: generic-design-system
description: Complete design system reference for any project - colors, typography, spacing, components, animations. Adapts to project theme and tech stack. Use when implementing UI, choosing colors, creating animations, or ensuring brand consistency.
---

# Generic Design System

Comprehensive design system reference that adapts to any project's visual language.

## Color System

### Defining Project Colors

Every project should define:
- **Primary**: Main brand color
- **Secondary**: Supporting color
- **Accent**: Highlight/CTA color
- **Background**: Base canvas color
- **Foreground**: Primary text color
- **Muted**: Secondary elements
- **Destructive**: Error/danger states
- **Success**: Positive states
- **Warning**: Caution states

### Color Mode Support

| Mode | Considerations |
|------|----------------|
| Light | Higher contrast, softer backgrounds |
| Dark | Reduced blue light, darker backgrounds |
| High Contrast | WCAG AAA compliance |

### Contrast Requirements

| Use Case | Minimum Ratio |
|----------|---------------|
| Body text | 4.5:1 (AA) |
| Large text (24px+) | 3:1 (AA) |
| UI components | 3:1 (AA) |
| Enhanced | 7:1 (AAA) |

## Typography

### Font Stack

```css
/* System fonts for performance */
--font-sans: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif;
--font-mono: 'SF Mono', Monaco, 'Courier New', monospace;

/* Custom fonts loaded via @font-face or Google Fonts */
```

### Type Scale

| Name | Size | Line Height | Use |
|------|------|-------------|-----|
| xs | 12px | 16px | Captions, labels |
| sm | 14px | 20px | Secondary text |
| base | 16px | 24px | Body text |
| lg | 18px | 28px | Lead paragraphs |
| xl | 20px | 28px | Section headers |
| 2xl | 24px | 32px | Page headers |
| 3xl | 30px | 36px | Hero text |
| 4xl | 36px | 40px | Display |

### Font Weights

| Weight | Value | Use |
|--------|-------|-----|
| Normal | 400 | Body text |
| Medium | 500 | UI elements |
| Semibold | 600 | Subheadings |
| Bold | 700 | Headings |

## Spacing System

### Base Unit

Recommend 4px or 8px base unit for consistency.

| Token | 4px Base | 8px Base | Use |
|-------|----------|----------|-----|
| 0 | 0 | 0 | None |
| 1 | 4px | 8px | Tight spacing |
| 2 | 8px | 16px | Default gap |
| 3 | 12px | 24px | Section padding |
| 4 | 16px | 32px | Card padding |
| 5 | 20px | 40px | Section margins |
| 6 | 24px | 48px | Page margins |
| 8 | 32px | 64px | Large sections |

### Layout Grid

```css
/* Common container widths */
--container-sm: 640px;
--container-md: 768px;
--container-lg: 1024px;
--container-xl: 1280px;
--container-2xl: 1536px;
```

## Border Radius

| Token | Value | Use |
|-------|-------|-----|
| none | 0 | Sharp corners |
| sm | 2px | Subtle rounding |
| DEFAULT | 4px | Buttons, inputs |
| md | 6px | Cards |
| lg | 8px | Modals |
| xl | 12px | Large cards |
| 2xl | 16px | Hero sections |
| full | 9999px | Pills, avatars |

## Shadows

| Token | Use |
|-------|-----|
| sm | Subtle elevation (buttons) |
| DEFAULT | Standard elevation (cards) |
| md | Medium elevation (dropdowns) |
| lg | High elevation (modals) |
| xl | Maximum elevation (popovers) |

## Animation

### Timing Functions

```css
--ease-default: cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1);
--ease-in: cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 1, 1);
--ease-out: cubic-bezier(0, 0, 0.2, 1);
--ease-in-out: cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1);
--ease-bounce: cubic-bezier(0.68, -0.55, 0.265, 1.55);
```

### Duration

| Token | Duration | Use |
|-------|----------|-----|
| fast | 100ms | Micro-interactions |
| DEFAULT | 200ms | Most transitions |
| slow | 300ms | Complex animations |
| slower | 500ms | Page transitions |

### GPU-Accelerated Properties

**DO animate:**
- `transform` (translate, scale, rotate)
- `opacity`

**AVOID animating:**
- `width`, `height`
- `top`, `left`, `right`, `bottom`
- `margin`, `padding`
- `background-color` (OK for simple)

## Components

### Buttons

| Variant | Use |
|---------|-----|
| Primary | Main actions (Submit, Save) |
| Secondary | Alternative actions |
| Ghost | Subtle actions |
| Outline | Less prominent actions |
| Destructive | Delete, remove actions |

### Form Inputs

- Clear focus states
- Error states with messages
- Placeholder text as hint
- Label always visible
- Required indicator

### Cards

- Consistent padding
- Optional header/footer
- Hover state for interactive
- Focus state for keyboard

### Modals/Dialogs

- Focus trapped inside
- Escape to close
- Click outside to close (optional)
- Centered or slide-in
- Dark overlay

## Icons

### Size Tokens

| Token | Size | Use |
|-------|------|-----|
| xs | 12px | Inline with small text |
| sm | 16px | Inline with body |
| md | 20px | Buttons, inputs |
| lg | 24px | Headers |
| xl | 32px | Features |

### Stroke Width

- 1.5px for light icons
- 2px for standard icons
- 2.5px for bold icons

## Responsive Breakpoints

| Token | Min Width | Devices |
|-------|-----------|---------|
| sm | 640px | Large phones |
| md | 768px | Tablets |
| lg | 1024px | Laptops |
| xl | 1280px | Desktops |
| 2xl | 1536px | Large desktops |

## Dark Mode Strategy

```css
/* CSS Variables approach */
:root {
  --background: #ffffff;
  --foreground: #000000;
}

[data-theme="dark"] {
  --background: #000000;
  --foreground: #ffffff;
}

/* Or media query */
@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
  :root {
    --background: #000000;
    --foreground: #ffffff;
  }
}
```

## Implementation Patterns

### CSS Variables (Recommended)

```css
:root {
  --color-primary: #3b82f6;
  --spacing-4: 16px;
  --radius-md: 6px;
}

.button {
  background: var(--color-primary);
  padding: var(--spacing-4);
  border-radius: var(--radius-md);
}
```

### Tailwind Config

```js
module.exports = {
  theme: {
    extend: {
      colors: { primary: '#3b82f6' },
      spacing: { '4': '16px' },
      borderRadius: { md: '6px' }
    }
  }
}
```

## See Also

- Project-specific design system skills for detailed brand requirements
- `.claude/workflow-checklist.md` - Styling checklist
- Project's `CLAUDE.md` for design constraints

     7→
     8→Professional UX expertise that adapts to any project's constraints and user needs.
     9→
    10→## Design Thinking Process
    11→
    12→### 1. Empathize
    13→- Understand user needs
    14→- Identify pain points
    15→- Map user journeys
    16→- Research competitors
    17→
    18→### 2. Define

    41→

    43→
    44→### Cognitive Load

    47→- Use progressive disclosure

    49→

    55→

    61→

    64→- Personality through copy

    67→
    68→## Interaction Patterns
    69→
    70→### Navigation
    71→
    72→| Pattern | Use Case |
    73→|---------|----------|
    74→| Top nav | Simple sites, < 7 items |
    75→| Side nav | Complex apps, many sections |
    76→| Bottom nav | Mobile apps, < 5 items |
    77→| Hamburger | Mobile, space-constrained |
    78→| Tabs | Related content sections |

    80→
    81→### Forms
    82→
    83→| Pattern | Benefit |
    84→|---------|---------|
    85→| Single column | Faster completion |
    86→| Inline validation | Immediate feedback |

    88→| Smart defaults | Reduced effort |

    93→
    94→| Pattern | Use Case |
    95→|---------|----------|

   102→
   103→### Feedback
   104→
   105→| Pattern | Use Case |
   106→|---------|----------|
   107→| Toast | Non-blocking info |
   108→| Modal | Blocking/important |
   109→| Inline | Contextual feedback |
   110→| Banner | Global notifications |
   111→| Progress | Long operations |
   112→| Skeleton | Loading states |
   113→
   114→## Mobile UX Considerations
   115→
   116→### Touch Targets
   117→- Minimum 44x44px
   118→- 8px spacing between targets
   119→- Thumb-friendly placement
   120→- Bottom sheet for actions
   121→
   122→### Gestures
   123→- Tap: Primary action
   124→- Swipe: Navigate/reveal
   125→- Long press: Secondary actions
   126→- Pinch: Zoom (media)
   127→
   128→### Mobile Patterns
   129→- Bottom navigation
   130→- Pull to refresh
   131→- Infinite scroll (carefully)
   132→- Full-screen modals
   133→- Native keyboard handling
   134→
   135→## Accessibility (UX Perspective)
   136→
   137→### Inclusive Design
   138→- Design for extremes, works for all
   139→- Consider temporary disabilities
   140→- Support different input methods
   141→- Test with real users
   142→
   143→### Key Considerations
   144→
   145→| Aspect | UX Approach |
   146→|--------|-------------|
   147→| Vision | High contrast, scalable text, alt text |
   148→| Motor | Large targets, keyboard nav, no timing |
   149→| Cognitive | Simple language, clear structure |
   150→| Hearing | Captions, visual indicators |
   151→
   152→### Testing
   153→- Screen reader walkthrough
   154→- Keyboard-only navigation
   155→- Color blindness simulation
   156→- Reduced motion mode
   157→
   158→## Design Critique Framework
   159→
   160→### Criteria
   161→
   162→| Aspect | Questions |
   163→|--------|-----------|
   164→| Usability | Can users complete tasks? |
   165→| Clarity | Is purpose immediately clear? |
   166→| Consistency | Follows established patterns? |
   167→| Hierarchy | Most important stands out? |
   168→| Feedback | Users know system state? |
   169→| Error handling | Recoverable mistakes? |
   170→| Accessibility | Works for everyone? |
   171→
   172→### Constructive Feedback
   173→
   174→**Instead of:** "This is confusing"
   175→**Say:** "Users might not understand X because Y. Consider Z."
   176→
   177→**Instead of:** "I don't like this"
   178→**Say:** "This pattern may cause issues because... Alternative approach..."
   179→
   180→## Common UX Patterns
   181→
   182→### Onboarding
   183→1. Progressive disclosure
   184→2. Contextual tips
   185→3. Interactive tutorials
   186→4. Skip option always
   187→5. Revisitable help
   188→
   189→### Empty States
   190→1. Explain what goes here
   191→2. Guide to first action
   192→3. Show example content
   193→4. Make it delightful
   194→
   195→### Loading States
   196→1. Skeleton screens > spinners
   197→2. Progress indicators for long ops
   198→3. Optimistic UI when safe
   199→4. Background loading when possible
   200→
   201→### Error States
   202→1. Explain what happened
   203→2. Suggest how to fix
   204→3. Provide recovery action
   205→4. Don't blame user
   206→
   207→### Success States
   208→1. Confirm action completed
   209→2. Show next steps
   210→3. Celebrate achievements
   211→4. Enable sharing if appropriate
   212→
   213→## Content Strategy
   214→
   215→### Microcopy
   216→- Be concise
   217→- Use active voice
   218→- Avoid jargon
   219→- Be human
   220→- Guide action
   221→
   222→### Labels & Instructions
   223→- Clear and specific
   224→- Explain why (if needed)
   225→- Example formats
   226→- Required vs optional
   227→
   228→### Error Messages
   229→- Specific problem
   230→- How to fix
   231→- No technical jargon
   232→- Apologetic when appropriate
   233→
   234→## Metrics That Matter
   235→
   236→### Engagement
   237→- Time on task
   238→- Completion rate
   239→- Return visits
   240→- Feature adoption
   241→
   242→### Satisfaction
   243→- NPS score
   244→- CSAT surveys
   245→- Support tickets
   246→- User feedback
   247→
   248→### Performance
   249→- Task success rate
   250→- Error rate
   251→- Time to first action
   252→- Abandonment points
   253→
   254→## Design Review Checklist
   255→
   256→### Functionality
   257→- [ ] Primary task achievable
   258→- [ ] Edge cases handled
   259→- [ ] Error states designed
   260→- [ ] Loading states designed
   261→
   262→### Usability
   263→- [ ] Clear visual hierarchy
   264→- [ ] Obvious next actions
   265→- [ ] Consistent with patterns
   266→- [ ] Minimal cognitive load
   267→
   268→### Accessibility
   269→- [ ] Keyboard navigable
   270→- [ ] Screen reader tested
   271→- [ ] Color contrast sufficient
   272→- [ ] Touch targets sized
   273→
   274→### Polish
   275→- [ ] Micro-interactions smooth
   276→- [ ] Copy clear and helpful
   277→- [ ] Responsive breakpoints
   278→- [ ] Performance acceptable
   279→
   280→## See Also
   281→
   282→- Project-specific UX skills for detailed requirements
   283→- `.claude/skills/generic-design-system/` - Visual design
   284→- `CLAUDE.md` - Project constraints

---
name: generic-ux-designer
description: Professional UX expertise for any project type. Covers user research, design thinking, interaction patterns, accessibility, and design critique. Use when designing new features, improving UX, conducting design reviews, or evaluating user flows.
---

# Generic UX Designer

Professional UX expertise that adapts to any project's constraints and user needs.

## Design Thinking Process

### 1. Empathize
- Understand user needs
- Identify pain points
- Map user journeys
- Research competitors

### 2. Define
- Problem statements
- User personas
- Success metrics
- Constraints

### 3. Ideate
- Brainstorm solutions
- Explore alternatives
- Consider edge cases
- Sketch concepts

### 4. Prototype
- Low-fidelity first
- Test assumptions
- Iterate quickly
- Validate with users

### 5. Test
- Usability testing
- A/B testing
- Analytics review
- Iterate based on data

## User Psychology Principles

### Cognitive Load
- Reduce choices (Hick's Law)
- Chunk information (Miller's Law)
- Use progressive disclosure
- Minimize user memory load

### Visual Hierarchy
- Size indicates importance
- Contrast draws attention
- Whitespace groups elements
- F-pattern/Z-pattern reading

### Behavioral Patterns
- Users scan, not read
- Familiarity breeds trust
- Immediate feedback essential
- Error prevention > recovery

### Emotional Design
- Delight in micro-interactions
- Personality through copy
- Trust through transparency
- Satisfaction through completion

## Interaction Patterns

### Navigation

| Pattern | Use Case |
|---------|----------|
| Top nav | Simple sites, < 7 items |
| Side nav | Complex apps, many sections |
| Bottom nav | Mobile apps, < 5 items |
| Hamburger | Mobile, space-constrained |
| Tabs | Related content sections |
| Breadcrumbs | Deep hierarchies |

### Forms

| Pattern | Benefit |
|---------|---------|
| Single column | Faster completion |
| Inline validation | Immediate feedback |
| Field grouping | Logical flow |
| Smart defaults | Reduced effort |
| Progress indicator | Clear expectations |
| Error summary | Easy correction |

### Data Display

| Pattern | Use Case |
|---------|----------|
| Cards | Browsable content |
| Tables | Comparable data |
| Lists | Sequential items |
| Timeline | Chronological events |
| Dashboard | At-a-glance metrics |
| Search + filters | Large datasets |

### Feedback

| Pattern | Use Case |
|---------|----------|
| Toast | Non-blocking info |
| Modal | Blocking/important |
| Inline | Contextual feedback |
| Banner | Global notifications |
| Progress | Long operations |
| Skeleton | Loading states |

## Mobile UX Considerations

### Touch Targets
- Minimum 44x44px
- 8px spacing between targets
- Thumb-friendly placement
- Bottom sheet for actions

### Gestures
- Tap: Primary action
- Swipe: Navigate/reveal
- Long press: Secondary actions
- Pinch: Zoom (media)

### Mobile Patterns
- Bottom navigation
- Pull to refresh
- Infinite scroll (carefully)
- Full-screen modals
- Native keyboard handling

## Accessibility (UX Perspective)

### Inclusive Design
- Design for extremes, works for all
- Consider temporary disabilities
- Support different input methods
- Test with real users

### Key Considerations

| Aspect | UX Approach |
|--------|-------------|
| Vision | High contrast, scalable text, alt text |
| Motor | Large targets, keyboard nav, no timing |
| Cognitive | Simple language, clear structure |
| Hearing | Captions, visual indicators |

### Testing
- Screen reader walkthrough
- Keyboard-only navigation
- Color blindness simulation
- Reduced motion mode

## Design Critique Framework

### Criteria

| Aspect | Questions |
|--------|-----------|
| Usability | Can users complete tasks? |
| Clarity | Is purpose immediately clear? |
| Consistency | Follows established patterns? |
| Hierarchy | Most important stands out? |
| Feedback | Users know system state? |
| Error handling | Recoverable mistakes? |
| Accessibility | Works for everyone? |

### Constructive Feedback

**Instead of:** "This is confusing"
**Say:** "Users might not understand X because Y. Consider Z."

**Instead of:** "I don't like this"
**Say:** "This pattern may cause issues because... Alternative approach..."

## Common UX Patterns

### Onboarding
1. Progressive disclosure
2. Contextual tips
3. Interactive tutorials
4. Skip option always
5. Revisitable help

### Empty States
1. Explain what goes here
2. Guide to first action
3. Show example content
4. Make it delightful

### Loading States
1. Skeleton screens > spinners
2. Progress indicators for long ops
3. Optimistic UI when safe
4. Background loading when possible

### Error States
1. Explain what happened
2. Suggest how to fix
3. Provide recovery action
4. Don't blame user

### Success States
1. Confirm action completed
2. Show next steps
3. Celebrate achievements
4. Enable sharing if appropriate

## Content Strategy

### Microcopy
- Be concise
- Use active voice
- Avoid jargon
- Be human
- Guide action

### Labels & Instructions
- Clear and specific
- Explain why (if needed)
- Example formats
- Required vs optional

### Error Messages
- Specific problem
- How to fix
- No technical jargon
- Apologetic when appropriate

## Metrics That Matter

### Engagement
- Time on task
- Completion rate
- Return visits
- Feature adoption

### Satisfaction
- NPS score
- CSAT surveys
- Support tickets
- User feedback

### Performance
- Task success rate
- Error rate
- Time to first action
- Abandonment points

## Design Review Checklist

### Functionality
- [ ] Primary task achievable
- [ ] Edge cases handled
- [ ] Error states designed
- [ ] Loading states designed

### Usability
- [ ] Clear visual hierarchy
- [ ] Obvious next actions
- [ ] Consistent with patterns
- [ ] Minimal cognitive load

### Accessibility
- [ ] Keyboard navigable
- [ ] Screen reader tested
- [ ] Color contrast sufficient
- [ ] Touch targets sized

### Polish
- [ ] Micro-interactions smooth
- [ ] Copy clear and helpful
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints
- [ ] Performance acceptable

## See Also

- Project-specific UX skills for detailed requirements
- `.claude/skills/generic-design-system/` - Visual design
- `CLAUDE.md` - Project constraints

# Code Review Standards

Shared reference for security, performance, accessibility, and quality requirements.

## Security Checks

### Frontend
- Sanitize user input (textContent, not innerHTML)
- Use `unknown` type for external data, validate before use
- No dynamic code execution (eval, Function constructor)
- No API keys in client code
- HTTPS for external requests

### Backend
- Input validation on all endpoints
- Authentication on protected routes
- No raw SQL (use ORM/parameterized queries)
- Secrets in environment variables
- Rate limiting on public endpoints

### General
- No tracking without consent
- Privacy-first data handling
- WebCrypto API for encryption (no custom crypto)

## Performance Requirements

### Bundle Size Targets
| Project Type | Target |
|--------------|--------|
| Static site | < 50KB |
| SPA/React | < 100KB gzipped |
| Full-stack | < 200KB gzipped |

### Animation
- GPU-accelerated only (transform, opacity)
- 60fps target
- Avoid layout thrashing
- Use requestAnimationFrame

### Loading
- Lazy load non-essential code/images
- Code splitting for large features
- Optimize images (WebP, responsive)

## Accessibility (WCAG AA)

### Required
- Focus indicators on all interactive elements
- Keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter, Escape, Arrows)
- Color contrast >= 4.5:1 for text
- ARIA labels on icon-only buttons
- Alt text for meaningful images
- Semantic HTML structure

### Interactive Elements
- Focus trapped in modals
- Escape closes dialogs
- Skip links for navigation
- Form errors announced

## Code Quality

### Principles
- DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself)
- Single Responsibility
- Explicit over implicit
- No magic numbers/strings
- Self-documenting code

### Language-Specific

**HTML:** Semantic tags, proper heading hierarchy, meaningful class names

**CSS:** Mobile-first, avoid !important, CSS custom properties, consistent naming

**JavaScript/TypeScript:** ES6+, strict mode, no `any` for user data, async/await, defensive programming

**Python:** Type hints, docstrings, PEP 8, context managers, proper exceptions

## Testing Requirements

| Type | When |
|------|------|
| Unit | Business logic, utilities |
| Integration | API endpoints, data flow |
| E2E | Critical user journeys |

## Review Checklist

### Pre-Commit
- [ ] Tests pass
- [ ] Type check passes
- [ ] Lint passes
- [ ] Build succeeds
- [ ] No console errors

### Security
- [ ] Input sanitized
- [ ] Auth on protected routes
- [ ] No exposed secrets

### Performance
- [ ] Bundle size acceptable
- [ ] Images optimized
- [ ] Lazy loading used

### Accessibility
- [ ] Keyboard navigable
- [ ] Focus indicators visible
- [ ] Contrast sufficient
- [ ] ARIA labels present

# Design Patterns

Shared reference for typography, spacing, colors, and visual design.

## Typography Scale

| Name | Size | Line Height | Use |
|------|------|-------------|-----|
| xs | 12px | 16px | Captions, labels |
| sm | 14px | 20px | Secondary text |
| base | 16px | 24px | Body text |
| lg | 18px | 28px | Lead paragraphs |
| xl | 20px | 28px | Section headers |
| 2xl | 24px | 32px | Page headers |
| 3xl | 30px | 36px | Hero text |
| 4xl | 36px | 40px | Display |

### Font Weights
| Weight | Value | Use |
|--------|-------|-----|
| Normal | 400 | Body text |
| Medium | 500 | UI elements |
| Semibold | 600 | Subheadings |
| Bold | 700 | Headings |

## Spacing System

Use 4px or 8px base unit for consistency.

| Token | 4px Base | 8px Base | Use |
|-------|----------|----------|-----|
| 1 | 4px | 8px | Tight spacing |
| 2 | 8px | 16px | Default gap |
| 3 | 12px | 24px | Section padding |
| 4 | 16px | 32px | Card padding |
| 6 | 24px | 48px | Page margins |
| 8 | 32px | 64px | Large sections |

## Responsive Breakpoints

| Token | Min Width | Devices |
|-------|-----------|---------|
| sm | 640px | Large phones |
| md | 768px | Tablets |
| lg | 1024px | Laptops |
| xl | 1280px | Desktops |
| 2xl | 1536px | Large desktops |

## Color System

Define for each project:
- **Primary**: Main brand color
- **Secondary**: Supporting color
- **Accent**: Highlight/CTA
- **Background/Foreground**: Base colors
- **Destructive/Success/Warning**: State colors

### Contrast Requirements
| Use Case | Minimum Ratio |
|----------|---------------|
| Body text | 4.5:1 (AA) |
| Large text | 3:1 (AA) |
| UI components | 3:1 (AA) |

## Border Radius

| Token | Value | Use |
|-------|-------|-----|
| sm | 2px | Subtle rounding |
| DEFAULT | 4px | Buttons, inputs |
| md | 6px | Cards |
| lg | 8px | Modals |
| full | 9999px | Pills, avatars |

## Animation

### Duration
| Token | Duration | Use |
|-------|----------|-----|
| fast | 100ms | Micro-interactions |
| DEFAULT | 200ms | Most transitions |
| slow | 300ms | Complex animations |

### GPU-Accelerated Properties
**DO animate:** transform, opacity
**AVOID:** width, height, top/left/right/bottom, margin, padding

## Visual Hierarchy

- Size indicates importance
- Contrast draws attention
- Whitespace groups elements
- F-pattern/Z-pattern reading

## Component Patterns

### Buttons
| Variant | Use |
|---------|-----|
| Primary | Main actions |
| Secondary | Alternative actions |
| Ghost | Subtle actions |
| Destructive | Delete/remove |

### Form Inputs
- Clear focus states
- Error states with messages
- Label always visible
- Required indicator

### Modals
- Focus trapped inside
- Escape to close
- Dark overlay
- Centered or slide-in
