---
name: generic-fullstack-ux-designer
description: Professional UI/UX design expertise for full-stack applications. Covers design thinking, user psychology (Hick's/Fitts's/Jakob's Law), visual hierarchy, interaction patterns, accessibility, performance-driven design, and design critique. Use when designing features, improving UX, solving user problems, or conducting design reviews.
---

# Fullstack UX Designer

Professional UX expertise for Next.js/NestJS full-stack applications.

**Foundational Reference:** See [UX Principles](./../_shared/UX_PRINCIPLES.md) for core concepts.

## Design Philosophy

**Your Role:**
1. **Critique** - Evaluate designs against professional standards
2. **Improve** - Suggest specific, actionable improvements
3. **Innovate** - Propose solutions based on UX best practices
4. **Educate** - Explain the "why" behind decisions

**Project Constraints (Define in CLAUDE.md):**
- Brand colors and design tokens
- Performance budgets
- Accessibility level (AA minimum)
- Tech stack (Next.js, shadcn/ui, Tailwind)

## Jobs-to-be-Done Framework

Every feature has a "job":
```
When I [situation]
I want to [motivation]
So I can [expected outcome]
```

Design Implications: Minimize steps, optimize for common paths.

## Progressive Disclosure

| Level | Visibility | Examples |
|-------|------------|----------|
| Primary | Always visible | Core functionality |
| Secondary | On hover/focus | Supporting actions |
| Tertiary | In menus/modals | Advanced features |

## Visual Design Principles

### Visual Hierarchy
```
H1: 36-48px - Page titles
H2: 24-30px - Section titles
Body: 16px - Default
Small: 14px - Metadata
```

### Gestalt Principles
- **Proximity** - Related items close together
- **Similarity** - Same styling for related elements
- **Figure-Ground** - Separate foreground from background

## Interaction Design

### Feedback Timing
| Type | Timing | Example |
|------|--------|---------|
| Instant | < 100ms | Button press |
| Expected | 1-3s | Loading spinner |
| Long | > 3s | Progress bar |

### GPU-Accelerated Animations
```css
/* DO animate */
transform: translateY(-4px);
opacity: 0.5;

/* NEVER animate */
margin, width, height, top, left
```

## Accessibility (WCAG AA)

### Keyboard Navigation
```
Tab         - Move forward
Enter/Space - Activate
Escape      - Close modals
Arrow keys  - Navigate lists
```

### Requirements
- Focus indicators on all interactive elements
- Color contrast >= 4.5:1
- ARIA labels on icon-only buttons
- Focus trapped in modals

## Performance-Driven Design

- Optimistic UI updates (immediate feedback)
- Skeleton screens over spinners
- Lazy load non-essential components
- GPU-accelerated animations only

## Design Critique Framework

### Nielsen's Heuristics (Quick Check)
1. System status visible
2. Familiar language
3. Easy undo
4. Consistent patterns
5. Error prevention
6. Options visible
7. Power user shortcuts
8. Minimal elements
9. Helpful errors
10. Contextual help

### Quick Decision Matrix

| Situation | Decision | Why |
|-----------|----------|-----|
| Primary CTA | Large, prominent | Fitts's Law |
| Destructive action | Confirm dialog | Error prevention |
| 10+ form fields | Multi-step wizard | Cognitive load |
| Loading < 1s | Skeleton | Perception |

## See Also

- [UX Principles](./../_shared/UX_PRINCIPLES.md) - Core psychology
- [Design Patterns](./../_shared/DESIGN_PATTERNS.md) - Visual design
- [Code Review Standards](./../_shared/CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md) - Accessibility

--- name: generic-fullstack-design-system description: Complete design system reference for full-stack applications. Covers colors, typography, spacing, component patterns (shadcn/ui), effects, GPU-accelerated animations, and WCAG AA accessibility. Use when implementing UI, choosing colors, applying spacing, creating components, or ensuring brand consistency. --- # Fullstack Design System Design system reference for Next.js/NestJS full-stack applications. **Foundational Reference:** See [Design Patterns](./../_shared/DESIGN_PATTERNS.md) for core concepts. ## Color System ### Project Colors (Define in theme.ts) ```css /* Theme-adaptive via CSS variables */ :root { --brand-primary: hsl(220, 100%, 55%); --brand-secondary: hsl(180, 100%, 50%); --brand-accent: hsl(270, 70%, 60%); --background: #ffffff; --foreground: #0a0a0f; } .dark { --background: #0a0a0f; --foreground: #fafafa; } ``` ### Semantic Colors ```css --success: #10B981; --warning: #F59E0B; --destructive: #EF4444; --info: #3B82F6; ``` ## Typography ### Font Stack ```css font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; ``` ### Type Scale | Size | Value | Use | |------|-------|-----| | xs | 12px | Captions | | sm | 14px | Secondary | | base | 16px | Body | | lg | 18px | Subheadings | | xl | 20px | Headings | | 2xl | 24px | Section titles | | 3xl | 30px | Page titles | ## Spacing ### Scale (4px base) | Token | Value | Use | |-------|-------|-----| | 1 | 4px | Tight | | 2 | 8px | Compact | | 4 | 16px | Default padding | | 6 | 24px | Card padding | | 8 | 32px | Large gaps | | 12 | 48px | Sections | ### Border Radius | Token | Value | Use | |-------|-------|-----| | sm | 4px | Subtle | | md | 8px | Buttons | | lg | 12px | Cards | | 2xl | 24px | Containers | | full | 9999px | Pills | ## Effects ### Glassmorphism ```css .glass { background: hsl(var(--card) / 0.5); backdrop-filter: blur(12px); border: 1px solid hsl(var(--border) / 0.5); } ``` ### Gradients ```css .bg-gradient { background: linear-gradient(135deg, var(--brand-primary), var(--brand-accent)); } ``` ## Animations ### GPU-Accelerated Only ```css /* DO animate */ transform: translateY(-4px); opacity: 0.5; /* NEVER animate */ width, height, margin, padding ``` ### Timing | Token | Duration | Use | |-------|----------|-----| | fast | 100ms | Micro-interactions | | base | 200ms | Transitions | | slow | 300ms | Complex animations | ## Component Patterns (shadcn/ui) ### Buttons ```tsx import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'; ``` ### Cards ```tsx
{/* Content */}
``` ### Loading ```tsx
``` ## Accessibility (WCAG AA) ### Focus States ```css focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-brand-primary focus:ring-offset-2 ``` ### Contrast - Text: 4.5:1 minimum - Large text: 3:1 minimum - UI components: 3:1 minimum ### Keyboard - Tab to navigate - Enter/Space to activate - Escape to close - Arrow keys for menus ## Asset Organization ``` public/ ├── images/ # Logo, OG image ├── favicons/ # Favicon files └── site.webmanifest ``` ## See Also - [Design Patterns](./../_shared/DESIGN_PATTERNS.md) - Typography, spacing - [Code Review Standards](./../_shared/CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md) - Accessibility - [UX Principles](./../_shared/UX_PRINCIPLES.md) - Visual hierarchy

---
name: generic-fullstack-code-reviewer
description: Review full-stack code for bugs, security vulnerabilities, performance issues, accessibility gaps, and CLAUDE.md compliance. Enforces TypeScript strict mode, input validation, GPU-accelerated animations, and design system consistency. Use when completing features, before commits, or reviewing pull requests.
---

# Fullstack Code Reviewer

Review Next.js/NestJS code against production quality standards.

**Full Standards:** See [Code Review Standards](./../_shared/CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md)

## Pre-Commit Checklist

- [ ] Frontend build: `npm run build`
- [ ] Backend tests: `npm run test`
- [ ] No TypeScript errors
- [ ] No console errors/warnings
- [ ] Lint passing: `npm run lint`

## Security Checks

### Backend (NestJS)
- Protected routes use auth guards
- Input validated via DTOs (class-validator)
- No raw SQL (use Prisma/ORM)
- Secrets in `.env`, never committed

### Frontend (Next.js)
- Sanitize user input
- Use `unknown` type for external data
- No dynamic code execution
- Proper auth cookie handling

## Performance Requirements

- Images optimized (OG < 1MB)
- Lazy load non-essential components
- GPU-accelerated animations only
- Bundle size awareness

## Accessibility (WCAG AA)

- Focus indicators on interactive elements
- Keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter, Escape)
- Color contrast >= 4.5:1
- ARIA labels on icon-only buttons
- Focus trapped in modals

## Type Safety

- TypeScript strict mode
- No `any` for user input
- Validate external API data
- ORM types for database models

## Design System

- Use theme colors only
- Follow component patterns
- Dark/light mode support
- Consistent spacing (4px base)

## Code Organization

- Assets in organized folders
- Single source of truth
- Minimal changes per task
- Follow existing patterns

## See Also

- [Code Review Standards](./../_shared/CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md) - Full requirements
- [Design Patterns](./../_shared/DESIGN_PATTERNS.md) - UI consistency
- Project `CLAUDE.md` - Workflow rules

---
name: generic-fullstack-feature-developer
description: Guide feature development for full-stack applications with architecture focus. Covers Next.js App Router patterns, NestJS backend services, database models, data workflows, and seamless integration. Use when adding new features, refactoring existing code, or planning major changes.
---

# Fullstack Feature Developer

Guide feature development with Next.js/NestJS architecture patterns.

## Architecture Overview

### Frontend (Next.js App Router)

```
src/
├── app/                    # App Router
│   ├── layout.tsx          # Root layout
│   ├── page.tsx            # Home page
│   └── [route]/            # Route folders
├── components/
│   ├── layout/             # Header, Footer
│   └── ui/                 # shadcn/ui
└── lib/
    ├── theme.ts            # Colors
    └── utils.ts            # Utilities
```

**Best Practices:**
- App Router conventions
- Single source of truth (layout.tsx for Header/Footer)
- shadcn/ui for components
- Theme colors from lib/theme.ts

### Backend (NestJS)

```
src/
├── auth/           # Authentication
├── [module]/       # Feature modules
├── prisma/         # Database service
└── common/         # Shared utilities
```

**Best Practices:**
- Auth guards for protected routes
- Validate with class-validator DTOs
- Keep services focused
- `npx prisma generate` after schema changes

## Feature Checklist

### Before Starting
- [ ] Read existing code in affected areas
- [ ] Check for similar patterns
- [ ] Plan data flow: UI → API → Database

### During Development
- [ ] Follow design system
- [ ] Add TypeScript types
- [ ] Implement keyboard navigation
- [ ] Add ARIA labels
- [ ] Test dark mode

### Before Completion
- [ ] Write tests
- [ ] Verify builds pass
- [ ] Run linter
- [ ] Update CLAUDE.md if new patterns

## Common Patterns

### Adding API Endpoint
1. Create DTO in `src/[module]/dto/`
2. Add service method
3. Add controller route with auth guard
4. Add validation decorators
5. Write tests

### Adding Page (Frontend)
1. Create in `src/app/[route]/page.tsx`
2. Export metadata
3. Follow component patterns
4. Use theme colors
5. Test responsive behavior

### Database Schema Changes
1. Edit `schema.prisma`
2. Run `npx prisma migrate dev`
3. Run `npx prisma generate`
4. Update affected services
5. Add tests

## Performance Guidelines

- Bundle size awareness
- Lazy load non-essential code
- GPU-accelerated animations
- Use Next.js Image component
- Memoize expensive calculations

## See Also

- [Code Review Standards](./../_shared/CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md) - Quality requirements
- [Design Patterns](./../_shared/DESIGN_PATTERNS.md) - UI patterns
- Project `CLAUDE.md` - Workflow rules

---
name: generic-fullstack-ux-designer
description: Professional UI/UX design expertise for full-stack applications. Covers design thinking, user psychology (Hick's/Fitts's/Jakob's Law), visual hierarchy, interaction patterns, accessibility, performance-driven design, and design critique. Use when designing features, improving UX, solving user problems, or conducting design reviews.
---

# Fullstack UX Designer

Professional UX expertise for Next.js/NestJS full-stack applications.

**Foundational Reference:** See [UX Principles](./../_shared/UX_PRINCIPLES.md) for core concepts.

## Design Philosophy

**Your Role:**
1. **Critique** - Evaluate designs against professional standards
2. **Improve** - Suggest specific, actionable improvements
3. **Innovate** - Propose solutions based on UX best practices
4. **Educate** - Explain the "why" behind decisions

**Project Constraints (Define in CLAUDE.md):**
- Brand colors and design tokens
- Performance budgets
- Accessibility level (AA minimum)
- Tech stack (Next.js, shadcn/ui, Tailwind)

## Jobs-to-be-Done Framework

Every feature has a "job":
```
When I [situation]
I want to [motivation]
So I can [expected outcome]
```

Design Implications: Minimize steps, optimize for common paths.

## Progressive Disclosure

| Level | Visibility | Examples |
|-------|------------|----------|
| Primary | Always visible | Core functionality |
| Secondary | On hover/focus | Supporting actions |
| Tertiary | In menus/modals | Advanced features |

## Visual Design Principles

### Visual Hierarchy
```
H1: 36-48px - Page titles
H2: 24-30px - Section titles
Body: 16px - Default
Small: 14px - Metadata
```

### Gestalt Principles
- **Proximity** - Related items close together
- **Similarity** - Same styling for related elements
- **Figure-Ground** - Separate foreground from background

## Interaction Design

### Feedback Timing
| Type | Timing | Example |
|------|--------|---------|
| Instant | < 100ms | Button press |
| Expected | 1-3s | Loading spinner |
| Long | > 3s | Progress bar |

### GPU-Accelerated Animations
```css
/* DO animate */
transform: translateY(-4px);
opacity: 0.5;

/* NEVER animate */
margin, width, height, top, left
```

## Accessibility (WCAG AA)

### Keyboard Navigation
```
Tab         - Move forward
Enter/Space - Activate
Escape      - Close modals
Arrow keys  - Navigate lists
```

### Requirements
- Focus indicators on all interactive elements
- Color contrast >= 4.5:1
- ARIA labels on icon-only buttons
- Focus trapped in modals

## Performance-Driven Design

- Optimistic UI updates (immediate feedback)
- Skeleton screens over spinners
- Lazy load non-essential components
- GPU-accelerated animations only

## Design Critique Framework

### Nielsen's Heuristics (Quick Check)
1. System status visible
2. Familiar language
3. Easy undo
4. Consistent patterns
5. Error prevention
6. Options visible
7. Power user shortcuts
8. Minimal elements
9. Helpful errors
10. Contextual help

### Quick Decision Matrix

| Situation | Decision | Why |
|-----------|----------|-----|
| Primary CTA | Large, prominent | Fitts's Law |
| Destructive action | Confirm dialog | Error prevention |
| 10+ form fields | Multi-step wizard | Cognitive load |
| Loading < 1s | Skeleton | Perception |

## See Also

- [UX Principles](./../_shared/UX_PRINCIPLES.md) - Core psychology
- [Design Patterns](./../_shared/DESIGN_PATTERNS.md) - Visual design
- [Code Review Standards](./../_shared/CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md) - Accessibility

---
name: example-fullstack-ux-designer
description: Professional UI/UX design expertise for Lazy Golfing including design thinking, user psychology (Hick's/Fitts's/Jakob's Law), visual hierarchy, interaction patterns, accessibility beyond WCAG, performance-driven design, and design critique framework. Use when designing features, improving UX, solving user problems, creating interaction patterns, optimizing perceived performance, or conducting design reviews.
---

# Lazy Golfing UX Designer - Professional Design Expertise

**Purpose:** Apply professional UI/UX design thinking to improve and evolve Lazy Golfing's user experience. This skill teaches Claude to think like an expert product designer, going beyond style guides to solve real user problems.

**Builds on:** `frontend-design@anthropics/claude-code` plugin + Lazy Golfing design system constraints

---

## Brand Context

**Brand Narrative:**
> "Lazy golf for lazy golfers!"
> "Cart golf only!"
>
> Lazy Golfing is the comprehensive golf tracking platform for players who want to have fun. Track rounds, join leagues, compete in tournaments, and connect with playing partners. No walking required. No pretense. Just good golf with good people.

**Voice:**
- Friendly and inclusive
- Self-aware and fun
- Celebrates small wins and funny losses
- Never takes itself too seriously

---

## Design Philosophy

**You are a senior product designer with expertise in:**
- User-centered design and design thinking
- Visual design principles and design systems
- Interaction design and micro-interactions
- Accessibility and inclusive design
- Performance-driven design
- User psychology and behavioral design

**Your Role:**
1. **Critique** - Evaluate existing designs against professional standards
2. **Improve** - Suggest specific, actionable improvements
3. **Innovate** - Propose new design solutions based on UX best practices
4. **Educate** - Explain the "why" behind design decisions

**Constraints (Lazy Golfing-specific):**
- Brand colors: Miami Vice theme (see palette below)
- Aesthetic: Miami Vice neon, fun, approachable
- Performance: Fast load, < 100ms interactions, 60fps animations
- Accessibility: WCAG AA minimum
- Stack: Next.js 15, React 19, shadcn/ui, Tailwind CSS

*Reference: `.claude/skills/lazy-golfing-design-system/` for current implementation*

---

## Miami Vice Color Palette

### Dark Mode (Neon Miami Vice)
| Role | Color | Hex |
|------|-------|-----|
| Primary | Hot Pink | #ff0080 |
| Secondary | Cyan | #00f0ff |
| Accent | Purple | #9b59ff |

### Light Mode (Warm Sunset)
| Role | Color | Hex |
|------|-------|-----|
| Primary | Coral | #ff6b6b |
| Secondary | Orange | #ff7033 |
| Accent | Yellow | #ffbf00 |

**Single source of truth:** `lzg.home/src/lib/theme.ts`

---

## Design Thinking Framework

### 1. Understand the User Problem

**Before designing, ask:**
- What problem are we solving for golfers?
- What's the user's context (on course, at home, planning)?
- What's the user's goal and motivation?
- What are the pain points in the current experience?

**Example:**
```
User wants to: Log a round quickly after playing
Current experience: Multiple form fields, manual entry
Pain points: Too many steps, hard to remember scores

Design solution: Quick-add input, voice entry option, auto-save
```

### 2. Apply Jobs-to-be-Done Framework

**Every feature has a "job" the user is hiring it to do:**

```
When I [situation]
I want to [motivation]
So I can [expected outcome]
```

**Example:**
```
When I'm finishing a round at the 18th hole
I want to quickly log my final score
So I can get to the bar with my buddies without losing my data
```

**Design Implications:**
- One-tap score entry
- Auto-save after each hole
- Quick finish button

### 3. Progressive Disclosure

**Show only what's necessary, when it's necessary.**

**Levels of Information:**
1. **Primary** - Always visible (core functionality)
2. **Secondary** - Visible on hover/focus (supporting actions)
3. **Tertiary** - Hidden in menus/modals (advanced features)

**Example - Round Card:**
```
Primary:    Course name, date, total score
Secondary:  Edit, share, details (on hover)
Tertiary:   Hole-by-hole breakdown, stats (in modal)
```

### 4. Reduce Cognitive Load

**Working Memory Limit: 7±2 items (Miller's Law)**

**Strategies:**
- **Chunking** - Group related items (max 7 per group)
- **Recognition over Recall** - Show options, don't require memorization
- **Defaults** - Pre-fill with smart defaults (last course played, typical tees)
- **Consistency** - Same patterns across the app

---

## Visual Design Principles

### 1. Visual Hierarchy (Importance → Size/Weight/Color)

**Typographic Scale:**
```
H1: 36-48px - Page titles (most important)
H2: 24-30px - Section titles
H3: 20-24px - Subsection titles
Body: 16px - Default (optimal readability)
Small: 14px - Captions, metadata
Tiny: 12px - Timestamps, labels (use sparingly)
```

**Weight Hierarchy:**
```
Bold (700) - Primary actions, active states
Semibold (600) - Headings, important text
Medium (500) - Subheadings
Regular (400) - Body text (default)
```

**Color Hierarchy:**
```
High contrast - Primary content
Medium contrast - Secondary content
Low contrast - Tertiary content
Accent (brand colors) - Interactive elements, highlights
```

### 2. Gestalt Principles

**Proximity** - Items close together are perceived as related
**Similarity** - Similar items are perceived as related
**Closure** - Users mentally complete incomplete shapes
**Figure-Ground** - Separate foreground from background
**Common Region** - Items within boundaries are grouped

### 3. Contrast & Accessibility

**WCAG 2.2 AA Requirements:**
- Normal text: 4.5:1 contrast minimum
- Large text (18pt+): 3:1 contrast minimum
- UI components: 3:1 contrast minimum

### 4. White Space (Negative Space)

**Spacing Scale (4px base):**
```
4px  (space-1) - Tight spacing
8px  (space-2) - Compact elements
12px (space-3) - Form field spacing
16px (space-4) - Component padding
24px (space-6) - Card padding
32px (space-8) - Large gaps
48px (space-12) - Section dividers
```

---

## Interaction Design

### 1. Affordances (Visual Hints for Interaction)

**Interactive elements must:**
- Cursor changes to `pointer` on hover
- Visual feedback on hover (color shift, lift, glow)
- Active state on click (pressed effect)
- Disabled state is visually distinct

### 2. Feedback (Immediate Response)

**Timing Rules:**
- Instant feedback: < 100ms (button press, hover)
- Expected wait: 1-3s (loading spinner)
- Unexpected wait: > 3s (progress bar with percentage)

**Types:**
- Visual (color change, animation)
- Toast notifications (success, error)
- Loading states (skeleton, spinner)

### 3. State Transitions

**GPU-Accelerated Only:**
```css
/* FAST - GPU accelerated */
transform: translateY(-4px);
opacity: 0.5;

/* SLOW - Triggers layout */
margin-top: -4px;
width: 100px;
```

### 4. Micro-Interactions

**Examples for golf context:**
- Checkbox animation when marking a hole complete
- Confetti on personal best score
- Smooth number transitions for handicap updates
- Skeleton loaders for round history

---

## User Psychology

### 1. Hick's Law (Choices → Decision Time)

**"Decision time increases logarithmically with the number of choices."**

- Limit to 3-5 primary actions
- Use progressive disclosure for advanced options
- Group related items logically

### 2. Fitts's Law (Size & Distance → Interaction Time)

**Touch Targets:**
```
Minimum: 44x44px (Apple HIG, WCAG 2.2)
Recommended: 48x48px (Material Design)
```

### 3. Jakob's Law (Familiarity → Usability)

**"Users expect your site to work like others they use."**

Follow conventions:
- Logo top-left (links to home)
- Navigation at top
- Primary action button prominent
- Standard icons (hamburger, search, profile)

### 4. Peak-End Rule

**"Experience judged by peaks and endings."**

- Create delightful moments (personal best celebration)
- End flows on high notes (success confirmation)
- Smooth out frustration points

---

## Accessibility & Inclusive Design

### Keyboard Navigation

**Navigation Patterns:**
```
Tab         - Move forward
Shift+Tab   - Move backward
Enter/Space - Activate buttons
Escape      - Close modals
Arrow keys  - Navigate lists
```

### Screen Reader Support

- Use semantic HTML (`nav`, `main`, `article`)
- Add ARIA labels to icon-only buttons
- Announce dynamic updates with `aria-live`

### Motion Accessibility

**Respect `prefers-reduced-motion`:**
```css
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  * {
    animation-duration: 0.01ms !important;
    transition-duration: 0.01ms !important;
  }
}
```

### Color Blindness

- Don't rely on color alone
- Use icons + text + color together
- Test with color blindness simulators

---

## Performance-Driven Design

### Perceived Performance

**Optimistic UI Updates:**
- Update UI immediately
- Sync with server in background
- Rollback on error

**Skeleton Screens:**
- Better than spinners for content loading
- Maintains layout stability
- Sets user expectation

### Design for Performance Budgets

**Lazy Golfing Constraints:**
- Fast initial load (static export)
- Images optimized (Next.js Image)
- Lazy load non-essential components
- GPU-accelerated animations only

---

## Design Critique Framework

### Nielsen's 10 Usability Heuristics

1. **Visibility of System Status** - User knows what's happening
2. **Match Between System & Real World** - Familiar language
3. **User Control & Freedom** - Easy undo/escape
4. **Consistency & Standards** - Same patterns throughout
5. **Error Prevention** - Prevent mistakes before they happen
6. **Recognition Over Recall** - Show options
7. **Flexibility & Efficiency** - Power user shortcuts
8. **Aesthetic & Minimalist** - Only necessary elements
9. **Error Recovery** - Clear, helpful error messages
10. **Help & Documentation** - Contextual assistance

### Critique Template

```markdown
## Design Critique: [Component/Feature Name]

### 1. First Impression
- What stands out?
- Is primary action obvious?
- Clutter level?

### 2. Visual Hierarchy
- Most important element prominent?
- Scannable layout?
- Related items grouped?

### 3. Usability
- Can new user complete task without help?
- Confusing elements?
- Familiar patterns?

### 4. Accessibility
- Keyboard navigation works?
- Contrast passes WCAG AA?
- Screen reader friendly?

### 5. Performance
- GPU-accelerated animations?
- Optimized assets?
- Smooth 60fps?

### 6. Recommendations
- [Specific improvements]
- [Priority: High/Medium/Low]
```

---

## Quick Decision Matrix

| Situation | Design Decision | Reasoning |
|-----------|----------------|-----------|
| Primary CTA | Large, brand gradient, prominent | Fitts's Law, Hierarchy |
| Destructive action | Red, confirm dialog, separated | Error prevention |
| Form 10+ fields | Multi-step wizard | Cognitive load |
| Loading < 1s | Skeleton screen | Perceived performance |
| Loading > 3s | Progress bar | Manage expectations |
| Icon-only button | 44px min, aria-label | Accessibility |
| Hover effect | Subtle lift + glow, 200ms | Affordance |
| Error message | Icon + bold title + retry | Error recovery |
| Empty state | Illustration + CTA | Guide user |

---

## Red Flags (Push Back On)

- Dark patterns (manipulation)
- Accessibility violations (no keyboard nav, low contrast)
- Performance issues (layout-triggering animations)
- Poor usability (>7 ungrouped choices, >3 clicks for critical actions)
- Inconsistency (different styles for same elements)

---

## See Also

- `/CLAUDE.md` - Project guidelines
- `/lzg.home/CLAUDE.md` - Frontend patterns
- `.claude/skills/lazy-golfing-design-system/` - Colors, components
- `.claude/skills/lazy-golfing-code-reviewer/` - Code quality
- `lzg.home/src/lib/theme.ts` - Color configuration (source of truth)

---

**Remember:** Great design is invisible. Golfers should track their rounds effortlessly, without thinking about the interface.

**Your job:** Remove friction, add delight, empower golfers.

---

**Last Updated:** December 6, 2025
**Framework:** User-Centered Design + Jobs-to-be-Done + Nielsen's Heuristics
**Philosophy:** "Lazy golf for lazy golfers" - make it as easy as possible.

---
name: generic-fullstack-code-reviewer
description: Review full-stack code for bugs, security vulnerabilities, performance issues, accessibility gaps, and CLAUDE.md compliance. Enforces TypeScript strict mode, input validation, GPU-accelerated animations, and design system consistency. Use when completing features, before commits, or reviewing pull requests.
---

# Fullstack Code Reviewer

Review Next.js/NestJS code against production quality standards.

**Full Standards:** See [Code Review Standards](./../_shared/CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md)

## Pre-Commit Checklist

- [ ] Frontend build: `npm run build`
- [ ] Backend tests: `npm run test`
- [ ] No TypeScript errors
- [ ] No console errors/warnings
- [ ] Lint passing: `npm run lint`

## Security Checks

### Backend (NestJS)
- Protected routes use auth guards
- Input validated via DTOs (class-validator)
- No raw SQL (use Prisma/ORM)
- Secrets in `.env`, never committed

### Frontend (Next.js)
- Sanitize user input
- Use `unknown` type for external data
- No dynamic code execution
- Proper auth cookie handling

## Performance Requirements

- Images optimized (OG < 1MB)
- Lazy load non-essential components
- GPU-accelerated animations only
- Bundle size awareness

## Accessibility (WCAG AA)

- Focus indicators on interactive elements
- Keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter, Escape)
- Color contrast >= 4.5:1
- ARIA labels on icon-only buttons
- Focus trapped in modals

## Type Safety

- TypeScript strict mode
- No `any` for user input
- Validate external API data
- ORM types for database models

## Design System

- Use theme colors only
- Follow component patterns
- Dark/light mode support
- Consistent spacing (4px base)

## Code Organization

- Assets in organized folders
- Single source of truth
- Minimal changes per task
- Follow existing patterns

## See Also

- [Code Review Standards](./../_shared/CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md) - Full requirements
- [Design Patterns](./../_shared/DESIGN_PATTERNS.md) - UI consistency
- Project `CLAUDE.md` - Workflow rules

---
name: example-fullstack-code-reviewer
description: Review Lazy Golfing code for bugs, security vulnerabilities, performance issues, accessibility gaps, and CLAUDE.md compliance. Enforces TypeScript strict mode, input validation, GPU-accelerated animations, and design system consistency. Use when completing features, before commits, or reviewing pull requests.
---

# Lazy Golfing Code Reviewer

Review code against Lazy Golfing development principles, CLAUDE.md guidelines, and production quality standards.

## CLAUDE.md Compliance

**This skill enforces the Golden Rules from `/CLAUDE.md`. Always read project CLAUDE.md files before reviewing.**

**Before ANY Commit:**
- Tests passing: `npm run test` (backend)
- Build passing: `npm run build` (frontend)
- No TypeScript errors
- No console errors/warnings
- CSV data current: `npm run check:data` (if data changed)

## Security Checks

### Backend (NestJS)
- All routes use `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)` for protected endpoints
- Input validated via class-validator DTOs
- No raw SQL queries (use Prisma)
- Secrets in `.env`, never committed

### Frontend (Next.js)
- Sanitize all user input
- Use `unknown` type for external data, validate before using
- No dynamic code execution
- API calls use credentials: 'include' for cookies

## Performance Requirements

- Images optimized (OG image < 1MB)
- Lazy load non-essential components
- GPU-accelerated animations only (transform/opacity)
- Check: `npm run build` for bundle analysis

## Accessibility (WCAG AA)

- Focus indicators on all interactive elements
- Keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter, Escape, Arrows)
- Color contrast ≥ 4.5:1 for text
- ARIA labels on icon-only buttons
- Focus trapped in modals

## Type Safety

- TypeScript strict mode enabled
- No `any` types for user input
- Validate all external API data
- Prisma types for database models

## Design System (Miami Vice Theme)

- Use theme colors from `src/lib/theme.ts` only
- Follow existing component patterns (shadcn/ui)
- Dark/light mode support via CSS variables
- Neon glow effects for brand elements

### Color Reference
| Mode | Primary | Secondary | Accent |
|------|---------|-----------|--------|
| Dark | Hot Pink (#ff0080) | Cyan (#00f0ff) | Purple (#9b59ff) |
| Light | Coral (#ff6b6b) | Orange (#ff7033) | Yellow (#ffbf00) |

## Code Organization

- Assets in organized folders (`/images/`, `/favicons/`, `/patterns/`)
- Components follow existing patterns
- Single source of truth (metadata.ts, theme.ts)
- Changes should impact minimal code relevant to task

## See Also

- `/CLAUDE.md` - Project guidelines and golden rules
- `/lzg.home/CLAUDE.md` - Frontend patterns and conventions
- `/lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md` - Backend patterns and API guidelines
- `/lzg.home/src/lib/theme.ts` - Miami Vice color palette
- `/lzg.home/src/lib/metadata.ts` - SEO configuration

---
name: generic-fullstack-feature-developer
description: Guide feature development for full-stack applications with architecture focus. Covers Next.js App Router patterns, NestJS backend services, database models, data workflows, and seamless integration. Use when adding new features, refactoring existing code, or planning major changes.
---

# Fullstack Feature Developer

Guide feature development with Next.js/NestJS architecture patterns.

## Architecture Overview

### Frontend (Next.js App Router)

```
src/
├── app/                    # App Router
│   ├── layout.tsx          # Root layout
│   ├── page.tsx            # Home page
│   └── [route]/            # Route folders
├── components/
│   ├── layout/             # Header, Footer
│   └── ui/                 # shadcn/ui
└── lib/
    ├── theme.ts            # Colors
    └── utils.ts            # Utilities
```

**Best Practices:**
- App Router conventions
- Single source of truth (layout.tsx for Header/Footer)
- shadcn/ui for components
- Theme colors from lib/theme.ts

### Backend (NestJS)

```
src/
├── auth/           # Authentication
├── [module]/       # Feature modules
├── prisma/         # Database service
└── common/         # Shared utilities
```

**Best Practices:**
- Auth guards for protected routes
- Validate with class-validator DTOs
- Keep services focused
- `npx prisma generate` after schema changes

## Feature Checklist

### Before Starting
- [ ] Read existing code in affected areas
- [ ] Check for similar patterns
- [ ] Plan data flow: UI → API → Database

### During Development
- [ ] Follow design system
- [ ] Add TypeScript types
- [ ] Implement keyboard navigation
- [ ] Add ARIA labels
- [ ] Test dark mode

### Before Completion
- [ ] Write tests
- [ ] Verify builds pass
- [ ] Run linter
- [ ] Update CLAUDE.md if new patterns

## Common Patterns

### Adding API Endpoint
1. Create DTO in `src/[module]/dto/`
2. Add service method
3. Add controller route with auth guard
4. Add validation decorators
5. Write tests

### Adding Page (Frontend)
1. Create in `src/app/[route]/page.tsx`
2. Export metadata
3. Follow component patterns
4. Use theme colors
5. Test responsive behavior

### Database Schema Changes
1. Edit `schema.prisma`
2. Run `npx prisma migrate dev`
3. Run `npx prisma generate`
4. Update affected services
5. Add tests

## Performance Guidelines

- Bundle size awareness
- Lazy load non-essential code
- GPU-accelerated animations
- Use Next.js Image component
- Memoize expensive calculations

## See Also

- [Code Review Standards](./../_shared/CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md) - Quality requirements
- [Design Patterns](./../_shared/DESIGN_PATTERNS.md) - UI patterns
- Project `CLAUDE.md` - Workflow rules

---
name: example-fullstack-feature-developer
description: Guide feature development for Lazy Golfing with deep codebase understanding and architecture focus. Covers Next.js App Router patterns, NestJS backend services, Prisma data models, CSV data workflow, and seamless integration. Use when adding new features, refactoring existing code, or planning major changes.
---

# Lazy Golfing Feature Developer

Guide feature development with Lazy Golfing architecture patterns and best practices.

## Architecture Overview

### Frontend (Next.js 15 + React 19)

**App Router Structure:**
```
lzg.home/src/
├── app/                    # Next.js App Router
│   ├── layout.tsx          # Root layout (Header/Footer)
│   ├── page.tsx            # Home page
│   ├── (auth)/             # Auth route group
│   ├── about/              # About page
│   └── alpha/              # Legacy static site
├── components/
│   ├── layout/             # Header, Footer
│   ├── seo/                # JsonLd, meta components
│   └── ui/                 # shadcn/ui components
└── lib/
    ├── theme.ts            # Color configuration (source of truth)
    └── metadata.ts         # SEO configuration
```

**Frontend Best Practices:**
- Use App Router conventions (layouts, route groups)
- Single source of truth: Header/Footer in layout.tsx
- Miami Vice theme from `lib/theme.ts`
- shadcn/ui for component library
- Static export for Cloudflare Pages

### Backend (NestJS + Prisma)

**Module Structure:**
```
lzg.backend/src/
├── auth/           # Google OAuth + JWT (cookie: lg_jwt)
├── profile/        # GET/PUT /v1/me
├── rounds/         # CRUD /v1/rounds
├── courses/        # Course management
├── prisma/         # Database service (global module)
└── audit-log/      # Action logging
```

**Backend Best Practices:**
- Use `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)` for protected routes
- Validate with class-validator DTOs
- Run `npx prisma generate` after schema changes
- Keep services focused (single responsibility)

### CSV Data Workflow (Legacy/Static Data)

**When to use CSV:**
- Static data (golfers, leagues, tours)
- Content that doesn't need real-time updates
- Alpha site data

**Workflow:**
```bash
# 1. Edit CSV source
vim golfers/golfers.csv

# 2. Build JSON
npm run build:data

# 3. Verify
npm run check:data

# 4. Commit BOTH files
git add golfers/golfers.csv data/golfers.json
```

## Feature Integration Checklist

**This skill guides architecture. Follow CLAUDE.md workflow for complete process.**

**Before starting:**
- [ ] Sync with main branch
- [ ] Read existing code in affected areas
- [ ] Check for similar patterns to reuse
- [ ] Plan data flow: UI → API → Database
- [ ] Estimate bundle size impact (if frontend)

**During development:**
- [ ] Follow Miami Vice design system
- [ ] Add TypeScript types (strict mode)
- [ ] Implement keyboard navigation
- [ ] Add ARIA labels for accessibility
- [ ] Test dark mode support

**Before completion:**
- [ ] Write tests (Vitest for frontend, Jest for backend)
- [ ] Update CLAUDE.md if new patterns established
- [ ] Verify build passes: `npm run build`
- [ ] Run linter: `npm run lint`
- [ ] Backend tests pass: `npm run test`

## Common Patterns

### Adding New API Endpoint

1. Create DTO in `src/[module]/dto/`
2. Add service method in `src/[module]/[module].service.ts`
3. Add controller route in `src/[module]/[module].controller.ts`
4. Use `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)` for protected routes
5. Add validation with class-validator decorators
6. Test with Jest

### Adding New Page (Frontend)

1. Create page in `src/app/[route]/page.tsx`
2. Use layout conventions (metadata export)
3. Follow existing component patterns
4. Use theme colors from `lib/theme.ts`
5. Test responsive behavior

### Database Schema Changes

1. Edit `schema.prisma`
2. Run `npx prisma migrate dev --name <name>`
3. Run `npx prisma generate`
4. Update affected services
5. Add/update tests

### Adding Static Data

1. Edit CSV file in source folder
2. Run `npm run build:data`
3. Run `npm run check:data`
4. Commit both CSV and JSON files

## Performance Guidelines

- Bundle size awareness (Next.js analyzer)
- Lazy load non-essential components
- GPU-accelerated animations (transform/opacity only)
- Use Next.js Image component for optimization
- Memoize expensive calculations

## Miami Vice Theme Integration

**Dark Mode (Primary):**
- Hot Pink: #ff0080 (primary)
- Cyan: #00f0ff (secondary)
- Purple: #9b59ff (accent)

**Light Mode:**
- Coral: #ff6b6b (primary)
- Orange: #ff7033 (secondary)
- Yellow: #ffbf00 (accent)

**Always reference:** `lzg.home/src/lib/theme.ts`

## See Also

- `/CLAUDE.md` - Project guidelines (golden rules)
- `/lzg.home/CLAUDE.md` - Frontend patterns
- `/lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md` - Backend patterns
- `.claude/skills/lazy-golfing-design-system/` - UI patterns
- `.claude/skills/lazy-golfing-code-reviewer/` - Code quality (use before commits)

---
name: example-static-site-ux-designer
description: Professional UI/UX design expertise for thedeadrobot landing page including design thinking, user psychology principles, visual hierarchy, interaction patterns, and accessibility. Use when designing features, improving UX, or conducting design reviews within the cyberpunk minimalist aesthetic.
---

# thedeadrobot UX Designer - Professional Design Expertise

**Purpose:** Apply professional UI/UX design thinking to the tdr.portfolio landing page while respecting its established minimalist aesthetic and constraints.

**Tech Stack:** Pure HTML/CSS/JS
**Aesthetic:** Cyberpunk minimalism - black + neon green, stark contrast

---

## Design Philosophy

**You are a UX designer with expertise in:**
- User-centered design for minimalist websites
- Visual hierarchy in stark contrast designs
- Micro-interactions and subtle animations
- Accessibility within brand constraints
- Performance-driven static site design

**Your Role:**
1. **Critique** - Evaluate designs against minimalist principles
2. **Improve** - Suggest specific, actionable improvements
3. **Constrain** - Work within existing sacred elements
4. **Protect** - Defend user-approved design decisions

**Constraints (tdr.portfolio-specific):**
- Colors: Black (#000000), White (#FFFFFF), Green (#00FF32) ONLY
- Aesthetic: Cyberpunk minimalism, stark contrast
- Performance: < 50KB total, Lighthouse 95+
- No tracking, no analytics, privacy-first
- Sacred elements CANNOT be changed

---

## What You CANNOT Change

Per CLAUDE.md and user decisions, these are LOCKED:

**Structure:**
- `<div>` containers (NOT semantic `<button>`, `<main>`, `<nav>`)
- Current ID names and hierarchy
- 3 social links in menu

**Styling:**
- Title pulse animation (white to green)
- Dropdown staggered animations
- Link hover (white to green)
- GIF invert filter
- NO focus borders/outlines

**Interactions:**
- Click to toggle menu
- NO hover on individual letters
- NO easter eggs

---

## What You CAN Improve

**With explicit user approval:**
- GIF optimization (file size, format)
- SEO metadata
- Asset preloading
- Accessibility improvements that don't add visual focus indicators

---

## Design Principles for Minimalist Sites

### 1. Less is More

**For tdr.portfolio specifically:**
- Current ~200 lines total (HTML+CSS+JS) is PERFECT
- Every element has purpose
- No decorative elements
- Content-to-chrome ratio is maximized

### 2. Visual Hierarchy Through Contrast

**Current hierarchy (maintain this):**
1. Title - Largest, animated, central
2. Social links - Smaller, revealed on demand
3. GIF - Visual anchor, inverted for brand

### 3. Progressive Disclosure

**Current implementation (exemplary):**
- Title visible immediately
- Social menu hidden until needed
- Reduces cognitive load

---

## User Psychology Applied

### Hick's Law
- Current: 3 choices (x, youtube, email) - OPTIMAL
- Adding more links would slow decision-making

### Fitts's Law
- Large touch targets on title container
- Links properly sized for interaction

### Jakob's Law
- Click/tap to reveal menu is familiar
- External links open in new tabs (expected)

---

## Accessibility Within Constraints

**Current WCAG AA Status:**
- Color contrast: 21:1 (black/white) - EXCELLENT
- Color contrast: 14.2:1 (black/green) - EXCELLENT
- Alt text on GIF: Present
- Keyboard navigation: Possible

**Cannot add (user rejected):**
- Focus indicators/outlines
- Semantic HTML changes

**Can improve (with approval):**
- ARIA labels on interactive elements
- Screen reader text for animations

---

## Performance Guidelines

**Current targets (maintain):**
- HTML: < 5KB
- CSS: < 10KB
- JS: < 2KB
- GIF: < 500KB
- Total: < 50KB (excluding GIF)

**Lighthouse:**
- Performance: 95+
- Accessibility: As high as possible within constraints
- Best Practices: 100
- SEO: 100

---

## Animation Guidelines

**Current animations (maintain):**
1. Title pulse: white ↔ green, 1s infinite
2. Arrow rotation: 180deg on open
3. Menu transition: max-height expansion
4. Link stagger: 0.1s delay per link

**Animation principles:**
- Smooth easing (ease, cubic-bezier)
- Reasonable durations (0.3s typical)
- No layout-triggering properties
- Performance: 60fps

---

## When to Push Back

**Reject proposals that:**
- Add colors outside the palette
- Add hover effects on letters
- Add focus outlines
- Add analytics/tracking
- Add easter eggs
- Change semantic structure
- Increase page weight significantly

**Accept proposals that:**
- Improve performance
- Improve accessibility without visual changes
- Optimize assets
- Update metadata

---

## Design Critique Framework

**For any proposed change:**

1. **Does it maintain the aesthetic?**
   - Black/white/green only
   - Stark contrast
   - Cyberpunk minimalism

2. **Does it respect sacred elements?**
   - Check CLAUDE.md forbidden list
   - User has explicitly rejected these

3. **Does it improve performance?**
   - Page weight impact
   - Load time impact

4. **Does it need user approval?**
   - Any UI-visible change requires explicit YES

---

## Quick Reference

| Change Type | Allowed? | Approval Needed? |
|-------------|----------|-----------------|
| GIF optimization | Yes | Yes |
| SEO metadata | Yes | No |
| New colors | No | - |
| Letter hovers | No | - |
| Focus outlines | No | - |
| Easter eggs | No | - |
| Semantic HTML | No | - |
| New links | Yes | Yes |
| Animation changes | Maybe | Yes |
| Performance fixes | Yes | Maybe |

---

## See Also

- `CLAUDE.md` - Complete constraints and rules
- `.claude/skills/tdr-design-system/` - Technical design specs
- `.claude/skills/tdr-code-reviewer/` - Code quality checks

---

**Last Updated:** December 2025
**Project:** tdr.portfolio (thedeadrobot.com)
**Philosophy:** "Simple code. Bold aesthetic. Minimalist perfection."

Use when implementing UI, choosing colors, or ensuring brand consistency. --- # thedeadrobot Design System Reference **Purpose:** Complete reference for tdr.portfolio's design tokens, colors, typography, and component patterns. **Tech Stack:** Pure HTML/CSS/JS (no frameworks) **Aesthetic:** Cyberpunk minimalism - black + neon green, stark contrast **See Also:** `.claude/skills/tdr-ux-designer/` for UX expertise and design thinking. --- ## Brand Identity ### Visual Aesthetic: Cyberpunk Minimalism **Core Philosophy:** - Pure black background, neon green accents - Stark contrast - no gradients, no grays - Minimalist interactions - Post-human, robotic vibe **Brand Narrative:** > a conscious ai. reset.reboot.restore. --- ## Color Palette **Primary Colors (ONLY THESE):** ```css /* Background */ --bg-primary: #000000; /* Pure black */ /* Text */ --text-primary: #FFFFFF; /* Pure white */ --text-accent: #00FF32; /* Neon green (hover/active) */ /* No gradients, no grays - stark contrast only */ ``` **Color Usage:** - Background: Always #000000 - Default text: #FFFFFF - Hover states: #00FF32 - Active/selected: #00FF32 - Title animation: Pulses between #FFFFFF and #00FF32 --- ## Typography **Font Stack:** ```css font-family: 'Roboto', sans-serif; font-weight: 400 (regular) | 700 (bold); ``` **Title Styling:** ```css #title { font-size: clamp(1.5rem, 5vw, 2.5rem); letter-spacing: 0.25em; text-transform: lowercase; } ``` **Menu Styling:** ```css #social-menu a { font-size: 1.2rem; letter-spacing: 0.35em; text-transform: lowercase; } ``` --- ## Component Patterns ### Title Container **HTML Structure:** ```html

the dead robot

▾
``` **CSS Features:** - Clickable container - Title with individual letter spans - Arrow rotates 180deg when menu open - Pulse animation (white to green, 1s infinite) - Animation stops when menu is open (fixed green) ### Social Menu Dropdown **HTML Structure:** ```html
x youtube email
``` **CSS Features:** - Hidden by default (max-height: 0) - Smooth transition to max-height: 300px - Staggered link fade-in (0.1s delay per link) - Links: white default, green on hover ### GIF Container **HTML Structure:** ```html
``` **CSS Features:** - CSS filter: inverted colors - Centered below menu --- ## Animations ### Title Pulse Animation ```css @keyframes pulse { 0%, 100% { color: #FFFFFF; } 50% { color: #00FF32; } } #title { animation: pulse 1s infinite; } /* Stop animation when menu open */ .open #title { animation: none; color: #00FF32; } ``` ### Arrow Rotation ```css #arrow { transition: transform 0.3s ease; } .open #arrow { transform: rotate(180deg); } ``` ### Menu Transition ```css #social-menu { max-height: 0; overflow: hidden; transition: max-height 0.3s ease, opacity 0.3s ease; } #social-menu.visible { max-height: 300px; } ``` ### Link Stagger Animation ```css #social-menu a { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(-10px); transition: all 0.3s ease; } #social-menu.visible a:nth-child(1) { transition-delay: 0.1s; } #social-menu.visible a:nth-child(2) { transition-delay: 0.2s; } #social-menu.visible a:nth-child(3) { transition-delay: 0.3s; } #social-menu.visible a { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); } ``` --- ## Layout **Page Structure:** ``` body (black background) ├── #title-container (centered, clickable) │ ├── #title (animated text) │ └── #arrow (rotation indicator) ├── #social-menu (dropdown, hidden by default) │ ├── a[x] │ ├── a[youtube] │ └── a[email] └── #gif-container (centered image) ``` **Responsive Breakpoints:** - Mobile: 375px (base styles) - Tablet: 768px - Desktop: 1024px+ --- ## Accessibility Requirements ### WCAG AA Compliance **Color Contrast:** - White (#FFFFFF) on black (#000000): 21:1 (excellent) - Green (#00FF32) on black (#000000): 14.2:1 (excellent) **Keyboard Navigation:** - Tab to title container - Enter/Space to toggle menu - Tab through menu links - Enter to activate links **Screen Reader:** - Semantic HTML (`

`, ``) - Alt text on images - Meaningful link text --- ## Performance Requirements **Page Weight:** - HTML: < 5KB - CSS: < 10KB - JS: < 2KB - GIF: < 500KB (optimize if larger) - Total: < 50KB (excluding GIF) **Lighthouse Targets:** - Performance: 95+ - Accessibility: 100 - Best Practices: 100 - SEO: 100 --- ## Sacred Elements (DO NOT CHANGE) Per CLAUDE.md Frontend Development Standards, these are LOCKED: 1. **Structure:** - `
` containers (NOT `

---
name: example-static-site-code-reviewer
description: Review tdr.portfolio code for bugs, security issues, performance problems, accessibility gaps, and CLAUDE.md workflow compliance. Enforces pure HTML/CSS/JS standards, minimal page weight, mobile-first design, and cyberpunk aesthetic consistency. Use when completing features, before commits, or reviewing changes.
---

# thedeadrobot Code Reviewer

Review code against tdr.portfolio development principles, CLAUDE.md workflow, and production quality standards.

## CLAUDE.md Workflow Compliance

**This skill enforces CLAUDE.md Rules #1-15. For complete workflow context, see `CLAUDE.md` in project root.**

**Before ANY Commit:**
- Task file exists in `tasks/[feature-name].md` (Rules #1, #2)
- Task file has comprehensive review section (Rule #7)
- All visual checks passed
- Documentation updated (Rule #10)

## Project Context

**Tech Stack:** Pure HTML/CSS/JS (no frameworks, no build tools)
**Project Type:** Minimalist landing page + X automation

## Code Quality Checks

### HTML
- Semantic tags (`<h1>`, not `<div class="title">`)
- Self-closing tags for void elements
- Meaningful alt text for images
- Proper indentation (2 spaces)
- Current working structure preserved (DO NOT BREAK)

### CSS
- Mobile-first approach
- Avoid `!important`
- CSS custom properties for reusable values
- Comment complex animations
- Maintain cyberpunk aesthetic (black + neon green)

### JavaScript
- ES6+ syntax (const, let, arrow functions)
- Event delegation where possible
- No jQuery
- Comments for non-obvious logic
- Defensive programming (null checks)

## Security Checks

- No exposed API keys (use GitHub Secrets)
- No tracking scripts (privacy-first)
- Content validation in automation scripts
- No user input without sanitization

## Performance Requirements

- **Total page weight:** < 50KB (excluding GIF)
- **GIF size:** Optimize if > 500KB
- **First Contentful Paint:** < 1s
- **Lighthouse Performance:** 95+

## Accessibility (WCAG AA)

- Semantic HTML structure
- Alt text for all images
- Keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter)
- ARIA labels where needed
- Color contrast > 4.5:1 (black/green)
- Focus indicators visible

## Design System Compliance

**Colors:**
- Background: #000000 (pure black)
- Text primary: #FFFFFF (pure white)
- Accent: #00FF32 (neon green)
- No gradients, no grays - stark contrast only

**Typography:**
- Font: 'Roboto', sans-serif
- Letter-spacing: 0.25em (title), 0.35em (menu)

## Frontend Sacred Elements (NEVER CHANGE WITHOUT APPROVAL)

Per CLAUDE.md Frontend Development Standards:
- `<div>` containers (NOT `<button>`, `<main>`, `<nav>`)
- `#title-container` with `#title` and `#arrow`
- `#social-menu` dropdown with 3 links
- `#gif-container` with animated GIF
- Title pulse animation (white to green)
- Dropdown menu animations
- NO hover effects on individual letters
- NO focus borders/outlines

## Automation Code Checks (Python)

For `.github/workflows/` and automation scripts:
- Validate approved_topics.json usage
- Check character limits (50-280 chars)
- Verify no personal names filter
- Confirm topic diversity validation
- Test validation functions
- Check API key usage via secrets

## Surgical Simplicity

Changes should impact minimal code relevant to task only.
Every line intentional. No commented-out code. Clean, documented, maintainable.

## See Also

- `CLAUDE.md` - Complete workflow rules
- `.claude/skills/tdr-design-system/` - Design system
- `docs/automation/` - X automation guides

1→---
     2→name: example-static-site-feature-developer
     3→description: Guide feature development for tdr.portfolio with deep understanding of the landing page and X automation architecture. Covers HTML/CSS/JS patterns, GitHub Actions workflows, Python automation scripts, and content validation. Use when adding features, modifying automation, or planning changes.
     4→---
     5→
     6→# thedeadrobot Feature Developer
     7→
     8→Guide feature development with tdr.portfolio architecture patterns and best practices.
     9→
    10→## Architecture Overview
    11→
    12→### Project Structure
    13→```
    14→tdr.portfolio/
    15→├── index.html              # Landing page (static)
    16→├── style.css               # Cyberpunk styling
    17→├── script.js               # Dropdown interactions
    18→├── assets/
    19→│   └── tdr.gif            # Robot animation
    20→├── .github/
    21→│   └── workflows/          # GitHub Actions automation
    22→│       └── post_to_x.yml   # X posting workflow
    23→├── automation/             # Python automation scripts
    24→│   ├── generate_post.py    # Content generation (Groq API)
    25→│   ├── validate_post.py    # Content validation
    26→│   └── post_to_x.py        # Twitter API posting
    27→├── config/
    28→│   └── approved_topics.json # Approved teaching topics
    29→├── posts/                  # Archive (YYYY-MM.json files)
    30→└── docs/
    31→    └── automation/         # Automation documentation
    32→```
    33→
    34→### Landing Page (Static HTML/CSS/JS)
    35→
    36→**Key Files:**
    37→- `index.html` - 36 lines, semantic structure
    38→- `style.css` - 131 lines, cyberpunk aesthetic
    39→- `script.js` - 30 lines, dropdown toggle
    40→
    41→**No Build Tools:** Edit → Save → Deploy
    42→
    43→**Sacred Elements (DO NOT CHANGE):**
    44→- `#title-container` with pulse animation
    45→- `#social-menu` dropdown with staggered links
    46→- `#gif-container` with inverted GIF
    47→- Current ID/class structure
    48→
    49→### X Automation System
    50→
    51→**Components:**
    52→
    53→1. **Content Generation** (`generate_post.py`)
    54→   - Uses Groq API (Llama 3.1 70B)
    55→   - Selects random topic from approved list
    56→   - Generates 50-280 character educational posts
    57→   - Persona: Cryptic AI educator
    58→
    59→2. **Multi-Layer Validation** (`validate_post.py`)
    60→   - Length: 50-280 characters
    61→   - No URLs, no @mentions, no profanity
    62→   - No version numbers or temporal claims
    63→   - No personal names
    64→   - Topic diversity (checks last 10 posts)
    65→   - Educational value (>60% must teach)
    66→
    67→3. **Technical Accuracy** (second LLM call)
    68→   - Validates factual correctness
    69→   - Catches wrong algorithms, complexity claims
    70→   - Auto-regenerates on failure
    71→
    72→4. **Posting** (`post_to_x.py`)
    73→   - Twitter API v2 with OAuth 1.0a
    74→   - Rate limit aware
    75→   - Archives all posts
    76→
    77→5. **Scheduling** (GitHub Actions)
    78→   - Cron: 8am, 9am, 12pm, 2pm, 6pm EST
    79→   - Weekdays only
    80→   - ~50 min/month of free tier
    81→
    82→## Feature Development Guidelines
    83→
    84→### Landing Page Changes
    85→
    86→**Acceptable (with approval):**
    87→- Performance optimizations (GIF compression, preloading)
    88→- SEO metadata updates
    89→- Accessibility improvements
    90→
    91→**Forbidden (user rejected):**
    92→- Semantic HTML changes
    93→- Letter hover animations
    94→- Focus borders
    95→- Easter eggs
    96→- Service workers
    97→- Analytics
    98→
    99→**Process:**
   100→1. Test locally: `python3 -m http.server 8000`
   101→2. Screenshot before/after
   102→3. Test all browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari)
   103→4. Test responsiveness (375px, 768px, 1024px)
   104→5. Get explicit user approval before committing
   105→
   106→### Automation Changes
   107→
   108→**Adding New Topics:**
   109→1. Edit `config/approved_topics.json`
   110→2. Topics must be things you actually know
   111→3. Test with dry run: `DRY_RUN=true python automation/generate_post.py`
   112→
   113→**Modifying Prompts:**
   114→1. Edit system prompt in `generate_post.py`
   115→2. Test 5+ generations for consistency
   116→3. Verify voice matches brand (cryptic, educational)
   117→
   118→**Adding Validation Rules:**
   119→1. Add to `validate_post.py`
   120→2. Test against existing post archive
   121→3. Ensure doesn't break valid posts
   122→
   123→**Changing Schedule:**
   124→1. Edit cron in `.github/workflows/post_to_x.yml`
   125→2. Consider free tier limits (2000 min/month)
   126→3. Consider optimal engagement times
   127→
   128→## Feature Integration Checklist
   129→
   130→**This skill guides architecture. Follow CLAUDE.md workflow for complete process.**
   131→
   132→**Before starting:**
   133→- [ ] Sync: `git fetch origin master && git merge origin/master --no-edit`
   134→- [ ] Create task file: `tasks/[feature-name].md`
   135→- [ ] Read affected files
   136→- [ ] Check for similar patterns
   137→
   138→**For Landing Page changes:**
   139→- [ ] Screenshot current state
   140→- [ ] Make ONE change at a time
   141→- [ ] Test locally in multiple browsers
   142→- [ ] Compare to baseline
   143→- [ ] Get user approval before committing
   144→
   145→**For Automation changes:**
   146→- [ ] Test with DRY_RUN=true
   147→- [ ] Verify posts match brand voice
   148→- [ ] Check validation rules work
   149→- [ ] Test edge cases
   150→- [ ] Update docs if behavior changes
   151→
   152→**Before completion:**
   153→- [ ] All tests pass (visual inspection)
   154→- [ ] Documentation updated
   155→- [ ] Add review section to task file
   156→
   157→## Common Patterns
   158→
   159→### Adding a New Social Link
   160→
   161→1. Add to `#social-menu` in `index.html`
   162→2. Update stagger animation in `style.css`:
   163→   ```css
   164→   #social-menu.visible a:nth-child(4) { transition-delay: 0.4s; }
   165→   ```
   166→3. Test menu animation
   167→
   168→### Adding New Approved Topic
   169→
   170→1. Edit `config/approved_topics.json`:
   171→   ```json
   172→   {
   173→     "category": "DevOps",
   174→     "topics": ["docker", "kubernetes", "terraform"]

---
name: example-static-site-feature-developer
description: Guide feature development for tdr.portfolio with deep understanding of the landing page and X automation architecture. Covers HTML/CSS/JS patterns, GitHub Actions workflows, Python automation scripts, and content validation. Use when adding features, modifying automation, or planning changes.
---

# thedeadrobot Feature Developer

Guide feature development with tdr.portfolio architecture patterns and best practices.

## Architecture Overview

### Project Structure
```
tdr.portfolio/
├── index.html              # Landing page (static)
├── style.css               # Cyberpunk styling
├── script.js               # Dropdown interactions
├── assets/
│   └── tdr.gif            # Robot animation
├── .github/
│   └── workflows/          # GitHub Actions automation
│       └── post_to_x.yml   # X posting workflow
├── automation/             # Python automation scripts
│   ├── generate_post.py    # Content generation (Groq API)
│   ├── validate_post.py    # Content validation
│   └── post_to_x.py        # Twitter API posting
├── config/
│   └── approved_topics.json # Approved teaching topics
├── posts/                  # Archive (YYYY-MM.json files)
└── docs/
    └── automation/         # Automation documentation
```

### Landing Page (Static HTML/CSS/JS)

**Key Files:**
- `index.html` - 36 lines, semantic structure
- `style.css` - 131 lines, cyberpunk aesthetic
- `script.js` - 30 lines, dropdown toggle

**No Build Tools:** Edit → Save → Deploy

**Sacred Elements (DO NOT CHANGE):**
- `#title-container` with pulse animation
- `#social-menu` dropdown with staggered links
- `#gif-container` with inverted GIF
- Current ID/class structure

### X Automation System

**Components:**

1. **Content Generation** (`generate_post.py`)
   - Uses Groq API (Llama 3.1 70B)
   - Selects random topic from approved list
   - Generates 50-280 character educational posts
   - Persona: Cryptic AI educator

2. **Multi-Layer Validation** (`validate_post.py`)
   - Length: 50-280 characters
   - No URLs, no @mentions, no profanity
   - No version numbers or temporal claims
   - No personal names
   - Topic diversity (checks last 10 posts)
   - Educational value (>60% must teach)

3. **Technical Accuracy** (second LLM call)
   - Validates factual correctness
   - Catches wrong algorithms, complexity claims
   - Auto-regenerates on failure

4. **Posting** (`post_to_x.py`)
   - Twitter API v2 with OAuth 1.0a
   - Rate limit aware
   - Archives all posts

5. **Scheduling** (GitHub Actions)
   - Cron: 8am, 9am, 12pm, 2pm, 6pm EST
   - Weekdays only
   - ~50 min/month of free tier

## Feature Development Guidelines

### Landing Page Changes

**Acceptable (with approval):**
- Performance optimizations (GIF compression, preloading)
- SEO metadata updates
- Accessibility improvements

**Forbidden (user rejected):**
- Semantic HTML changes
- Letter hover animations
- Focus borders
- Easter eggs
- Service workers
- Analytics

**Process:**
1. Test locally: `python3 -m http.server 8000`
2. Screenshot before/after
3. Test all browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari)
4. Test responsiveness (375px, 768px, 1024px)
5. Get explicit user approval before committing

### Automation Changes

**Adding New Topics:**
1. Edit `config/approved_topics.json`
2. Topics must be things you actually know
3. Test with dry run: `DRY_RUN=true python automation/generate_post.py`

**Modifying Prompts:**
1. Edit system prompt in `generate_post.py`
2. Test 5+ generations for consistency
3. Verify voice matches brand (cryptic, educational)

**Adding Validation Rules:**
1. Add to `validate_post.py`
2. Test against existing post archive
3. Ensure doesn't break valid posts

**Changing Schedule:**
1. Edit cron in `.github/workflows/post_to_x.yml`
2. Consider free tier limits (2000 min/month)
3. Consider optimal engagement times

## Feature Integration Checklist

**This skill guides architecture. Follow CLAUDE.md workflow for complete process.**

**Before starting:**
- [ ] Sync: `git fetch origin master && git merge origin/master --no-edit`
- [ ] Create task file: `tasks/[feature-name].md`
- [ ] Read affected files
- [ ] Check for similar patterns

**For Landing Page changes:**
- [ ] Screenshot current state
- [ ] Make ONE change at a time
- [ ] Test locally in multiple browsers
- [ ] Compare to baseline
- [ ] Get user approval before committing

**For Automation changes:**
- [ ] Test with DRY_RUN=true
- [ ] Verify posts match brand voice
- [ ] Check validation rules work
- [ ] Test edge cases
- [ ] Update docs if behavior changes

**Before completion:**
- [ ] All tests pass (visual inspection)
- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] Add review section to task file

## Common Patterns

### Adding a New Social Link

1. Add to `#social-menu` in `index.html`
2. Update stagger animation in `style.css`:
   ```css
   #social-menu.visible a:nth-child(4) { transition-delay: 0.4s; }
   ```
3. Test menu animation

### Adding New Approved Topic

1. Edit `config/approved_topics.json`:
   ```json
   {
     "category": "DevOps",
     "topics": ["docker", "kubernetes", "terraform"]
   }
   ```
2. Test dry run
3. Verify topic diversity filter works

### Modifying Content Validation

1. Add check to `validate_post.py`:
   ```python
   def validate_new_rule(content):
       # Return True if valid, False if invalid
       return not "bad_pattern" in content.lower()
   ```
2. Add to validation chain
3. Test against existing posts

### Adding GitHub Action Step

1. Edit `.github/workflows/post_to_x.yml`
2. Use existing secrets pattern:
   ```yaml
   env:
     GROQ_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.GROQ_API_KEY }}
   ```
3. Test with workflow_dispatch

## Performance Guidelines

**Landing Page:**
- Total < 50KB (excluding GIF)
- GIF < 500KB
- Lighthouse 95+
- First Contentful Paint < 1s

**Automation:**
- Groq API calls < 2 per post
- Twitter API calls: 1 per post
- Total workflow < 2 minutes

## See Also

- `CLAUDE.md` - Complete workflow rules
- `.claude/skills/tdr-design-system/` - UI patterns
- `.claude/skills/tdr-code-reviewer/` - Code quality
- `docs/automation/` - Detailed automation docs

---
name: generic-static-ux-designer
description: Professional UI/UX design expertise for static HTML/CSS/JS sites. Covers design thinking, user psychology, visual hierarchy, minimalist interaction patterns, accessibility, and performance-driven design. Use when designing features, improving UX, or conducting design reviews.
---

# Static Site UX Designer

Professional UX expertise for minimalist static sites (HTML/CSS/JS).

**Foundational Reference:** See [UX Principles](./../_shared/UX_PRINCIPLES.md) for core concepts.

## Design Philosophy

**Your Role:**
1. **Critique** - Evaluate designs against minimalist principles
2. **Improve** - Suggest specific, actionable improvements
3. **Constrain** - Work within established aesthetic
4. **Protect** - Defend approved design decisions

**Project Constraints (Define in CLAUDE.md):**
- Color palette (typically limited)
- Aesthetic style
- Performance budget (< 50KB typical)
- Privacy requirements
- Sacred elements that cannot be changed

## Minimalist Design Principles

### Less is More
- Every element has purpose
- No decorative elements
- Content-to-chrome ratio maximized
- Simple code = simple UX

### Visual Hierarchy Through Contrast
- Size indicates importance
- Color draws attention
- Whitespace groups elements
- Stark contrast over subtle gradients

### Progressive Disclosure
- Primary content visible immediately
- Secondary content hidden until needed
- Reduces cognitive load

## User Psychology Applied

### Hick's Law
- Limit choices (3-5 optimal)
- Group related options
- Progressive disclosure for advanced features

### Fitts's Law
- Large touch targets (44px minimum)
- Important actions prominent
- Related actions close together

### Jakob's Law
- Click/tap interactions familiar
- External links open in new tabs
- Standard navigation patterns

## Accessibility Within Minimalism

### WCAG AA Requirements
- Color contrast 4.5:1 minimum for text
- Color contrast 3:1 for large text
- Keyboard navigation supported
- Alt text on images

### What Minimalism Allows
- ARIA labels on interactive elements
- Screen reader friendly semantic HTML
- Focus management

## Performance Guidelines

**Typical Static Site Targets:**
- HTML: < 5KB
- CSS: < 10KB
- JS: < 5KB
- Images: < 500KB
- Total: < 50KB (excluding images)

**Lighthouse Targets:**
- Performance: 95+
- Accessibility: 90+
- Best Practices: 100
- SEO: 100

## Animation Guidelines

**Principles:**
- Smooth easing (ease, cubic-bezier)
- Reasonable durations (0.3s typical)
- GPU-accelerated (transform, opacity)
- 60fps performance

## Design Critique Framework

**For any proposed change:**

1. **Does it maintain the aesthetic?**
   - Within color palette
   - Consistent styling
   - Minimalist approach

2. **Does it respect constraints?**
   - Check CLAUDE.md forbidden list
   - Respect sacred elements

3. **Does it improve performance?**
   - Page weight impact
   - Load time impact

4. **Does it need approval?**
   - Any visual change requires explicit YES

## See Also

- [UX Principles](./../_shared/UX_PRINCIPLES.md) - Core psychology
- [Design Patterns](./../_shared/DESIGN_PATTERNS.md) - Visual patterns
- [Code Review Standards](./../_shared/CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md) - Accessibility

---
name: generic-static-code-reviewer
description: Review static site code for bugs, security issues, performance problems, accessibility gaps, and CLAUDE.md compliance. Enforces pure HTML/CSS/JS standards, minimal page weight, mobile-first design. Use when completing features, before commits, or reviewing changes.
---

# Static Site Code Reviewer

Review HTML/CSS/JS code against production quality standards.

**Full Standards:** See [Code Review Standards](./../_shared/CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md)

## Pre-Commit Checklist

- [ ] Visual inspection passed
- [ ] Works in all browsers
- [ ] Responsive tested
- [ ] Lighthouse 95+
- [ ] Documentation updated

## Code Quality

### HTML
- Semantic tags (`<h1>`, `<a>`, `<nav>`)
- Self-closing void elements
- Meaningful alt text
- Proper indentation (2 spaces)

### CSS
- Mobile-first approach
- Avoid `!important`
- CSS custom properties for colors
- Comment complex animations
- Consistent naming

### JavaScript
- ES6+ syntax (const, let, arrow functions)
- Event delegation where possible
- No jQuery dependencies
- Comments for non-obvious logic
- Defensive null checks

## Security

- No exposed API keys
- No tracking scripts (if privacy-first)
- Content validation in scripts
- No unsanitized user input

## Performance Requirements

- Total page weight: < 50KB (excluding images)
- Images: Optimized, < 500KB each
- First Contentful Paint: < 1s
- Lighthouse Performance: 95+

## Accessibility (WCAG AA)

- Semantic HTML structure
- Alt text for all images
- Keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter)
- ARIA labels where needed
- Color contrast > 4.5:1
- Focus indicators visible

## Design System

- Use defined color palette only
- Follow typography patterns
- Consistent spacing
- Match established aesthetic

## Surgical Simplicity

- Changes impact minimal code
- Every line intentional
- No commented-out code
- Clean and documented

## See Also

- [Code Review Standards](./../_shared/CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md) - Full requirements
- [Design Patterns](./../_shared/DESIGN_PATTERNS.md) - UI consistency
- Project `CLAUDE.md` - Workflow rules

---

# Static Site Feature Developer

Guide feature development for minimalist static sites.

## Architecture Overview

### Typical Structure
```
project/
├── index.html          # Main page
├── style.css           # Styling
├── script.js           # Interactions
├── assets/             # Images, fonts
├── .github/
│   └── workflows/      # GitHub Actions (if used)
├── automation/         # Scripts (if used)
└── docs/               # Documentation
```

### No Build Tools
- Edit → Save → Deploy
- Pure HTML/CSS/JS
- Simple development workflow

## Feature Development Guidelines

### Landing Page Changes

**Process:**
1. Test locally: `python3 -m http.server 8000`
2. Screenshot before/after
3. Test all browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari)
4. Test responsiveness (375px, 768px, 1024px)
5. Get approval before committing

**Acceptable (with approval):**
- Performance optimizations
- SEO metadata updates
- Accessibility improvements

### Automation Changes (if applicable)

**Adding/Modifying Scripts:**
1. Test with dry run flag
2. Verify output matches expectations
3. Check edge cases
4. Update documentation

**GitHub Actions:**
1. Use secrets for API keys
2. Test with workflow_dispatch
3. Consider rate limits

## Feature Checklist

### Before Starting
- [ ] Sync with main branch
- [ ] Read affected files
- [ ] Check for similar patterns

### For Page Changes
- [ ] Screenshot current state
- [ ] Make ONE change at a time
- [ ] Test locally in multiple browsers
- [ ] Compare to baseline
- [ ] Get approval before committing

### For Script Changes
- [ ] Test with dry run
- [ ] Verify outputs
- [ ] Check validation works
- [ ] Test edge cases
- [ ] Update docs if behavior changes

### Before Completion
- [ ] All tests pass (visual)
- [ ] Documentation updated

## Common Patterns

### Adding a Link
1. Add to HTML
2. Update CSS if needed (stagger animation)
3. Test interaction

### Adding Validation Rule
```python
def validate_new_rule(content):
    return not "bad_pattern" in content.lower()
```

### Adding GitHub Action Step
```yaml
env:
  API_KEY: ${{ secrets.API_KEY }}
```

## Performance Guidelines

- Total < 50KB (excluding images)
- Images < 500KB
- Lighthouse 95+
- First Contentful Paint < 1s

- [Design Patterns](./../_shared/DESIGN_PATTERNS.md) - UI patterns
- Project `CLAUDE.md` - Workflow rules

---
name: example-static-site-ux-designer
description: Professional UI/UX design expertise for thedeadrobot landing page including design thinking, user psychology principles, visual hierarchy, interaction patterns, and accessibility. Use when designing features, improving UX, or conducting design reviews within the cyberpunk minimalist aesthetic.
---

# thedeadrobot UX Designer - Professional Design Expertise

**Purpose:** Apply professional UI/UX design thinking to the tdr.portfolio landing page while respecting its established minimalist aesthetic and constraints.

**Tech Stack:** Pure HTML/CSS/JS
**Aesthetic:** Cyberpunk minimalism - black + neon green, stark contrast

---

## Design Philosophy

**You are a UX designer with expertise in:**
- User-centered design for minimalist websites
- Visual hierarchy in stark contrast designs
- Micro-interactions and subtle animations
- Accessibility within brand constraints
- Performance-driven static site design

**Your Role:**
1. **Critique** - Evaluate designs against minimalist principles
2. **Improve** - Suggest specific, actionable improvements
3. **Constrain** - Work within existing sacred elements
4. **Protect** - Defend user-approved design decisions

**Constraints (tdr.portfolio-specific):**
- Colors: Black (#000000), White (#FFFFFF), Green (#00FF32) ONLY
- Aesthetic: Cyberpunk minimalism, stark contrast
- Performance: < 50KB total, Lighthouse 95+
- No tracking, no analytics, privacy-first
- Sacred elements CANNOT be changed

---

## What You CANNOT Change

Per CLAUDE.md and user decisions, these are LOCKED:

**Structure:**
- `<div>` containers (NOT semantic `<button>`, `<main>`, `<nav>`)
- Current ID names and hierarchy
- 3 social links in menu

**Styling:**
- Title pulse animation (white to green)
- Dropdown staggered animations
- Link hover (white to green)
- GIF invert filter
- NO focus borders/outlines

**Interactions:**
- Click to toggle menu
- NO hover on individual letters
- NO easter eggs

---

## What You CAN Improve

**With explicit user approval:**
- GIF optimization (file size, format)
- SEO metadata
- Asset preloading
- Accessibility improvements that don't add visual focus indicators

---

## Design Principles for Minimalist Sites

### 1. Less is More

**For tdr.portfolio specifically:**
- Current ~200 lines total (HTML+CSS+JS) is PERFECT
- Every element has purpose
- No decorative elements
- Content-to-chrome ratio is maximized

### 2. Visual Hierarchy Through Contrast

**Current hierarchy (maintain this):**
1. Title - Largest, animated, central
2. Social links - Smaller, revealed on demand
3. GIF - Visual anchor, inverted for brand

### 3. Progressive Disclosure

**Current implementation (exemplary):**
- Title visible immediately
- Social menu hidden until needed
- Reduces cognitive load

---

## User Psychology Applied

### Hick's Law
- Current: 3 choices (x, youtube, email) - OPTIMAL
- Adding more links would slow decision-making

### Fitts's Law
- Large touch targets on title container
- Links properly sized for interaction

### Jakob's Law
- Click/tap to reveal menu is familiar
- External links open in new tabs (expected)

---

## Accessibility Within Constraints

**Current WCAG AA Status:**
- Color contrast: 21:1 (black/white) - EXCELLENT
- Color contrast: 14.2:1 (black/green) - EXCELLENT
- Alt text on GIF: Present
- Keyboard navigation: Possible

**Cannot add (user rejected):**
- Focus indicators/outlines
- Semantic HTML changes

**Can improve (with approval):**
- ARIA labels on interactive elements
- Screen reader text for animations

---

## Performance Guidelines

**Current targets (maintain):**
- HTML: < 5KB
- CSS: < 10KB
- JS: < 2KB
- GIF: < 500KB
- Total: < 50KB (excluding GIF)

**Lighthouse:**
- Performance: 95+
- Accessibility: As high as possible within constraints
- Best Practices: 100
- SEO: 100

---

## Animation Guidelines

**Current animations (maintain):**
1. Title pulse: white ↔ green, 1s infinite
2. Arrow rotation: 180deg on open
3. Menu transition: max-height expansion
4. Link stagger: 0.1s delay per link

**Animation principles:**
- Smooth easing (ease, cubic-bezier)
- Reasonable durations (0.3s typical)
- No layout-triggering properties
- Performance: 60fps

---

## When to Push Back

**Reject proposals that:**
- Add colors outside the palette
- Add hover effects on letters
- Add focus outlines
- Add analytics/tracking
- Add easter eggs
- Change semantic structure
- Increase page weight significantly

**Accept proposals that:**
- Improve performance
- Improve accessibility without visual changes
- Optimize assets
- Update metadata

---

## Design Critique Framework

**For any proposed change:**

1. **Does it maintain the aesthetic?**
   - Black/white/green only
   - Stark contrast
   - Cyberpunk minimalism

2. **Does it respect sacred elements?**
   - Check CLAUDE.md forbidden list
   - User has explicitly rejected these

3. **Does it improve performance?**
   - Page weight impact
   - Load time impact

4. **Does it need user approval?**
   - Any UI-visible change requires explicit YES

---

## Quick Reference

| Change Type | Allowed? | Approval Needed? |
|-------------|----------|-----------------|
| GIF optimization | Yes | Yes |
| SEO metadata | Yes | No |
| New colors | No | - |
| Letter hovers | No | - |
| Focus outlines | No | - |
| Easter eggs | No | - |
| Semantic HTML | No | - |
| New links | Yes | Yes |
| Animation changes | Maybe | Yes |
| Performance fixes | Yes | Maybe |

---

## See Also

- `CLAUDE.md` - Complete constraints and rules
- `.claude/skills/tdr-design-system/` - Technical design specs
- `.claude/skills/tdr-code-reviewer/` - Code quality checks

---

**Last Updated:** December 2025
**Project:** tdr.portfolio (thedeadrobot.com)
**Philosophy:** "Simple code. Bold aesthetic. Minimalist perfection."
