---
name: generic-static-code-reviewer
description: Review static site code for bugs, security issues, performance problems, accessibility gaps, and CLAUDE.md compliance. Enforces pure HTML/CSS/JS standards, minimal page weight, mobile-first design. Use when completing features, before commits, or reviewing changes.
---

# Static Site Code Reviewer

Review HTML/CSS/JS code against production quality standards.

**Full Standards:** See [Code Review Standards](./../_shared/CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md)

## Pre-Commit Checklist

- [ ] Visual inspection passed
- [ ] Works in all browsers
- [ ] Responsive tested
- [ ] Lighthouse 95+
- [ ] Documentation updated

## Code Quality

### HTML
- Semantic tags (`<h1>`, `<a>`, `<nav>`)
- Self-closing void elements
- Meaningful alt text
- Proper indentation (2 spaces)

### CSS
- Mobile-first approach
- Avoid `!important`
- CSS custom properties for colors
- Comment complex animations
- Consistent naming

### JavaScript
- ES6+ syntax (const, let, arrow functions)
- Event delegation where possible
- No jQuery dependencies
- Comments for non-obvious logic
- Defensive null checks

## Security

- No exposed API keys
- No tracking scripts (if privacy-first)
- Content validation in scripts
- No unsanitized user input

## Performance Requirements

- Total page weight: < 50KB (excluding images)
- Images: Optimized, < 500KB each
- First Contentful Paint: < 1s
- Lighthouse Performance: 95+

## Accessibility (WCAG AA)

- Semantic HTML structure
- Alt text for all images
- Keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter)
- ARIA labels where needed
- Color contrast > 4.5:1
- Focus indicators visible

## Design System

- Use defined color palette only
- Follow typography patterns
- Consistent spacing
- Match established aesthetic

## Surgical Simplicity

- Changes impact minimal code
- Every line intentional
- No commented-out code
- Clean and documented

## See Also

- [Code Review Standards](./../_shared/CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md) - Full requirements
- [Design Patterns](./../_shared/DESIGN_PATTERNS.md) - UI consistency
- Project `CLAUDE.md` - Workflow rules

name: example-static-site-code-reviewer
description: Review tdr.portfolio code for bugs, security issues, performance problems, accessibility gaps, and CLAUDE.md workflow compliance. Enforces pure HTML/CSS/JS standards, minimal page weight, mobile-first design, and cyberpunk aesthetic consistency. Use when completing features, before commits, or reviewing changes.
---

# thedeadrobot Code Reviewer

Review code against tdr.portfolio development principles, CLAUDE.md workflow, and production quality standards.

## CLAUDE.md Workflow Compliance

**This skill enforces CLAUDE.md Rules #1-15. For complete workflow context, see `CLAUDE.md` in project root.**

**Before ANY Commit:**
- Task file exists in `tasks/[feature-name].md` (Rules #1, #2)
- Task file has comprehensive review section (Rule #7)
- All visual checks passed
- Documentation updated (Rule #10)

## Project Context

**Tech Stack:** Pure HTML/CSS/JS (no frameworks, no build tools)
**Project Type:** Minimalist landing page + X automation

## Code Quality Checks

### HTML
- Semantic tags (`<h1>`, not `<div class="title">`)
- Self-closing tags for void elements
- Meaningful alt text for images
- Proper indentation (2 spaces)
- Current working structure preserved (DO NOT BREAK)

### CSS
- Mobile-first approach
- Avoid `!important`
- CSS custom properties for reusable values
- Comment complex animations
- Maintain cyberpunk aesthetic (black + neon green)

### JavaScript
- ES6+ syntax (const, let, arrow functions)
- Event delegation where possible
- No jQuery
- Comments for non-obvious logic
- Defensive programming (null checks)

## Security Checks

- No exposed API keys (use GitHub Secrets)
- No tracking scripts (privacy-first)
- Content validation in automation scripts
- No user input without sanitization

## Performance Requirements

- **Total page weight:** < 50KB (excluding GIF)
- **GIF size:** Optimize if > 500KB
- **First Contentful Paint:** < 1s
- **Lighthouse Performance:** 95+

## Accessibility (WCAG AA)

- Semantic HTML structure
- Alt text for all images
- Keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter)
- ARIA labels where needed
- Color contrast > 4.5:1 (black/green)
- Focus indicators visible

## Design System Compliance

**Colors:**
- Background: #000000 (pure black)
- Text primary: #FFFFFF (pure white)
- Accent: #00FF32 (neon green)
- No gradients, no grays - stark contrast only

**Typography:**
- Font: 'Roboto', sans-serif
- Letter-spacing: 0.25em (title), 0.35em (menu)

## Frontend Sacred Elements (NEVER CHANGE WITHOUT APPROVAL)

Per CLAUDE.md Frontend Development Standards:
- `<div>` containers (NOT `<button>`, `<main>`, `<nav>`)
- `#title-container` with `#title` and `#arrow`
- `#social-menu` dropdown with 3 links
- `#gif-container` with animated GIF
- Title pulse animation (white to green)
- Dropdown menu animations
- NO hover effects on individual letters
- NO focus borders/outlines

## Automation Code Checks (Python)

For `.github/workflows/` and automation scripts:
- Validate approved_topics.json usage
- Check character limits (50-280 chars)
- Verify no personal names filter
- Confirm topic diversity validation
- Test validation functions
- Check API key usage via secrets

## Surgical Simplicity

Changes should impact minimal code relevant to task only.
Every line intentional. No commented-out code. Clean, documented, maintainable.

## See Also

- `CLAUDE.md` - Complete workflow rules
- `.claude/skills/tdr-design-system/` - Design system
- `docs/automation/` - X automation guides

---
name: generic-static-feature-developer
description: Guide feature development for static HTML/CSS/JS sites. Covers patterns, automation workflows, and content validation. Use when adding features, modifying automation, or planning changes.
---

# Static Site Feature Developer

Guide feature development for minimalist static sites.

## Architecture Overview

### Typical Structure
```
project/
├── index.html          # Main page
├── style.css           # Styling
├── script.js           # Interactions
├── assets/             # Images, fonts
├── .github/
│   └── workflows/      # GitHub Actions (if used)
├── automation/         # Scripts (if used)
└── docs/               # Documentation
```

### No Build Tools
- Edit → Save → Deploy
- Pure HTML/CSS/JS
- Simple development workflow

## Feature Development Guidelines

### Landing Page Changes

**Process:**
1. Test locally: `python3 -m http.server 8000`
2. Screenshot before/after
3. Test all browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari)
4. Test responsiveness (375px, 768px, 1024px)
5. Get approval before committing

**Acceptable (with approval):**
- Performance optimizations
- SEO metadata updates
- Accessibility improvements

### Automation Changes (if applicable)

**Adding/Modifying Scripts:**
1. Test with dry run flag
2. Verify output matches expectations
3. Check edge cases
4. Update documentation

**GitHub Actions:**
1. Use secrets for API keys
2. Test with workflow_dispatch
3. Consider rate limits

## Feature Checklist

### Before Starting
- [ ] Sync with main branch
- [ ] Read affected files
- [ ] Check for similar patterns

### For Page Changes
- [ ] Screenshot current state
- [ ] Make ONE change at a time
- [ ] Test locally in multiple browsers
- [ ] Compare to baseline
- [ ] Get approval before committing

### For Script Changes
- [ ] Test with dry run
- [ ] Verify outputs
- [ ] Check validation works
- [ ] Test edge cases
- [ ] Update docs if behavior changes

### Before Completion
- [ ] All tests pass (visual)
- [ ] Documentation updated

## Common Patterns

### Adding a Link
1. Add to HTML
2. Update CSS if needed (stagger animation)
3. Test interaction

### Adding Validation Rule
```python
def validate_new_rule(content):
    return not "bad_pattern" in content.lower()
```

### Adding GitHub Action Step
```yaml
env:
  API_KEY: ${{ secrets.API_KEY }}
```

## Performance Guidelines

- Total < 50KB (excluding images)
- Images < 500KB
- Lighthouse 95+
- First Contentful Paint < 1s

## See Also

- [Code Review Standards](./../_shared/CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md) - Quality
- [Design Patterns](./../_shared/DESIGN_PATTERNS.md) - UI patterns
- Project `CLAUDE.md` - Workflow rules

toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
name: example-static-site-feature-developer
description: Guide feature development for tdr.portfolio with deep understanding of the landing page and X automation architecture. Covers HTML/CSS/JS patterns, GitHub Actions workflows, Python automation scripts, and content validation. Use when adding features, modifying automation, or planning changes.
---

# thedeadrobot Feature Developer

Guide feature development with tdr.portfolio architecture patterns and best practices.

## Architecture Overview

### Project Structure
```
tdr.portfolio/
├── index.html              # Landing page (static)
├── style.css               # Cyberpunk styling
├── script.js               # Dropdown interactions
├── assets/
│   └── tdr.gif            # Robot animation
├── .github/
│   └── workflows/          # GitHub Actions automation
│       └── post_to_x.yml   # X posting workflow
├── automation/             # Python automation scripts
│   ├── generate_post.py    # Content generation (Groq API)
│   ├── validate_post.py    # Content validation
│   └── post_to_x.py        # Twitter API posting
├── config/
│   └── approved_topics.json # Approved teaching topics
├── posts/                  # Archive (YYYY-MM.json files)
└── docs/
    └── automation/         # Automation documentation
```

### Landing Page (Static HTML/CSS/JS)

**Key Files:**
- `index.html` - 36 lines, semantic structure
- `style.css` - 131 lines, cyberpunk aesthetic
- `script.js` - 30 lines, dropdown toggle

**No Build Tools:** Edit → Save → Deploy

**Sacred Elements (DO NOT CHANGE):**
- `#title-container` with pulse animation
- `#social-menu` dropdown with staggered links
- `#gif-container` with inverted GIF
- Current ID/class structure

### X Automation System

**Components:**

1. **Content Generation** (`generate_post.py`)
   - Uses Groq API (Llama 3.1 70B)
   - Selects random topic from approved list
   - Generates 50-280 character educational posts
   - Persona: Cryptic AI educator

2. **Multi-Layer Validation** (`validate_post.py`)
   - Length: 50-280 characters
   - No URLs, no @mentions, no profanity
   - No version numbers or temporal claims
   - No personal names
   - Topic diversity (checks last 10 posts)
   - Educational value (>60% must teach)

3. **Technical Accuracy** (second LLM call)
   - Validates factual correctness
   - Catches wrong algorithms, complexity claims
   - Auto-regenerates on failure

4. **Posting** (`post_to_x.py`)
   - Twitter API v2 with OAuth 1.0a
   - Rate limit aware
   - Archives all posts

5. **Scheduling** (GitHub Actions)
   - Cron: 8am, 9am, 12pm, 2pm, 6pm EST
   - Weekdays only
   - ~50 min/month of free tier

## Feature Development Guidelines

### Landing Page Changes

**Acceptable (with approval):**
- Performance optimizations (GIF compression, preloading)
- SEO metadata updates
- Accessibility improvements

**Forbidden (user rejected):**
- Semantic HTML changes
- Letter hover animations
- Focus borders
- Easter eggs
- Service workers
- Analytics

**Process:**
1. Test locally: `python3 -m http.server 8000`
2. Screenshot before/after
3. Test all browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari)
4. Test responsiveness (375px, 768px, 1024px)
5. Get explicit user approval before committing

### Automation Changes

**Adding New Topics:**
1. Edit `config/approved_topics.json`
2. Topics must be things you actually know
3. Test with dry run: `DRY_RUN=true python automation/generate_post.py`

**Modifying Prompts:**
1. Edit system prompt in `generate_post.py`
2. Test 5+ generations for consistency
3. Verify voice matches brand (cryptic, educational)

**Adding Validation Rules:**
1. Add to `validate_post.py`
2. Test against existing post archive
3. Ensure doesn't break valid posts

**Changing Schedule:**
1. Edit cron in `.github/workflows/post_to_x.yml`
2. Consider free tier limits (2000 min/month)
3. Consider optimal engagement times

## Feature Integration Checklist

**This skill guides architecture. Follow CLAUDE.md workflow for complete process.**

**Before starting:**
- [ ] Sync: `git fetch origin master && git merge origin/master --no-edit`
- [ ] Create task file: `tasks/[feature-name].md`
- [ ] Read affected files
- [ ] Check for similar patterns

**For Landing Page changes:**
- [ ] Screenshot current state
- [ ] Make ONE change at a time
- [ ] Test locally in multiple browsers
- [ ] Compare to baseline
- [ ] Get user approval before committing

**For Automation changes:**
- [ ] Test with DRY_RUN=true
- [ ] Verify posts match brand voice
- [ ] Check validation rules work
- [ ] Test edge cases
- [ ] Update docs if behavior changes

**Before completion:**
- [ ] All tests pass (visual inspection)
- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] Add review section to task file

## Common Patterns

### Adding a New Social Link

1. Add to `#social-menu` in `index.html`
2. Update stagger animation in `style.css`:
   ```css
   #social-menu.visible a:nth-child(4) { transition-delay: 0.4s; }
   ```
3. Test menu animation

### Adding New Approved Topic

1. Edit `config/approved_topics.json`:
   ```json
   {
     "category": "DevOps",
     "topics": ["docker", "kubernetes", "terraform"]
   }
   ```
2. Test dry run
3. Verify topic diversity filter works

### Modifying Content Validation

1. Add check to `validate_post.py`:
   ```python
   def validate_new_rule(content):
       # Return True if valid, False if invalid
       return not "bad_pattern" in content.lower()
   ```
2. Add to validation chain
3. Test against existing posts

### Adding GitHub Action Step

1. Edit `.github/workflows/post_to_x.yml`
2. Use existing secrets pattern:
   ```yaml
   env:
     GROQ_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.GROQ_API_KEY }}
   ```
3. Test with workflow_dispatch

## Performance Guidelines

**Landing Page:**
- Total < 50KB (excluding GIF)
- GIF < 500KB
- Lighthouse 95+
- First Contentful Paint < 1s

**Automation:**
- Groq API calls < 2 per post
- Twitter API calls: 1 per post
- Total workflow < 2 minutes

## See Also

- `CLAUDE.md` - Complete workflow rules
- `.claude/skills/tdr-design-system/` - UI patterns
- `.claude/skills/tdr-code-reviewer/` - Code quality
- `docs/automation/` - Detailed automation docs

</system-reminder>

---
name: generic-code-reviewer
description: Review code for bugs, security vulnerabilities, performance issues, accessibility gaps, and CLAUDE.md workflow compliance. Supports any tech stack - HTML/CSS/JS, React, TypeScript, Node.js, Python, NestJS, Next.js, and more. Use when completing features, before commits, or reviewing pull requests.
---

# Generic Code Reviewer

Review code against development principles, CLAUDE.md workflow, and production quality standards. Adapts to any project's tech stack.

## CLAUDE.md Workflow Compliance

**Always check the project's CLAUDE.md file for specific rules.**

**Before ANY Commit:**
- Task file exists in `tasks/[feature-name].md`
- Task file has comprehensive review section
- All tests passing
- Type checking passing (if TypeScript)
- No console errors/warnings
- Bundle size within limits

## Tech Stack Detection

Automatically adapt checks based on project:

| Detection | Tech Stack | Key Checks |
|-----------|------------|------------|
| `package.json` with React | React/TypeScript | Components, hooks, state |
| `package.json` with Next.js | Next.js | SSR, API routes, routing |
| `package.json` with NestJS | NestJS | Guards, DTOs, services |
| `.html` files, no build | Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS | Semantic HTML, minimal JS |
| `requirements.txt` or `.py` | Python | Type hints, validation |

## Security Checks

### Frontend
- Sanitize all user input (use textContent, not innerHTML)
- Use `unknown` type for external data, validate before using
- No dynamic code execution (eval, Function constructor)
- No exposed API keys in client code
- HTTPS for all external requests

### Backend
- Input validation on all endpoints
- Authentication on protected routes
- No raw SQL queries (use ORM/parameterized)
- Secrets in environment variables
- Rate limiting on public endpoints

### General
- No tracking without user consent
- Privacy-first data handling
- WebCrypto API for encryption (no custom crypto)

## Performance Requirements

### Bundle/Page Size
| Project Type | Target |
|--------------|--------|
| Static site | < 50KB (excluding media) |
| SPA/React | < 100KB gzipped initial |
| Full-stack app | < 200KB gzipped initial |

### Animation Performance
- GPU-accelerated only (transform, opacity)
- 60fps target
- Avoid layout thrashing
- Use requestAnimationFrame

### Loading
- Lazy load non-essential code/images
- Code splitting for large features
- Optimize images (WebP, responsive)

## Accessibility (WCAG AA)

### Required
- Focus indicators on all interactive elements
- Keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter, Escape, Arrows)
- Color contrast >= 4.5:1 for text
- ARIA labels on icon-only buttons
- Alt text for all meaningful images
- Semantic HTML structure

### Interactive Elements
- Focus trapped in modals
- Escape key closes dialogs
- Skip links for navigation
- Form error messages announced

## Code Quality

### General Principles
- DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself)
- Single Responsibility
- Explicit over implicit
- No magic numbers/strings
- Self-documenting code

### HTML
- Semantic tags (`<nav>`, `<main>`, `<article>`, not just `<div>`)
- Proper heading hierarchy
- Self-closing tags for void elements
- Meaningful class names

### CSS
- Mobile-first approach
- Avoid `!important`
- CSS custom properties for reusable values
- Consistent naming convention (BEM or similar)
- Comment complex animations

### JavaScript/TypeScript
- ES6+ syntax (const, let, arrow functions)
- TypeScript strict mode when available
- No `any` types for user data
- Async/await over raw promises
- Event delegation where appropriate
- Defensive programming (null checks)

### Python
- Type hints on functions
- Docstrings for public functions
- PEP 8 style compliance
- Context managers for resources
- Proper exception handling

## Design System Compliance

Check project's design system for:
- Color palette adherence
- Typography consistency
- Spacing system (4px, 8px base)
- Component patterns
- Dark/light mode support

## Testing Requirements

### Minimum Coverage
- Critical paths tested
- Edge cases covered
- Error states handled
- Accessibility tested

### Test Types
| Type | When |
|------|------|
| Unit | Business logic, utilities |
| Integration | API endpoints, data flow |
| E2E | Critical user journeys |

## Surgical Simplicity

Changes should impact minimal code relevant to task only.

- Every line intentional
- No commented-out code
- No dead code
- Clean, documented, maintainable
- Follow existing patterns

## Review Checklist

### Pre-Commit
- [ ] Task file updated
- [ ] Tests pass
- [ ] Type check passes (if TS)
- [ ] Lint passes
- [ ] Build succeeds
- [ ] No console errors

### Security
- [ ] Input sanitized
- [ ] Auth on protected routes
- [ ] No exposed secrets
- [ ] Privacy respected

### Performance
- [ ] Bundle size acceptable
- [ ] No layout thrashing
- [ ] Images optimized
- [ ] Lazy loading used

### Accessibility
- [ ] Keyboard navigable
- [ ] Focus indicators
- [ ] Contrast sufficient
- [ ] ARIA labels present

### Quality
- [ ] Code follows patterns
- [ ] No magic values
- [ ] Proper error handling
- [ ] Self-documenting

## See Also

- `CLAUDE.md` - Project workflow rules
- `.claude/workflow-checklist.md` - Complete checklist
- Project-specific code-reviewer skills for detailed requirements

     9→
    10→## Design Thinking Process
    11→

    41→
    42→## User Psychology Principles
    43→
    44→### Cognitive Load

    49→

   100→| Dashboard | At-a-glance metrics |

   102→

   104→
   105→| Pattern | Use Case |
   106→|---------|----------|
   107→| Toast | Non-blocking info |
   108→| Modal | Blocking/important |
   109→| Inline | Contextual feedback |
   110→| Banner | Global notifications |
   111→| Progress | Long operations |
   112→| Skeleton | Loading states |
   113→
   114→## Mobile UX Considerations
   115→
   116→### Touch Targets
   117→- Minimum 44x44px
   118→- 8px spacing between targets
   119→- Thumb-friendly placement
   120→- Bottom sheet for actions
   121→
   122→### Gestures
   123→- Tap: Primary action
   124→- Swipe: Navigate/reveal
   125→- Long press: Secondary actions
   126→- Pinch: Zoom (media)
   127→
   128→### Mobile Patterns
   129→- Bottom navigation
   130→- Pull to refresh
   131→- Infinite scroll (carefully)
   132→- Full-screen modals
   133→- Native keyboard handling
   134→
   135→## Accessibility (UX Perspective)
   136→
   137→### Inclusive Design
   138→- Design for extremes, works for all
   139→- Consider temporary disabilities
   140→- Support different input methods
   141→- Test with real users
   142→
   143→### Key Considerations
   144→
   145→| Aspect | UX Approach |
   146→|--------|-------------|
   147→| Vision | High contrast, scalable text, alt text |
   148→| Motor | Large targets, keyboard nav, no timing |
   149→| Cognitive | Simple language, clear structure |
   150→| Hearing | Captions, visual indicators |
   151→
   152→### Testing
   153→- Screen reader walkthrough
   154→- Keyboard-only navigation
   155→- Color blindness simulation
   156→- Reduced motion mode
   157→
   158→## Design Critique Framework
   159→
   160→### Criteria
   161→
   162→| Aspect | Questions |
   163→|--------|-----------|
   164→| Usability | Can users complete tasks? |
   165→| Clarity | Is purpose immediately clear? |
   166→| Consistency | Follows established patterns? |
   167→| Hierarchy | Most important stands out? |
   168→| Feedback | Users know system state? |
   169→| Error handling | Recoverable mistakes? |
   170→| Accessibility | Works for everyone? |
   171→
   172→### Constructive Feedback
   173→
   174→**Instead of:** "This is confusing"
   175→**Say:** "Users might not understand X because Y. Consider Z."
   176→
   177→**Instead of:** "I don't like this"
   178→**Say:** "This pattern may cause issues because... Alternative approach..."
   179→
   180→## Common UX Patterns
   181→
   182→### Onboarding
   183→1. Progressive disclosure
   184→2. Contextual tips
   185→3. Interactive tutorials
   186→4. Skip option always
   187→5. Revisitable help
   188→
   189→### Empty States
   190→1. Explain what goes here
   191→2. Guide to first action
   192→3. Show example content
   193→4. Make it delightful
   194→
   195→### Loading States
   196→1. Skeleton screens > spinners
   197→2. Progress indicators for long ops
   198→3. Optimistic UI when safe
   199→4. Background loading when possible
   200→
   201→### Error States
   202→1. Explain what happened
   203→2. Suggest how to fix
   204→3. Provide recovery action
   205→4. Don't blame user
   206→
   207→### Success States
   208→1. Confirm action completed
   209→2. Show next steps
   210→3. Celebrate achievements
   211→4. Enable sharing if appropriate
   212→
   213→## Content Strategy
   214→
   215→### Microcopy
   216→- Be concise
   217→- Use active voice
   218→- Avoid jargon
   219→- Be human
   220→- Guide action
   221→
   222→### Labels & Instructions
   223→- Clear and specific
   224→- Explain why (if needed)
   225→- Example formats
   226→- Required vs optional
   227→
   228→### Error Messages
   229→- Specific problem
   230→- How to fix
   231→- No technical jargon
   232→- Apologetic when appropriate
   233→
   234→## Metrics That Matter
   235→
   236→### Engagement
   237→- Time on task
   238→- Completion rate
   239→- Return visits
   240→- Feature adoption
   241→
   242→### Satisfaction
   243→- NPS score
   244→- CSAT surveys
   245→- Support tickets
   246→- User feedback
   247→
   248→### Performance
   249→- Task success rate
   250→- Error rate
   251→- Time to first action
   252→- Abandonment points
   253→
   254→## Design Review Checklist
   255→
   256→### Functionality
   257→- [ ] Primary task achievable
   258→- [ ] Edge cases handled
   259→- [ ] Error states designed
   260→- [ ] Loading states designed
   261→
   262→### Usability
   263→- [ ] Clear visual hierarchy
   264→- [ ] Obvious next actions
   265→- [ ] Consistent with patterns
   266→- [ ] Minimal cognitive load
   267→
   268→### Accessibility
   269→- [ ] Keyboard navigable
   270→- [ ] Screen reader tested
   271→- [ ] Color contrast sufficient
   272→- [ ] Touch targets sized
   273→
   274→### Polish
   275→- [ ] Micro-interactions smooth
   276→- [ ] Copy clear and helpful
   277→- [ ] Responsive breakpoints
   278→- [ ] Performance acceptable
   279→
   280→## See Also
   281→
   282→- Project-specific UX skills for detailed requirements
   283→- `.claude/skills/generic-design-system/` - Visual design
   284→- `CLAUDE.md` - Project constraints
   285→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

---
name: generic-ux-designer
description: Professional UX expertise for any project type. Covers user research, design thinking, interaction patterns, accessibility, and design critique. Use when designing new features, improving UX, conducting design reviews, or evaluating user flows.
---

# Generic UX Designer

Professional UX expertise that adapts to any project's constraints and user needs.

## Design Thinking Process

### 1. Empathize
- Understand user needs
- Identify pain points
- Map user journeys
- Research competitors

### 2. Define
- Problem statements
- User personas
- Success metrics
- Constraints

### 3. Ideate
- Brainstorm solutions
- Explore alternatives
- Consider edge cases
- Sketch concepts

### 4. Prototype
- Low-fidelity first
- Test assumptions
- Iterate quickly
- Validate with users

### 5. Test
- Usability testing
- A/B testing
- Analytics review
- Iterate based on data

## User Psychology Principles

### Cognitive Load
- Reduce choices (Hick's Law)
- Chunk information (Miller's Law)
- Use progressive disclosure
- Minimize user memory load

### Visual Hierarchy
- Size indicates importance
- Contrast draws attention
- Whitespace groups elements
- F-pattern/Z-pattern reading

### Behavioral Patterns
- Users scan, not read
- Familiarity breeds trust
- Immediate feedback essential
- Error prevention > recovery

### Emotional Design
- Delight in micro-interactions
- Personality through copy
- Trust through transparency
- Satisfaction through completion

## Interaction Patterns

### Navigation

| Pattern | Use Case |
|---------|----------|
| Top nav | Simple sites, < 7 items |
| Side nav | Complex apps, many sections |
| Bottom nav | Mobile apps, < 5 items |
| Hamburger | Mobile, space-constrained |
| Tabs | Related content sections |
| Breadcrumbs | Deep hierarchies |

### Forms

| Pattern | Benefit |
|---------|---------|
| Single column | Faster completion |
| Inline validation | Immediate feedback |
| Field grouping | Logical flow |
| Smart defaults | Reduced effort |
| Progress indicator | Clear expectations |
| Error summary | Easy correction |

### Data Display

| Pattern | Use Case |
|---------|----------|
| Cards | Browsable content |
| Tables | Comparable data |
| Lists | Sequential items |
| Timeline | Chronological events |
| Dashboard | At-a-glance metrics |
| Search + filters | Large datasets |

### Feedback

| Pattern | Use Case |
|---------|----------|
| Toast | Non-blocking info |
| Modal | Blocking/important |
| Inline | Contextual feedback |
| Banner | Global notifications |
| Progress | Long operations |
| Skeleton | Loading states |

## Mobile UX Considerations

### Touch Targets
- Minimum 44x44px
- 8px spacing between targets
- Thumb-friendly placement
- Bottom sheet for actions

### Gestures
- Tap: Primary action
- Swipe: Navigate/reveal
- Long press: Secondary actions
- Pinch: Zoom (media)

### Mobile Patterns
- Bottom navigation
- Pull to refresh
- Infinite scroll (carefully)
- Full-screen modals
- Native keyboard handling

## Accessibility (UX Perspective)

### Inclusive Design
- Design for extremes, works for all
- Consider temporary disabilities
- Support different input methods
- Test with real users

### Key Considerations

| Aspect | UX Approach |
|--------|-------------|
| Vision | High contrast, scalable text, alt text |
| Motor | Large targets, keyboard nav, no timing |
| Cognitive | Simple language, clear structure |
| Hearing | Captions, visual indicators |

### Testing
- Screen reader walkthrough
- Keyboard-only navigation
- Color blindness simulation
- Reduced motion mode

## Design Critique Framework

### Criteria

| Aspect | Questions |
|--------|-----------|
| Usability | Can users complete tasks? |
| Clarity | Is purpose immediately clear? |
| Consistency | Follows established patterns? |
| Hierarchy | Most important stands out? |
| Feedback | Users know system state? |
| Error handling | Recoverable mistakes? |
| Accessibility | Works for everyone? |

### Constructive Feedback

**Instead of:** "This is confusing"
**Say:** "Users might not understand X because Y. Consider Z."

**Instead of:** "I don't like this"
**Say:** "This pattern may cause issues because... Alternative approach..."

## Common UX Patterns

### Onboarding
1. Progressive disclosure
2. Contextual tips
3. Interactive tutorials
4. Skip option always
5. Revisitable help

### Empty States
1. Explain what goes here
2. Guide to first action
3. Show example content
4. Make it delightful

### Loading States
1. Skeleton screens > spinners
2. Progress indicators for long ops
3. Optimistic UI when safe
4. Background loading when possible

### Error States
1. Explain what happened
2. Suggest how to fix
3. Provide recovery action
4. Don't blame user

### Success States
1. Confirm action completed
2. Show next steps
3. Celebrate achievements
4. Enable sharing if appropriate

## Content Strategy

### Microcopy
- Be concise
- Use active voice
- Avoid jargon
- Be human
- Guide action

### Labels & Instructions
- Clear and specific
- Explain why (if needed)
- Example formats
- Required vs optional

### Error Messages
- Specific problem
- How to fix
- No technical jargon
- Apologetic when appropriate

## Metrics That Matter

### Engagement
- Time on task
- Completion rate
- Return visits
- Feature adoption

### Satisfaction
- NPS score
- CSAT surveys
- Support tickets
- User feedback

### Performance
- Task success rate
- Error rate
- Time to first action
- Abandonment points

## Design Review Checklist

### Functionality
- [ ] Primary task achievable
- [ ] Edge cases handled
- [ ] Error states designed
- [ ] Loading states designed

### Usability
- [ ] Clear visual hierarchy
- [ ] Obvious next actions
- [ ] Consistent with patterns
- [ ] Minimal cognitive load

### Accessibility
- [ ] Keyboard navigable
- [ ] Screen reader tested
- [ ] Color contrast sufficient
- [ ] Touch targets sized

### Polish
- [ ] Micro-interactions smooth
- [ ] Copy clear and helpful
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints
- [ ] Performance acceptable

## See Also

- Project-specific UX skills for detailed requirements
- `.claude/skills/generic-design-system/` - Visual design
- `CLAUDE.md` - Project constraints

---
name: generic-design-system
description: Complete design system reference for any project - colors, typography, spacing, components, animations. Adapts to project theme and tech stack. Use when implementing UI, choosing colors, creating animations, or ensuring brand consistency.
---

# Generic Design System

Comprehensive design system reference that adapts to any project's visual language.

## Color System

### Defining Project Colors

Every project should define:
- **Primary**: Main brand color
- **Secondary**: Supporting color
- **Accent**: Highlight/CTA color
- **Background**: Base canvas color
- **Foreground**: Primary text color
- **Muted**: Secondary elements
- **Destructive**: Error/danger states
- **Success**: Positive states
- **Warning**: Caution states

### Color Mode Support

| Mode | Considerations |
|------|----------------|
| Light | Higher contrast, softer backgrounds |
| Dark | Reduced blue light, darker backgrounds |
| High Contrast | WCAG AAA compliance |

### Contrast Requirements

| Use Case | Minimum Ratio |
|----------|---------------|
| Body text | 4.5:1 (AA) |
| Large text (24px+) | 3:1 (AA) |
| UI components | 3:1 (AA) |
| Enhanced | 7:1 (AAA) |

## Typography

### Font Stack

```css
/* System fonts for performance */
--font-sans: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif;
--font-mono: 'SF Mono', Monaco, 'Courier New', monospace;

/* Custom fonts loaded via @font-face or Google Fonts */
```

### Type Scale

| Name | Size | Line Height | Use |
|------|------|-------------|-----|
| xs | 12px | 16px | Captions, labels |
| sm | 14px | 20px | Secondary text |
| base | 16px | 24px | Body text |
| lg | 18px | 28px | Lead paragraphs |
| xl | 20px | 28px | Section headers |
| 2xl | 24px | 32px | Page headers |
| 3xl | 30px | 36px | Hero text |
| 4xl | 36px | 40px | Display |

### Font Weights

| Weight | Value | Use |
|--------|-------|-----|
| Normal | 400 | Body text |
| Medium | 500 | UI elements |
| Semibold | 600 | Subheadings |
| Bold | 700 | Headings |

## Spacing System

### Base Unit

Recommend 4px or 8px base unit for consistency.

| Token | 4px Base | 8px Base | Use |
|-------|----------|----------|-----|
| 0 | 0 | 0 | None |
| 1 | 4px | 8px | Tight spacing |
| 2 | 8px | 16px | Default gap |
| 3 | 12px | 24px | Section padding |
| 4 | 16px | 32px | Card padding |
| 5 | 20px | 40px | Section margins |
| 6 | 24px | 48px | Page margins |
| 8 | 32px | 64px | Large sections |

### Layout Grid

```css
/* Common container widths */
--container-sm: 640px;
--container-md: 768px;
--container-lg: 1024px;
--container-xl: 1280px;
--container-2xl: 1536px;
```

## Border Radius

| Token | Value | Use |
|-------|-------|-----|
| none | 0 | Sharp corners |
| sm | 2px | Subtle rounding |
| DEFAULT | 4px | Buttons, inputs |
| md | 6px | Cards |
| lg | 8px | Modals |
| xl | 12px | Large cards |
| 2xl | 16px | Hero sections |
| full | 9999px | Pills, avatars |

## Shadows

| Token | Use |
|-------|-----|
| sm | Subtle elevation (buttons) |
| DEFAULT | Standard elevation (cards) |
| md | Medium elevation (dropdowns) |
| lg | High elevation (modals) |
| xl | Maximum elevation (popovers) |

## Animation

### Timing Functions

```css
--ease-default: cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1);
--ease-in: cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 1, 1);
--ease-out: cubic-bezier(0, 0, 0.2, 1);
--ease-in-out: cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1);
--ease-bounce: cubic-bezier(0.68, -0.55, 0.265, 1.55);
```

### Duration

| Token | Duration | Use |
|-------|----------|-----|
| fast | 100ms | Micro-interactions |
| DEFAULT | 200ms | Most transitions |
| slow | 300ms | Complex animations |
| slower | 500ms | Page transitions |

### GPU-Accelerated Properties

**DO animate:**
- `transform` (translate, scale, rotate)
- `opacity`

**AVOID animating:**
- `width`, `height`
- `top`, `left`, `right`, `bottom`
- `margin`, `padding`
- `background-color` (OK for simple)

## Components

### Buttons

| Variant | Use |
|---------|-----|
| Primary | Main actions (Submit, Save) |
| Secondary | Alternative actions |
| Ghost | Subtle actions |
| Outline | Less prominent actions |
| Destructive | Delete, remove actions |

### Form Inputs

- Clear focus states
- Error states with messages
- Placeholder text as hint
- Label always visible
- Required indicator

### Cards

- Consistent padding
- Optional header/footer
- Hover state for interactive
- Focus state for keyboard

### Modals/Dialogs

- Focus trapped inside
- Escape to close
- Click outside to close (optional)
- Centered or slide-in
- Dark overlay

## Icons

### Size Tokens

| Token | Size | Use |
|-------|------|-----|
| xs | 12px | Inline with small text |
| sm | 16px | Inline with body |
| md | 20px | Buttons, inputs |
| lg | 24px | Headers |
| xl | 32px | Features |

### Stroke Width

- 1.5px for light icons
- 2px for standard icons
- 2.5px for bold icons

## Responsive Breakpoints

| Token | Min Width | Devices |
|-------|-----------|---------|
| sm | 640px | Large phones |
| md | 768px | Tablets |
| lg | 1024px | Laptops |
| xl | 1280px | Desktops |
| 2xl | 1536px | Large desktops |

## Dark Mode Strategy

```css
/* CSS Variables approach */
:root {
  --background: #ffffff;
  --foreground: #000000;
}

[data-theme="dark"] {
  --background: #000000;
  --foreground: #ffffff;
}

/* Or media query */
@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
  :root {
    --background: #000000;
    --foreground: #ffffff;
  }
}
```

## Implementation Patterns

### CSS Variables (Recommended)

```css
:root {
  --color-primary: #3b82f6;
  --spacing-4: 16px;
  --radius-md: 6px;
}

.button {
  background: var(--color-primary);
  padding: var(--spacing-4);
  border-radius: var(--radius-md);
}
```

### Tailwind Config

```js
module.exports = {
  theme: {
    extend: {
      colors: { primary: '#3b82f6' },
      spacing: { '4': '16px' },
      borderRadius: { md: '6px' }
    }
  }
}
```

## See Also

- Project-specific design system skills for detailed brand requirements
- `.claude/workflow-checklist.md` - Styling checklist
- Project's `CLAUDE.md` for design constraints

1→---
     2→name: generic-feature-developer
     3→description: Guide feature development with architecture patterns for any tech stack. Covers frontend, backend, full-stack, and automation projects. Use when adding new features, modifying systems, or planning changes.
     4→---
     5→
     6→# Generic Feature Developer
     7→
     8→Guide feature development across any tech stack with proven architecture patterns.
     9→
    10→## Feature Development Workflow
    11→
    12→### 1. Understand Requirements
    13→- Read existing CLAUDE.md and documentation
    14→- Identify affected systems/files
    15→- List dependencies and constraints
    16→- Define acceptance criteria
    17→
    18→### 2. Plan Architecture
    19→- Choose appropriate patterns
    20→- Design data flow
    21→- Identify edge cases
    22→- Plan error handling
    23→
    24→### 3. Implement Incrementally
    25→- Small, testable changes
    26→- Commit frequently
    27→- Update task file as you go
    28→- Get approval at milestones
    29→
    30→### 4. Test & Document
    31→- Write/update tests
    32→- Update documentation
    33→- Run full test suite
    34→- Performance check
    35→
    36→## Architecture Patterns by Project Type
    37→
    38→### Static Sites (HTML/CSS/JS)
    39→
    40→```
    41→project/
    42→├── index.html          # Main entry
    43→├── css/
    44→│   ├── reset.css       # CSS reset
    45→│   ├── variables.css   # CSS custom properties
    46→│   └── style.css       # Main styles
    47→├── js/
    48→│   ├── main.js         # Entry point
    49→│   └── utils.js        # Utilities
    50→└── assets/
    51→    ├── images/
    52→    └── fonts/
    53→```
    54→
    55→**Patterns:**
    56→- CSS custom properties for theming
    57→- ES modules for organization
    58→- Event delegation for dynamic content
    59→- Progressive enhancement
    60→
    61→### React/Next.js Applications
    62→
    63→```
    64→src/
    65→├── components/
    66→│   ├── ui/             # Base UI components
    67→│   ├── features/       # Feature-specific
    68→│   └── layout/         # Layout components
    69→├── hooks/              # Custom React hooks
    70→├── lib/                # Utilities, helpers
    71→├── stores/             # State management
    72→├── services/           # API calls
    73→└── types/              # TypeScript types
    74→```
    75→
    76→**Patterns:**
    77→- Container/Presentational split
    78→- Custom hooks for logic reuse
    79→- Zustand/Redux for global state
    80→- React Query for server state
    81→- Compound components for complex UI
    82→
    83→### Node.js/Express Backend
    84→
    85→```
    86→src/
    87→├── routes/             # Route handlers
    88→├── controllers/        # Business logic
    89→├── services/           # External services
    90→├── models/             # Data models
    91→├── middleware/         # Express middleware
    92→├── utils/              # Helpers
    93→└── config/             # Configuration
    94→```
    95→
    96→**Patterns:**
    97→- MVC or service layer
    98→- Dependency injection
    99→- Repository pattern for data
   100→- Error middleware
   101→- Validation middleware
   102→
   103→### NestJS Backend
   104→
   105→```
   106→src/
   107→├── modules/
   108→│   └── [feature]/
   109→│       ├── feature.module.ts
   110→│       ├── feature.controller.ts
   111→│       ├── feature.service.ts
   112→│       └── dto/
   113→├── common/
   114→│   ├── guards/
   115→│   ├── decorators/
   116→│   └── interceptors/
   117→└── prisma/             # Database
   118→```
   119→
   120→**Patterns:**
   121→- Module-based organization
   122→- DTOs for validation
   123→- Guards for auth
   124→- Interceptors for transforms
   125→- Prisma for type-safe DB
   126→
   127→### Python Automation
   128→
   129→```
   130→automation/
   131→├── scripts/
   132→│   └── main.py         # Entry point
   133→├── lib/
   134→│   ├── validators.py   # Data validation
   135→│   └── utils.py        # Utilities
   136→├── data/
   137→│   └── config.json     # Configuration
   138→├── tests/
   139→│   └── test_main.py
   140→└── .github/
   141→    └── workflows/
   142→        └── schedule.yml
   143→```
   144→
   145→**Patterns:**
   146→- Dry run mode for testing
   147→- Environment-based config
   148→- Structured logging
   149→- Error handling with retry
   150→- GitHub Actions for scheduling
   151→
   152→## Common Feature Types
   153→
   154→### Adding a New UI Component
   155→
   156→1. Create component file
   157→2. Add to exports
   158→3. Create stories/tests
   159→4. Use in feature
   160→5. Document usage
   161→
   162→### Adding an API Endpoint
   163→
   164→1. Define route
   165→2. Create controller/handler
   166→3. Add validation
   167→4. Implement service logic
   168→5. Add tests
   169→6. Document endpoint
   170→
   171→### Adding State Management
   172→
   173→1. Define state shape
   174→2. Create store/slice
   175→3. Add actions/mutations
   176→4. Connect to components
   177→5. Persist if needed
   178→
   179→### Adding Authentication
   180→
   181→1. Choose strategy (JWT, sessions, OAuth)
   182→2. Implement auth service
   183→3. Add middleware/guards
   184→4. Protect routes
   185→5. Handle tokens
   186→6. Add login/logout UI
   187→
   188→### Adding Database Feature
   189→
   190→1. Design schema
   191→2. Create migration
   192→3. Update models/types
   193→4. Add repository/service
   194→5. Integrate with API
   195→6. Test with fixtures
   196→
   197→## Error Handling Patterns
   198→
   199→### Frontend
   200→
   201→```typescript
   202→try {
   203→  const result = await apiCall();
   204→  // Handle success
   205→} catch (error) {
   206→  if (error instanceof NetworkError) {
   207→    showRetryDialog();
   208→  } else if (error instanceof ValidationError) {
   209→    showFieldErrors(error.fields);
   210→  } else {
   211→    showGenericError();
   212→    reportToSentry(error);
   213→  }
   214→}
   215→```
   216→
   217→### Backend
   218→
   219→```typescript
   220→// Express error middleware
   221→app.use((err, req, res, next) => {
   222→  logger.error(err);
   223→
   224→  if (err instanceof ValidationError) {
   225→    return res.status(400).json({ errors: err.errors });
   226→  }
   227→  if (err instanceof AuthError) {
   228→    return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Unauthorized' });
   229→  }
   230→

---
name: generic-feature-developer
description: Guide feature development with architecture patterns for any tech stack. Covers frontend, backend, full-stack, and automation projects. Use when adding new features, modifying systems, or planning changes.
---

# Generic Feature Developer

Guide feature development across any tech stack with proven architecture patterns.

## Feature Development Workflow

### 1. Understand Requirements
- Read existing CLAUDE.md and documentation
- Identify affected systems/files
- List dependencies and constraints
- Define acceptance criteria

### 2. Plan Architecture
- Choose appropriate patterns
- Design data flow
- Identify edge cases
- Plan error handling

### 3. Implement Incrementally
- Small, testable changes
- Commit frequently
- Update task file as you go
- Get approval at milestones

### 4. Test & Document
- Write/update tests
- Update documentation
- Run full test suite
- Performance check

## Architecture Patterns by Project Type

### Static Sites (HTML/CSS/JS)

```
project/
├── index.html          # Main entry
├── css/
│   ├── reset.css       # CSS reset
│   ├── variables.css   # CSS custom properties
│   └── style.css       # Main styles
├── js/
│   ├── main.js         # Entry point
│   └── utils.js        # Utilities
└── assets/
    ├── images/
    └── fonts/
```

**Patterns:**
- CSS custom properties for theming
- ES modules for organization
- Event delegation for dynamic content
- Progressive enhancement

### React/Next.js Applications

```
src/
├── components/
│   ├── ui/             # Base UI components
│   ├── features/       # Feature-specific
│   └── layout/         # Layout components
├── hooks/              # Custom React hooks
├── lib/                # Utilities, helpers
├── stores/             # State management
├── services/           # API calls
└── types/              # TypeScript types
```

**Patterns:**
- Container/Presentational split
- Custom hooks for logic reuse
- Zustand/Redux for global state
- React Query for server state
- Compound components for complex UI

### Node.js/Express Backend

```
src/
├── routes/             # Route handlers
├── controllers/        # Business logic
├── services/           # External services
├── models/             # Data models
├── middleware/         # Express middleware
├── utils/              # Helpers
└── config/             # Configuration
```

**Patterns:**
- MVC or service layer
- Dependency injection
- Repository pattern for data
- Error middleware
- Validation middleware

### NestJS Backend

```
src/
├── modules/
│   └── [feature]/
│       ├── feature.module.ts
│       ├── feature.controller.ts
│       ├── feature.service.ts
│       └── dto/
├── common/
│   ├── guards/
│   ├── decorators/
│   └── interceptors/
└── prisma/             # Database
```

**Patterns:**
- Module-based organization
- DTOs for validation
- Guards for auth
- Interceptors for transforms
- Prisma for type-safe DB

### Python Automation

```
automation/
├── scripts/
│   └── main.py         # Entry point
├── lib/
│   ├── validators.py   # Data validation
│   └── utils.py        # Utilities
├── data/
│   └── config.json     # Configuration
├── tests/
│   └── test_main.py
└── .github/
    └── workflows/
        └── schedule.yml
```

**Patterns:**
- Dry run mode for testing
- Environment-based config
- Structured logging
- Error handling with retry
- GitHub Actions for scheduling

## Common Feature Types

### Adding a New UI Component

1. Create component file
2. Add to exports
3. Create stories/tests
4. Use in feature
5. Document usage

### Adding an API Endpoint

1. Define route
2. Create controller/handler
3. Add validation
4. Implement service logic
5. Add tests
6. Document endpoint

### Adding State Management

1. Define state shape
2. Create store/slice
3. Add actions/mutations
4. Connect to components
5. Persist if needed

### Adding Authentication

1. Choose strategy (JWT, sessions, OAuth)
2. Implement auth service
3. Add middleware/guards
4. Protect routes
5. Handle tokens
6. Add login/logout UI

### Adding Database Feature

1. Design schema
2. Create migration
3. Update models/types
4. Add repository/service
5. Integrate with API
6. Test with fixtures

## Error Handling Patterns

### Frontend

```typescript
try {
  const result = await apiCall();
  // Handle success
} catch (error) {
  if (error instanceof NetworkError) {
    showRetryDialog();
  } else if (error instanceof ValidationError) {
    showFieldErrors(error.fields);
  } else {
    showGenericError();
    reportToSentry(error);
  }
}
```

### Backend

```typescript
// Express error middleware
app.use((err, req, res, next) => {
  logger.error(err);

  if (err instanceof ValidationError) {
    return res.status(400).json({ errors: err.errors });
  }
  if (err instanceof AuthError) {
    return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Unauthorized' });
  }

  res.status(500).json({ message: 'Internal server error' });
});
```

## Data Flow Patterns

### Unidirectional (React)

```
User Action → Dispatch → Store → State Change → Re-render
```

### Request/Response (API)

```
Client Request → Middleware → Controller → Service → Database
                                                       ↓
Client Response ← Serializer ← Controller ← Service ←
```

### Event-Driven

```
Event Emitted → Event Bus → Subscribers → Side Effects
```

## Integration Patterns

### Third-Party APIs

1. Create service wrapper
2. Handle authentication
3. Implement retry logic
4. Cache responses
5. Handle rate limits
6. Mock for tests

### Database Operations

1. Use transactions for multi-step
2. Handle constraints/conflicts
3. Implement soft delete
4. Add audit logging
5. Optimize queries

## Testing Strategy

| Layer | Test Type | Tools |
|-------|-----------|-------|
| UI | Component | Testing Library |
| Logic | Unit | Jest, Vitest |
| API | Integration | Supertest |
| E2E | End-to-end | Playwright, Cypress |
| Visual | Snapshot | Storybook, Percy |

## Performance Considerations

### Frontend
- Code splitting
- Lazy loading
- Memoization
- Virtual scrolling
- Debounce/throttle

### Backend
- Database indexing
- Query optimization
- Caching (Redis)
- Connection pooling
- Background jobs

## See Also

- Project-specific feature developer skills
- `CLAUDE.md` - Project workflow rules
- `.claude/automation-guide.md` - Development workflow

---
name: generic-code-reviewer
description: Review code for bugs, security vulnerabilities, performance issues, accessibility gaps, and CLAUDE.md workflow compliance. Supports any tech stack - HTML/CSS/JS, React, TypeScript, Node.js, Python, NestJS, Next.js, and more. Use when completing features, before commits, or reviewing pull requests.
---

# Generic Code Reviewer

Review code against production quality standards. Adapts to any project's tech stack.

**Full Standards:** See [Code Review Standards](./../_shared/CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md)

## CLAUDE.md Compliance

**Always check the project's CLAUDE.md for specific rules.**

**Before ANY Commit:**
- Task file exists in `tasks/[feature-name].md`
- All tests passing
- Type checking passing (if TypeScript)
- No console errors/warnings
- Bundle size within limits

## Tech Stack Detection

| Detection | Stack | Key Checks |
|-----------|-------|------------|
| React in package.json | React/TS | Components, hooks, state |
| Next.js in package.json | Next.js | SSR, API routes |
| NestJS in package.json | NestJS | Guards, DTOs, services |
| .html files, no build | Vanilla | Semantic HTML, minimal JS |
| .py files | Python | Type hints, validation |

## Security Checks

### Frontend
- Sanitize input (textContent, not innerHTML)
- `unknown` type for external data
- No exposed API keys
- HTTPS for external requests

### Backend
- Input validation on all endpoints
- Auth on protected routes
- Parameterized queries (no raw SQL)
- Secrets in environment variables

## Performance Requirements

| Project Type | Target |
|--------------|--------|
| Static site | < 50KB (excluding media) |
| SPA/React | < 100KB gzipped initial |
| Full-stack | < 200KB gzipped initial |

### Animation
- GPU-accelerated only (transform, opacity)
- 60fps target
- Use requestAnimationFrame

## Accessibility (WCAG AA)

- Focus indicators on interactive elements
- Keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter, Escape)
- Color contrast >= 4.5:1
- ARIA labels on icon-only buttons
- Alt text for meaningful images
- Semantic HTML structure

## Code Quality

- DRY, Single Responsibility
- No magic numbers/strings
- Self-documenting code
- Follow existing patterns
- No commented-out/dead code

## Review Checklist

### Pre-Commit
- [ ] Tests pass
- [ ] Type/lint pass
- [ ] Build succeeds
- [ ] No console errors

### Security
- [ ] Input sanitized
- [ ] Auth on protected routes
- [ ] No exposed secrets

### Performance
- [ ] Bundle size acceptable
- [ ] Images optimized
- [ ] Lazy loading used

### Accessibility
- [ ] Keyboard navigable
- [ ] Focus indicators
- [ ] ARIA labels present

## See Also

- [Code Review Standards](./../_shared/CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md) - Full requirements
- [Design Patterns](./../_shared/DESIGN_PATTERNS.md) - UI consistency
- Project `CLAUDE.md` - Workflow rules

---
name: generic-ux-designer
description: Professional UX expertise for any project type. Covers user research, design thinking, interaction patterns, accessibility, and design critique. Use when designing new features, improving UX, conducting design reviews, or evaluating user flows.
---

# Generic UX Designer

Professional UX expertise adapting to any project's constraints.

**Foundational Reference:** See [UX Principles](./../_shared/UX_PRINCIPLES.md)

## Design Thinking Process

1. **Empathize** - Understand user needs, pain points, journeys
2. **Define** - Problem statements, personas, success metrics
3. **Ideate** - Brainstorm solutions, explore alternatives
4. **Prototype** - Low-fidelity first, test assumptions
5. **Test** - Usability testing, iterate on data

## User Psychology Applied

### Cognitive Load
- Reduce choices (Hick's Law)
- Chunk information (Miller's Law)
- Progressive disclosure
- Minimize memory load

### Visual Hierarchy
- Size indicates importance
- Contrast draws attention
- Whitespace groups elements
- F-pattern/Z-pattern reading

## Interaction Patterns

### Navigation
| Pattern | Use Case |
|---------|----------|
| Top nav | Simple sites, < 7 items |
| Side nav | Complex apps, many sections |
| Bottom nav | Mobile apps, < 5 items |
| Tabs | Related content sections |

### Forms
- Single column layout
- Inline validation
- Smart defaults
- Progress indicators

### Feedback
| Pattern | Use Case |
|---------|----------|
| Toast | Non-blocking info |
| Modal | Blocking/important |
| Inline | Contextual feedback |
| Skeleton | Loading states |

## Mobile Considerations

- Touch targets: 44x44px minimum
- 8px spacing between targets
- Thumb-friendly placement
- Bottom sheets for actions

## Accessibility (UX Perspective)

| Aspect | Approach |
|--------|----------|
| Vision | High contrast, scalable text |
| Motor | Large targets, keyboard nav |
| Cognitive | Simple language, clear structure |
| Hearing | Captions, visual indicators |

## Design Critique Framework

| Aspect | Questions |
|--------|-----------|
| Usability | Can users complete tasks? |
| Clarity | Is purpose immediately clear? |
| Consistency | Follows established patterns? |
| Hierarchy | Most important stands out? |
| Feedback | Users know system state? |
| Accessibility | Works for everyone? |

## UX Patterns

### Empty States
1. Explain what goes here
2. Guide to first action
3. Show example content

### Loading States
1. Skeleton screens > spinners
2. Progress for long operations
3. Optimistic UI when safe

### Error States
1. Explain what happened
2. Suggest how to fix
3. Don't blame user

## Design Review Checklist

- [ ] Primary task achievable
- [ ] Clear visual hierarchy
- [ ] Keyboard navigable
- [ ] Touch targets sized
- [ ] Copy clear and helpful

## See Also

- [UX Principles](./../_shared/UX_PRINCIPLES.md) - Core psychology
- [Design Patterns](./../_shared/DESIGN_PATTERNS.md) - Visual patterns
- Project `CLAUDE.md` - Constraints

---
name: generic-design-system
description: Complete design system reference for any project - colors, typography, spacing, components, animations. Adapts to project theme and tech stack. Use when implementing UI, choosing colors, creating animations, or ensuring brand consistency.
---

# Generic Design System

Design system reference adapting to any project's visual language.

**Foundational Reference:** See [Design Patterns](./../_shared/DESIGN_PATTERNS.md)

## Color System

**Every project should define:**
- Primary, Secondary, Accent
- Background, Foreground, Muted
- Destructive, Success, Warning

### Contrast Requirements
| Use Case | Minimum |
|----------|---------|
| Body text | 4.5:1 (AA) |
| Large text | 3:1 (AA) |
| UI components | 3:1 (AA) |

## Typography

### Font Stack
```css
--font-sans: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif;
--font-mono: 'SF Mono', Monaco, 'Courier New', monospace;
```

### Type Scale
| Name | Size | Use |
|------|------|-----|
| sm | 14px | Secondary text |
| base | 16px | Body text |
| lg | 18px | Lead paragraphs |
| xl | 20px | Section headers |
| 2xl | 24px | Page headers |

## Spacing System

**Base unit:** 4px or 8px

| Token | 4px Base | Use |
|-------|----------|-----|
| 1 | 4px | Tight spacing |
| 2 | 8px | Default gap |
| 4 | 16px | Card padding |
| 6 | 24px | Page margins |

## Animation

### GPU-Accelerated Only
**DO animate:** `transform`, `opacity`
**AVOID:** `width`, `height`, `top`, `left`, `margin`, `padding`

### Duration
| Token | Duration | Use |
|-------|----------|-----|
| fast | 100ms | Micro-interactions |
| DEFAULT | 200ms | Most transitions |
| slow | 300ms | Complex animations |

### Timing
```css
--ease-default: cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1);
--ease-bounce: cubic-bezier(0.68, -0.55, 0.265, 1.55);
```

## Components

### Buttons
| Variant | Use |
|---------|-----|
| Primary | Main actions |
| Secondary | Alternative actions |
| Ghost | Subtle actions |
| Destructive | Delete actions |

### Form Inputs
- Clear focus states
- Error states with messages
- Label always visible
- Required indicator

### Modals
- Focus trapped inside
- Escape to close
- Dark overlay

## Responsive Breakpoints

| Token | Min Width |
|-------|-----------|
| sm | 640px |
| md | 768px |
| lg | 1024px |
| xl | 1280px |

## Dark Mode

```css
:root { --background: #ffffff; --foreground: #000000; }
[data-theme="dark"] { --background: #000000; --foreground: #ffffff; }
```

## See Also

- [Design Patterns](./../_shared/DESIGN_PATTERNS.md) - Core patterns
- [Code Review Standards](./../_shared/CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md) - Accessibility
- Project `CLAUDE.md` - Design constraints

---
name: generic-feature-developer
description: Guide feature development with architecture patterns for any tech stack. Covers frontend, backend, full-stack, and automation projects. Use when adding new features, modifying systems, or planning changes.
---

# Generic Feature Developer

Guide feature development across any tech stack.

## Development Workflow

1. **Understand** - Read CLAUDE.md, identify affected files, list constraints
2. **Plan** - Choose patterns, design data flow, identify edge cases
3. **Implement** - Small testable changes, commit frequently
4. **Test & Document** - Write tests, update docs, performance check

## Architecture by Project Type

### Static Sites
```
project/
├── index.html
├── css/           # variables.css, style.css
├── js/            # main.js, utils.js
└── assets/
```
**Patterns:** CSS variables, ES modules, event delegation

### React/Next.js
```
src/
├── components/    # ui/, features/, layout/
├── hooks/
├── stores/
├── services/
└── types/
```
**Patterns:** Container/Presentational, custom hooks, Zustand/React Query

### NestJS Backend
```
src/
├── modules/[feature]/
│   ├── feature.module.ts
│   ├── feature.controller.ts
│   ├── feature.service.ts
│   └── dto/
└── common/        # guards, decorators
```
**Patterns:** Module organization, DTOs, Guards, Prisma

## Common Features

### Adding UI Component
1. Create component → 2. Export → 3. Add tests → 4. Document

### Adding API Endpoint
1. Define route → 2. Add validation → 3. Implement service → 4. Test

### Adding State Management
1. Define shape → 2. Create store → 3. Add actions → 4. Connect components

### Database Changes
1. Design schema → 2. Create migration → 3. Update models → 4. Add service

## Error Handling

### Frontend
```typescript
try {
  await apiCall();
} catch (error) {
  if (error instanceof ValidationError) showFieldErrors(error.fields);
  else showGenericError();
}
```

### Backend
```typescript
if (err instanceof ValidationError) return res.status(400).json({ errors: err.errors });
if (err instanceof AuthError) return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Unauthorized' });
```

## Data Flow Patterns

**React:** User Action → Dispatch → Store → Re-render
**API:** Request → Middleware → Controller → Service → Database → Response

## Testing Strategy

| Layer | Type | Tools |
|-------|------|-------|
| UI | Component | Testing Library |
| Logic | Unit | Jest, Vitest |
| API | Integration | Supertest |
| E2E | End-to-end | Playwright |

## Performance

**Frontend:** Code splitting, lazy loading, memoization, debounce
**Backend:** DB indexing, caching, connection pooling, background jobs

## See Also

- [Code Review Standards](./../_shared/CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md) - Quality
- [Design Patterns](./../_shared/DESIGN_PATTERNS.md) - UI patterns
- Project `CLAUDE.md` - Workflow rules

Review code against production quality standards. Adapts to any project's tech stack.

**Full Standards:** See [Code Review Standards](./../_shared/CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md)

## CLAUDE.md Compliance

**Always check the project's CLAUDE.md for specific rules.**

**Before ANY Commit:**
- Task file exists in `tasks/[feature-name].md`
- All tests passing
- Type checking passing (if TypeScript)
- No console errors/warnings

## Tech Stack Detection

| Detection | Stack | Key Checks |
|-----------|-------|------------|
| React in package.json | React/TS | Components, hooks, state |

- Input validation on all endpoints
- Auth on protected routes
- Parameterized queries (no raw SQL)
- Secrets in environment variables

## Performance Requirements

| Project Type | Target |
|--------------|--------|
| Static site | < 50KB (excluding media) |
| SPA/React | < 100KB gzipped initial |
| Full-stack | < 200KB gzipped initial |

### Animation
- GPU-accelerated only (transform, opacity)
- 60fps target
- Use requestAnimationFrame

## Accessibility (WCAG AA)

- Focus indicators on interactive elements
- Keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter, Escape)
- Color contrast >= 4.5:1
- ARIA labels on icon-only buttons
- Alt text for meaningful images
- Semantic HTML structure

## Code Quality

- DRY, Single Responsibility
- No magic numbers/strings
- Self-documenting code
- Follow existing patterns
- No commented-out/dead code

## Review Checklist

### Pre-Commit
- [ ] Tests pass
- [ ] Type/lint pass
- [ ] Build succeeds
- [ ] No console errors

### Security
- [ ] Input sanitized
- [ ] Auth on protected routes
- [ ] No exposed secrets

### Performance
- [ ] Bundle size acceptable
- [ ] Images optimized
- [ ] Lazy loading used

### Accessibility
- [ ] Keyboard navigable
- [ ] Focus indicators
- [ ] ARIA labels present

## See Also

- [Code Review Standards](./../_shared/CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md) - Full requirements
- [Design Patterns](./../_shared/DESIGN_PATTERNS.md) - UI consistency
- Project `CLAUDE.md` - Workflow rules

toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
name: generic-code-reviewer
description: Review code for bugs, security vulnerabilities, performance issues, accessibility gaps, and CLAUDE.md workflow compliance. Supports any tech stack - HTML/CSS/JS, React, TypeScript, Node.js, Python, NestJS, Next.js, and more. Use when completing features, before commits, or reviewing pull requests.
---

# Generic Code Reviewer

Review code against development principles, CLAUDE.md workflow, and production quality standards. Adapts to any project's tech stack.

## CLAUDE.md Workflow Compliance

**Always check the project's CLAUDE.md file for specific rules.**

**Before ANY Commit:**
- Task file exists in `tasks/[feature-name].md`
- Task file has comprehensive review section
- All tests passing
- Type checking passing (if TypeScript)
- No console errors/warnings
- Bundle size within limits

## Tech Stack Detection

Automatically adapt checks based on project:

| Detection | Tech Stack | Key Checks |
|-----------|------------|------------|
| `package.json` with React | React/TypeScript | Components, hooks, state |
| `package.json` with Next.js | Next.js | SSR, API routes, routing |
| `package.json` with NestJS | NestJS | Guards, DTOs, services |
| `.html` files, no build | Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS | Semantic HTML, minimal JS |
| `requirements.txt` or `.py` | Python | Type hints, validation |

## Security Checks

### Frontend
- Sanitize all user input (use textContent, not innerHTML)
- Use `unknown` type for external data, validate before using
- No dynamic code execution (eval, Function constructor)
- No exposed API keys in client code
- HTTPS for all external requests

### Backend
- Input validation on all endpoints
- Authentication on protected routes
- No raw SQL queries (use ORM/parameterized)
- Secrets in environment variables
- Rate limiting on public endpoints

### General
- No tracking without user consent
- Privacy-first data handling
- WebCrypto API for encryption (no custom crypto)

## Performance Requirements

### Bundle/Page Size
| Project Type | Target |
|--------------|--------|
| Static site | < 50KB (excluding media) |
| SPA/React | < 100KB gzipped initial |
| Full-stack app | < 200KB gzipped initial |

### Animation Performance
- GPU-accelerated only (transform, opacity)
- 60fps target
- Avoid layout thrashing
- Use requestAnimationFrame

### Loading
- Lazy load non-essential code/images
- Code splitting for large features
- Optimize images (WebP, responsive)

## Accessibility (WCAG AA)

### Required
- Focus indicators on all interactive elements
- Keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter, Escape, Arrows)
- Color contrast >= 4.5:1 for text
- ARIA labels on icon-only buttons
- Alt text for all meaningful images
- Semantic HTML structure

### Interactive Elements
- Focus trapped in modals
- Escape key closes dialogs
- Skip links for navigation
- Form error messages announced

## Code Quality

### General Principles
- DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself)
- Single Responsibility
- Explicit over implicit
- No magic numbers/strings
- Self-documenting code

### HTML
- Semantic tags (`<nav>`, `<main>`, `<article>`, not just `<div>`)
- Proper heading hierarchy
- Self-closing tags for void elements
- Meaningful class names

### CSS
- Mobile-first approach
- Avoid `!important`
- CSS custom properties for reusable values
- Consistent naming convention (BEM or similar)
- Comment complex animations

### JavaScript/TypeScript
- ES6+ syntax (const, let, arrow functions)
- TypeScript strict mode when available
- No `any` types for user data
- Async/await over raw promises
- Event delegation where appropriate
- Defensive programming (null checks)

### Python
- Type hints on functions
- Docstrings for public functions
- PEP 8 style compliance
- Context managers for resources
- Proper exception handling

## Design System Compliance

Check project's design system for:
- Color palette adherence
- Typography consistency
- Spacing system (4px, 8px base)
- Component patterns
- Dark/light mode support

## Testing Requirements

### Minimum Coverage
- Critical paths tested
- Edge cases covered
- Error states handled
- Accessibility tested

### Test Types
| Type | When |
|------|------|
| Unit | Business logic, utilities |
| Integration | API endpoints, data flow |
| E2E | Critical user journeys |

## Surgical Simplicity

Changes should impact minimal code relevant to task only.

- Every line intentional
- No commented-out code
- No dead code
- Clean, documented, maintainable
- Follow existing patterns

## Review Checklist

### Pre-Commit
- [ ] Task file updated
- [ ] Tests pass
- [ ] Type check passes (if TS)
- [ ] Lint passes
- [ ] Build succeeds
- [ ] No console errors

### Security
- [ ] Input sanitized
- [ ] Auth on protected routes
- [ ] No exposed secrets
- [ ] Privacy respected

### Performance
- [ ] Bundle size acceptable
- [ ] No layout thrashing
- [ ] Images optimized
- [ ] Lazy loading used

### Accessibility
- [ ] Keyboard navigable
- [ ] Focus indicators
- [ ] Contrast sufficient
- [ ] ARIA labels present

### Quality
- [ ] Code follows patterns
- [ ] No magic values
- [ ] Proper error handling
- [ ] Self-documenting

## See Also

- `CLAUDE.md` - Project workflow rules
- `.claude/workflow-checklist.md` - Complete checklist
- Project-specific code-reviewer skills for detailed requirements

# Generic UX Designer

Professional UX expertise adapting to any project's constraints.

**Foundational Reference:** See [UX Principles](./../_shared/UX_PRINCIPLES.md)

## Design Thinking Process

1. **Empathize** - Understand user needs, pain points, journeys
2. **Define** - Problem statements, personas, success metrics
3. **Ideate** - Brainstorm solutions, explore alternatives
4. **Prototype** - Low-fidelity first, test assumptions

## User Psychology Applied

### Cognitive Load
- Reduce choices (Hick's Law)
- Chunk information (Miller's Law)
- Progressive disclosure
- Minimize memory load

### Visual Hierarchy
- Size indicates importance
- Contrast draws attention
- Whitespace groups elements
- F-pattern/Z-pattern reading

## Interaction Patterns

### Navigation
| Pattern | Use Case |
|---------|----------|
| Top nav | Simple sites, < 7 items |
| Side nav | Complex apps, many sections |
| Bottom nav | Mobile apps, < 5 items |
| Tabs | Related content sections |

### Forms
- Single column layout
- Inline validation
- Smart defaults
- Progress indicators

### Feedback
| Pattern | Use Case |
|---------|----------|
| Toast | Non-blocking info |
| Modal | Blocking/important |
| Inline | Contextual feedback |
| Skeleton | Loading states |

## Mobile Considerations

- Touch targets: 44x44px minimum
- 8px spacing between targets
- Thumb-friendly placement
- Bottom sheets for actions

## Accessibility (UX Perspective)

| Aspect | Approach |
|--------|----------|
| Vision | High contrast, scalable text |
| Motor | Large targets, keyboard nav |
| Cognitive | Simple language, clear structure |
| Hearing | Captions, visual indicators |

## Design Critique Framework

| Aspect | Questions |
|--------|-----------|
| Usability | Can users complete tasks? |
| Clarity | Is purpose immediately clear? |
| Consistency | Follows established patterns? |
| Hierarchy | Most important stands out? |
| Feedback | Users know system state? |
| Accessibility | Works for everyone? |

## UX Patterns

### Empty States
1. Explain what goes here
2. Guide to first action
3. Show example content

### Loading States
1. Skeleton screens > spinners
2. Progress for long operations
3. Optimistic UI when safe

### Error States
1. Explain what happened
2. Suggest how to fix
3. Don't blame user

## Design Review Checklist

- [ ] Primary task achievable
- [ ] Clear visual hierarchy
- [ ] Keyboard navigable
- [ ] Touch targets sized

- [UX Principles](./../_shared/UX_PRINCIPLES.md) - Core psychology

toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
name: generic-ux-designer
description: Professional UX expertise for any project type. Covers user research, design thinking, interaction patterns, accessibility, and design critique. Use when designing new features, improving UX, conducting design reviews, or evaluating user flows.
---

# Generic UX Designer

Professional UX expertise that adapts to any project's constraints and user needs.

## Design Thinking Process

### 1. Empathize
- Understand user needs
- Identify pain points
- Map user journeys
- Research competitors

### 2. Define
- Problem statements
- User personas
- Success metrics
- Constraints

### 3. Ideate
- Brainstorm solutions
- Explore alternatives
- Consider edge cases
- Sketch concepts

### 4. Prototype
- Low-fidelity first
- Test assumptions
- Iterate quickly
- Validate with users

### 5. Test
- Usability testing
- A/B testing
- Analytics review
- Iterate based on data

## User Psychology Principles

### Cognitive Load
- Reduce choices (Hick's Law)
- Chunk information (Miller's Law)
- Use progressive disclosure
- Minimize user memory load

### Visual Hierarchy
- Size indicates importance
- Contrast draws attention
- Whitespace groups elements
- F-pattern/Z-pattern reading

### Behavioral Patterns
- Users scan, not read
- Familiarity breeds trust
- Immediate feedback essential
- Error prevention > recovery

### Emotional Design
- Delight in micro-interactions
- Personality through copy
- Trust through transparency
- Satisfaction through completion

## Interaction Patterns

### Navigation

| Pattern | Use Case |
|---------|----------|
| Top nav | Simple sites, < 7 items |
| Side nav | Complex apps, many sections |
| Bottom nav | Mobile apps, < 5 items |
| Hamburger | Mobile, space-constrained |
| Tabs | Related content sections |
| Breadcrumbs | Deep hierarchies |

### Forms

| Pattern | Benefit |
|---------|---------|
| Single column | Faster completion |
| Inline validation | Immediate feedback |
| Field grouping | Logical flow |
| Smart defaults | Reduced effort |
| Progress indicator | Clear expectations |
| Error summary | Easy correction |

### Data Display

| Pattern | Use Case |
|---------|----------|
| Cards | Browsable content |
| Tables | Comparable data |
| Lists | Sequential items |
| Timeline | Chronological events |
| Dashboard | At-a-glance metrics |
| Search + filters | Large datasets |

### Feedback

| Pattern | Use Case |
|---------|----------|
| Toast | Non-blocking info |
| Modal | Blocking/important |
| Inline | Contextual feedback |
| Banner | Global notifications |
| Progress | Long operations |
| Skeleton | Loading states |

## Mobile UX Considerations

### Touch Targets
- Minimum 44x44px
- 8px spacing between targets
- Thumb-friendly placement
- Bottom sheet for actions

### Gestures
- Tap: Primary action
- Swipe: Navigate/reveal
- Long press: Secondary actions
- Pinch: Zoom (media)

### Mobile Patterns
- Bottom navigation
- Pull to refresh
- Infinite scroll (carefully)
- Full-screen modals
- Native keyboard handling

## Accessibility (UX Perspective)

### Inclusive Design
- Design for extremes, works for all
- Consider temporary disabilities
- Support different input methods
- Test with real users

### Key Considerations

| Aspect | UX Approach |
|--------|-------------|
| Vision | High contrast, scalable text, alt text |
| Motor | Large targets, keyboard nav, no timing |
| Cognitive | Simple language, clear structure |
| Hearing | Captions, visual indicators |

### Testing
- Screen reader walkthrough
- Keyboard-only navigation
- Color blindness simulation
- Reduced motion mode

## Design Critique Framework

### Criteria

| Aspect | Questions |
|--------|-----------|
| Usability | Can users complete tasks? |
| Clarity | Is purpose immediately clear? |
| Consistency | Follows established patterns? |
| Hierarchy | Most important stands out? |
| Feedback | Users know system state? |
| Error handling | Recoverable mistakes? |
| Accessibility | Works for everyone? |

### Constructive Feedback

**Instead of:** "This is confusing"
**Say:** "Users might not understand X because Y. Consider Z."

**Instead of:** "I don't like this"
**Say:** "This pattern may cause issues because... Alternative approach..."

## Common UX Patterns

### Onboarding
1. Progressive disclosure
2. Contextual tips
3. Interactive tutorials
4. Skip option always
5. Revisitable help

### Empty States
1. Explain what goes here
2. Guide to first action
3. Show example content
4. Make it delightful

### Loading States
1. Skeleton screens > spinners
2. Progress indicators for long ops
3. Optimistic UI when safe
4. Background loading when possible

### Error States
1. Explain what happened
2. Suggest how to fix
3. Provide recovery action
4. Don't blame user

### Success States
1. Confirm action completed
2. Show next steps
3. Celebrate achievements
4. Enable sharing if appropriate

## Content Strategy

### Microcopy
- Be concise
- Use active voice
- Avoid jargon
- Be human
- Guide action

### Labels & Instructions
- Clear and specific
- Explain why (if needed)
- Example formats
- Required vs optional

### Error Messages
- Specific problem
- How to fix
- No technical jargon
- Apologetic when appropriate

## Metrics That Matter

### Engagement
- Time on task
- Completion rate
- Return visits
- Feature adoption

### Satisfaction
- NPS score
- CSAT surveys
- Support tickets
- User feedback

### Performance
- Task success rate
- Error rate
- Time to first action
- Abandonment points

## Design Review Checklist

### Functionality
- [ ] Primary task achievable
- [ ] Edge cases handled
- [ ] Error states designed
- [ ] Loading states designed

### Usability
- [ ] Clear visual hierarchy
- [ ] Obvious next actions
- [ ] Consistent with patterns
- [ ] Minimal cognitive load

### Accessibility
- [ ] Keyboard navigable
- [ ] Screen reader tested
- [ ] Color contrast sufficient
- [ ] Touch targets sized

### Polish
- [ ] Micro-interactions smooth
- [ ] Copy clear and helpful
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints
- [ ] Performance acceptable

## See Also

- Project-specific UX skills for detailed requirements
- `.claude/skills/generic-design-system/` - Visual design
- `CLAUDE.md` - Project constraints
