   409→### When to Ask

   416→### When to Proceed

   425→## Brand Guidelines

   448→
   449→## Workspace Organization Rules
   450→

   452→
   453→### Single Source of Truth Principle
   454→

   456→

# CLAUDE.md - Lazy Golfing Platform

> AI assistant guidelines for the LazyGolfing monorepo

**Last Updated:** 2025-12-06

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Quick Reference](#quick-reference) - Commands, endpoints, test snippets
2. [Repository Structure](#repository-structure) - Directory layout, documentation hierarchy
3. [Development Setup](#development-setup) - Environment, first-time setup
4. [Critical Development Priorities](#-critical-development-priorities) - **READ THIS FIRST**
5. [Golden Rules](#golden-rules-never-break) - Non-negotiable practices
6. [Architecture Overview](#architecture-overview) - Data flow, modules, models
7. [Environment Variables](#environment-variables) - Configuration
8. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting-quick-reference) - Common issues
9. [Documentation Links](#documentation-links) - All docs in one place
10. [AI Working Principles](#ai-working-principles) - How Claude should work
11. [Brand Guidelines](#brand-guidelines) - Voice, colors, theme
12. [Workspace Organization](#workspace-organization-rules) - Standards

---

## Auto-Discovered Context

Claude Code automatically loads CLAUDE.md files based on working directory:

| Working In | CLAUDE.md Files Loaded |
|------------|----------------------|
| `/` (root) | This file only |
| `/lzg.home/` | This file + `lzg.home/CLAUDE.md` |
| `/lzg.home/src/components/` | This file + `lzg.home/CLAUDE.md` + `components/CLAUDE.md` |
| `/lzg.backend/` | This file + `lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md` |
| `/lzg.backend/src/auth/` | This file + `lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md` + `auth/CLAUDE.md` |

**Detailed guides imported as needed:**
- Completion checklists: `@docs/guidelines/COMPLETION_CHECKLISTS.md`
- Naming conventions: `@docs/guidelines/NAMING_CONVENTIONS.md`

---

## Quick Reference

### Most-Used Commands

```bash
# Frontend (lzg.home/)
npm run dev                     # Development server (localhost:3000)
npm run build                   # Production build (static export)
npm run lint                    # ESLint checks
npm run build:data              # CSV → JSON (legacy /alpha site)
npm run check:data              # Verify JSON is current

# Backend (lzg.backend/) - Run on VM (192.168.1.40)
npm run start:dev               # Development server with hot-reload
npm run test                    # Unit tests
npx prisma generate             # REQUIRED after schema.prisma changes
npx prisma migrate dev --name <name>  # Create migration
npx prisma studio               # Database GUI (192.168.1.40:5555)

# Production
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 100     # View backend logs
pm2 restart lg-api              # Restart after deploy
```

### Key API Endpoints

| Endpoint | Auth | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| `GET /auth/google` | None | Start OAuth flow |
| `GET /auth/session` | None | Check login status |
| `POST /auth/logout` | CSRF | Clear session |
| `GET /v1/me` | JWT+CSRF | Get user profile |
| `PUT /v1/me` | JWT+CSRF | Update profile |
| `GET /v1/rounds` | JWT+CSRF | List rounds (paginated) |
| `POST /v1/rounds` | JWT+CSRF | Create round |
| `GET /v1/courses/search` | None | Search courses |

See [API Reference](./docs/architecture/API_REFERENCE.md) for complete endpoint documentation.

### Test Commands

```bash
# Test API connectivity
curl https://api.lazygolfing.com/
curl https://api.lazygolfing.com/auth/session
```

---

## Repository Structure

```
Lazy Golfing/
├── CLAUDE.md                    # This file - root AI instructions
├── docs/                        # Centralized documentation
│   ├── plans/                   # Product roadmap, feature roadmap
│   ├── todo/                    # Task tracking
│   ├── technical-debt/          # Code quality issues
│   ├── design/                  # Design system documentation
│   └── architecture/            # System design docs
├── media/                       # Brand assets (not deployed)
│   ├── logos/                   # Official logos and variants
│   ├── kikman/                  # Kikman sub-brand assets
│   └── archive/                 # Historical planning screenshots
├── lzg.home/                    # Frontend (Next.js 15 + React 19)
│   ├── CLAUDE.md               # Frontend-specific AI instructions
│   ├── src/app/                # Next.js App Router pages
│   ├── src/components/         # React components + shadcn/ui
│   ├── src/lib/theme.ts        # Centralized color config
│   └── public/alpha/           # Legacy static site (preserved)
└── lzg.backend/                 # Backend (NestJS API)
    ├── CLAUDE.md               # Backend-specific AI instructions
    └── README.md               # Backend documentation
```

### Documentation Hierarchy

| File | Purpose | When to Read |
|------|---------|--------------|
| `/CLAUDE.md` | Quick reference, critical rules | Always |
| `/docs/` | Detailed documentation | Planning, research |
| `/lzg.home/CLAUDE.md` | Next.js/React patterns | Working on frontend |
| `/lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md` | API/database details | Working on backend |

---

## Development Setup

### Environment

- **Code editing:** Windows machine (this repo)
- **Backend runtime:** Ubuntu VM at `192.168.1.40`
- **Production API:** https://api.lazygolfing.com
- **Production Frontend:** https://lazygolfing.com

### First-Time Setup

```bash
# Frontend
cd lzg.home && npm ci && npm run build:data

# Backend (on VM)
cd lzg.backend && npm ci
cp .env.example .env  # Configure environment
npx prisma generate
npx prisma migrate dev
npm run start:dev
```

---

## ⚠️ Critical Development Priorities

> **These principles are NON-NEGOTIABLE.** Every decision, feature, and line of code must align with these priorities. When in conflict, priorities higher in the list take precedence.

### Priority Hierarchy (In Order)

#### 1. 🔒 SECURITY (Non-Negotiable)
- **Defense in depth** - Multiple layers of protection, never rely on single control
- **Never trust user input** - Validate, sanitize, escape at every boundary
- **Secure by default** - New features must be secure out of the box
- **Zero tolerance for secrets in code** - No API keys, tokens, passwords in source
- **OWASP Top 10 awareness** - Actively prevent XSS, injection, CSRF, auth issues
- **Data minimization** - Only collect/store what's absolutely necessary
- **Audit logging** - Track security-relevant actions for forensics

#### 2. 🏗️ FOUNDATIONAL (Build Right)
- **Solid architecture first** - No shortcuts that create tech debt
- **Single source of truth** - One authoritative location for every piece of data
- **Clean integration** - New features must integrate with existing systems cleanly
- **Backward compatibility** - Don't break existing functionality without migration path
- **Database integrity** - Proper constraints, indexes, foreign keys, cascades

#### 3. 🛡️ STABILITY (Never Crash)
- **Zero tolerance for crashes** - Comprehensive error handling everywhere
- **No data loss** - Every user action either succeeds completely or fails safely
- **Graceful degradation** - Features fail gracefully when dependencies unavailable
- **Test critical paths** - Auth, payments, data mutations must have test coverage
- **Defensive programming** - Assume inputs are malformed, APIs are down, state is corrupted

#### 4. ✨ UX/UI (User Delight)
- **Speed is a feature** - Target <100ms perceived interaction latency
- **Mobile-first responsive** - Design for mobile, enhance for desktop
- **Intuitive flows** - Users should never wonder "what do I do next?"
- **Accessibility required** - WCAG 2.1 AA compliance, keyboard nav, screen readers
- **Consistent design language** - Miami Vice theme applied uniformly
- **Feedback on every action** - Loading states, success messages, error explanations
- **Forgiveness** - Easy undo, confirmation for destructive actions

#### 5. 🎯 LEAN (Ship Minimal)
- **MVP first** - Ship the minimum viable solution, iterate based on feedback
- **No feature creep** - If it's not solving a real user problem, don't build it
- **Question every dependency** - Each package adds risk, size, and maintenance burden
- **Simpler is better** - Fewer abstractions, less code, easier maintenance
- **Delete before adding** - Remove unused code before adding new features

#### 6. 🧹 CLEAN (Readable Code)
- **Readable > clever** - Anyone should understand code without deep context
- **Consistent naming** - Follow established conventions throughout
- **Single responsibility** - Each function/component does one thing well
- **No dead code** - Remove unused imports, functions, files immediately
- **Self-documenting** - Code explains "what", comments explain "why"

#### 7. 📈 RETENTION (Bring Users Back)
- **Valuable data** - Help users track stats they can't get elsewhere
- **Progress visualization** - Show improvement over time
- **Social features** - Playing partners, comparisons, friendly competition
- **Low friction** - One-tap round logging, smart defaults, remember preferences
- **Notifications done right** - Valuable reminders, never spam

#### 8. ⚡ EFFICIENT + OPTIMIZED (Performance)
- **Measure first** - Profile before optimizing, no premature optimization
- **Performance budgets** - Set and enforce bundle size limits
- **Core Web Vitals** - LCP < 2.5s, FID < 100ms, CLS < 0.1
- **Database optimization** - Proper indexes, query analysis, N+1 prevention
- **Caching strategy** - Cache at every layer (CDN, API, database, client)
- **Lazy loading** - Load code and data only when needed

#### 9. 🔄 UPDATED + KNOWLEDGEABLE (Stay Current)
- **Current dependencies** - Regular updates, security patches applied promptly
- **Modern patterns** - Use current best practices, avoid deprecated approaches
- **Understand before changing** - Read existing code thoroughly before modifying
- **Know the "why"** - Understand reasoning behind architectural decisions
- **Documentation current** - CLAUDE.md and docs updated with every change

#### 10. 🏌️ INDUSTRY-FAMILIAR (Golf Domain Expertise)
- **Handicap systems** - GHIN, WHS, course/slope ratings, handicap index calculation
- **Golf terminology** - Correct use of GIR, FIR, putts per round, scoring terms
- **Course data** - Par, rating, slope, tee boxes, layouts
- **Tournament formats** - Stroke play, match play, Stableford, scramble
- **Competitor awareness** - Know what GHIN, Golf Pad, 18Birdies offer

---

### Golden Rules (Never Break)

1. **CSV is source of truth** - Never edit JSON in `/data/` directly
2. **Always run `npx prisma generate`** after schema.prisma changes
3. **Protected routes need `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)`**
4. **Admin routes need `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)`**
5. **All inputs validated** via class-validator DTOs
6. **Never commit secrets** - Check `.env`, API keys, tokens
7. **Test before deploy** - No untested code reaches production
8. **Mobile-first** - Every feature must work on mobile devices

### Before Completing ANY Task

**Quick checklist** (see `@docs/guidelines/COMPLETION_CHECKLISTS.md` for detailed checklists):

- [ ] **Security:** No vulnerabilities (injection, XSS, auth bypass, secrets)
- [ ] **Stability:** Error handling for all failure modes
- [ ] **UX:** Works on mobile, accessible, has loading/error states
- [ ] **Lean:** No unnecessary code, dependencies, or features
- [ ] **Clean:** Follows existing patterns, readable, no dead code
- [ ] **Tests:** `npm run test` (backend) / `npm run build` (frontend) pass
- [ ] **Docs:** CLAUDE.md updated if patterns changed

### CSV Data Workflow

```bash
# 1. Edit CSV file
vim golfers/golfers.csv

# 2. Regenerate JSON
npm run build:data

# 3. Verify
npm run check:data

# 4. Commit BOTH files
git add golfers/golfers.csv data/golfers.json
git commit -m "data(golfers): update..."
```

---

## Architecture Overview

### Frontend Data Flow

```
CSV files (source of truth)     JSON (build artifact)       Browser
golfers/golfers.csv      -->    data/golfers.json     -->   fetch + render
leagues/leagues.csv      -->    data/leagues.json
tours/tours.csv          -->    data/tours.json
```

### Backend Module Structure

```
src/
├── auth/           # Google OAuth + JWT (cookie: lg_jwt)
├── profile/        # GET/PUT /v1/me
├── rounds/         # CRUD /v1/rounds
├── courses/        # Course management
├── prisma/         # Database service (global module)
└── audit-log/      # Action logging
```

### Database Models

| Model | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `User` | OAuth identity (Google), 1:1 with Player |
| `Player` | Golf profile (handicap, GHIN), has many Rounds |
| `Round` | Golf rounds with stats |
| `Course` | Course data with API integration |
| `RoundLink` | Playing partner linking |
| `Role/UserRole` | RBAC |
| `AuditLog` | Action tracking |

### Authentication Flow

1. Frontend → `GET /auth/google` → Google consent
2. Google → Backend callback with code
3. Backend creates/updates User + Player
4. Backend signs JWT, sets `lg_jwt` HttpOnly cookie
5. Backend redirects to frontend success page
6. Frontend calls `/auth/session` to confirm

---

```ini
NODE_ENV=development|production
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/lazygolfing
JWT_SECRET=<openssl rand -base64 32>

GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=<from-google-console>
GOOGLE_CALLBACK_URL=http://localhost:3000/auth/google/redirect
```

### CORS Origins (src/main.ts)

```
https://lazygolfing.com
https://www.lazygolfing.com
https://lazygolfing-com.pages.dev
```

For local development, add your origin (e.g., `http://localhost:8080`).

---

## Troubleshooting Quick Reference

| Symptom | Cause | Solution |
|---------|-------|----------|
| Table not rendering | JSON not loaded | Check Network tab for 404 |
| Stale data | Browser cache | Hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R) |
| CSV changes not showing | Forgot build | `npm run build:data` |
| `Prisma Client not found` | Schema changed | `npx prisma generate` |
| 401 Unauthorized | Invalid JWT | Re-login |
| CORS error | Origin not allowed | Add to `main.ts` allowlist |

## Documentation Links

### Development Resources

## AI Working Principles

### Approach

1. **Read first, act later** - Understand existing code before modifying
2. **Follow patterns** - Match the style of surrounding code
3. **Test before completing** - Run tests, verify manually
4. **Security first** - Never expose secrets, validate inputs

### When to Ask

- Requirements are ambiguous
- Security implications unclear
- Breaking changes needed
- Performance trade-offs exist

### When to Proceed

- Implementation matches existing patterns
- Non-breaking additions
- Bug fixes with clear root cause
- Refactoring that preserves behavior

## Workspace Organization Rules

These rules apply across all repositories in the Lazy Golfing workspace.

### Single Source of Truth Principle

Every piece of data/configuration should have ONE authoritative source:

| What | Source of Truth | Don't Duplicate In |
|------|----------------|-------------------|
| Brand colors | `lzg.home/src/lib/theme.ts` | Individual CSS files |
| SEO metadata | `lzg.home/src/lib/metadata.ts` | Individual page files |
| API types | `lzg.backend/src/` DTOs | Frontend type files |
| CSV data | `public/alpha/*.csv` files | JSON directly |

### Static Asset Organization

All static assets MUST be organized in appropriate folders:

```
public/
├── favicons/     # All favicon files
├── images/       # Logos, OG images, photos
├── patterns/     # SVG patterns, textures
└── fonts/        # Custom fonts (if any)
```

**Never place files loose in public/ root** (except site.webmanifest, robots.txt).

### Social Sharing Standards

| Standard | Specification |
|----------|--------------|
| OG Image Size | 1200x630 pixels (1.91:1 ratio) |
| OG Title | All lowercase for brand ("lazy golfing") |
| OG Description | Tagline: "lazy golf for lazy golfers!" |
| Safe Zone | Center 800x400 area for cropping |

### Documentation Hierarchy

```
/CLAUDE.md                    # Quick reference, critical rules
/docs/                        # Detailed documentation
  ├── plans/                  # Roadmaps, feature lists
  ├── research/               # Market research, competitors
  ├── technical-debt/         # Code quality tracking
  └── architecture/           # System design
/lzg.home/CLAUDE.md          # Frontend-specific rules
/lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md       # Backend-specific rules
```

   527→

   533→
   534→## ⚠️ Critical Priorities (Backend API)
   535→
   536→> **See [/CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md) for the complete priority hierarchy.** This section covers backend-specific applications of those principles.
   537→
   538→### Backend Priority Applications
   539→
   540→| Priority | Backend Implementation |
   541→|----------|----------------------|
   542→| 🔒 **Security** | JWT + CSRF on all mutations, input validation via DTOs, parameterized queries (Prisma), rate limiting, never log secrets, RBAC with AdminGuard |
   543→| 🏗️ **Foundational** | Prisma as single DB interface, NestJS module structure, consistent error responses, proper database constraints/indexes |
   544→| 🛡️ **Stability** | Try-catch on all async, transaction rollbacks, connection pooling, graceful shutdown, health checks |
   545→| ✨ **UX/UI** | Fast API responses (<200ms), meaningful error messages, proper HTTP status codes, pagination on lists |
   546→| 🎯 **Lean** | No unused endpoints, minimal dependencies, efficient queries (avoid N+1), lazy load relations |
   547→| 🧹 **Clean** | Service/Controller separation, DTOs for all inputs, consistent naming, typed Prisma queries |
   548→| ⚡ **Performance** | Database indexes on query fields, query optimization, connection pooling, response caching where safe |
   549→| 📊 **Observable** | Audit logging for mutations, structured error logging, PM2 monitoring |
   550→
   551→### Backend Checklist (Every Change)
   552→
   553→**Quick checklist** (see `@../docs/guidelines/COMPLETION_CHECKLISTS.md` for detailed checklists):
   554→
   555→- [ ] Protected routes have `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)`
   556→- [ ] Admin routes have `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)`
   557→- [ ] All inputs validated via class-validator DTOs
   558→- [ ] User can only access their own data (no IDOR)
   559→- [ ] No secrets in code or logs
   560→- [ ] `npm run test` passes
   561→- [ ] `npx prisma generate` run after schema changes
   562→
   563→### Security Non-Negotiables
   564→
   565→```typescript
   566→// ✅ ALWAYS: Protected mutation endpoint
   567→@Post()
   568→@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)
   569→async create(@Body() dto: CreateDto, @CurrentUser() user: User) {
   570→  // Validate ownership in service layer
   571→}
   572→
   573→// ✅ ALWAYS: Admin-only endpoint
   574→@Delete(':id')
   575→@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)
   576→async delete(@Param('id') id: string) { }
   577→
   578→// ❌ NEVER: Unprotected mutation
   579→@Post()
   580→async create(@Body() dto: CreateDto) { }  // NO GUARDS = SECURITY HOLE
   581→```

   673→## Task Completion Workflow
   674→
   675→**MANDATORY:** After completing any task or feature, follow this workflow.
   676→
   677→### 1. Verify Build & Tests
   678→```bash
   679→npm run build
   680→npm run test
   681→```
   682→
   683→### 2. Commit Changes
   684→```bash
   685→git status
   686→git add -A
   687→git commit -m "<type>(<scope>): <description>"
   688→```
   689→
   690→**Commit types:** `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `style`, `refactor`, `perf`, `test`, `chore`
   691→
   692→### 3. Push to Main
   693→```bash
   694→git push origin main
   695→```
   696→
   697→### 4. Build AND Restart API
   698→```bash
   699→npm run build && pm2 restart lg-api
   700→pm2 logs lg-api --lines 20
   701→```
   702→
   703→**⚠️ CRITICAL:** You MUST run `npm run build` before `pm2 restart`:
   704→- PM2 runs compiled JavaScript from `dist/` folder
   705→- Without building, PM2 restarts with OLD compiled code
   706→- Your source changes will NOT take effect without building first
   707→
   708→**Also check for changes in:**
   709→- `/home/lzg/lazygolfing-frontend/lzg.home/` - Frontend repository

   878→## Database Schema
   879→
   880→### Model Relationships
   881→
   882→```
   883→User (OAuth identity)
   884→  ├── 1:1 Player (golf profile)
   885→  │        └── 1:N Round
   886→  │                  └── N:1 Course (optional)
   887→  ├── 1:N Notification (user notifications)
   888→  ├── 1:N GroupInvite (sent/received)
   889→  ├── 1:N SideGameInvite (sent/received)
   890→  ├── 1:N Friendship (requester/addressee)
   891→  └── 1:N Challenge (creator/receiver)
   892→
   893→Role ←─ N:N ─→ User (via UserRole)
   894→AuditLog (standalone)
   895→```
   896→
   897→### Key Fields
   898→
   899→| Model | Key Fields |
   900→|-------|------------|
   901→| User | email, provider, providerId, avatarUrl, lastLoginAt |
   902→| Player | firstName, lastName, ghinNumber, handicap |
   903→| Round | strokes, front9, back9, girPct, firPct, totalPutts, birdies, pars, bogeys |
   904→| Course | name, city, state, totalPar, courseRating, slopeRating, apiCourseId |
   905→| Notification | type, title, userId, isRead, actionUrl, resourceId |
   906→| GroupInvite | groupId, inviteeId, inviterId, status, message |
   907→| SideGameInvite | gameId, inviteeId, inviterId, status, message |
   908→| Friendship | requesterId, addresseeId, status |
   909→| Challenge | challengerId, challengedId, status, startDate, endDate |
   910→
   911→---
   912→
   913→## Authentication Flow

   915→```

# CLAUDE.md - Backend (lzg.backend)

> AI assistant guidelines for the NestJS API backend

**Last Updated:** 2025-12-11

---

## ⚠️ Critical Priorities (Backend API)

> **See [/CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md) for the complete priority hierarchy.** This section covers backend-specific applications of those principles.

### Backend Priority Applications

| Priority | Backend Implementation |
|----------|----------------------|
| 🔒 **Security** | JWT + CSRF on all mutations, input validation via DTOs, parameterized queries (Prisma), rate limiting, never log secrets, RBAC with AdminGuard |
| 🏗️ **Foundational** | Prisma as single DB interface, NestJS module structure, consistent error responses, proper database constraints/indexes |
| 🛡️ **Stability** | Try-catch on all async, transaction rollbacks, connection pooling, graceful shutdown, health checks |
| ✨ **UX/UI** | Fast API responses (<200ms), meaningful error messages, proper HTTP status codes, pagination on lists |
| 🎯 **Lean** | No unused endpoints, minimal dependencies, efficient queries (avoid N+1), lazy load relations |
| 🧹 **Clean** | Service/Controller separation, DTOs for all inputs, consistent naming, typed Prisma queries |
| ⚡ **Performance** | Database indexes on query fields, query optimization, connection pooling, response caching where safe |
| 📊 **Observable** | Audit logging for mutations, structured error logging, PM2 monitoring |

### Backend Checklist (Every Change)

**Quick checklist** (see `@../docs/guidelines/COMPLETION_CHECKLISTS.md` for detailed checklists):

- [ ] Protected routes have `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)`
- [ ] Admin routes have `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)`
- [ ] All inputs validated via class-validator DTOs
- [ ] User can only access their own data (no IDOR)
- [ ] No secrets in code or logs
- [ ] `npm run test` passes
- [ ] `npx prisma generate` run after schema changes

### Security Non-Negotiables

```typescript
// ✅ ALWAYS: Protected mutation endpoint
@Post()
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)
async create(@Body() dto: CreateDto, @CurrentUser() user: User) {
  // Validate ownership in service layer
}

// ✅ ALWAYS: Admin-only endpoint
@Delete(':id')
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)
async delete(@Param('id') id: string) { }

// ❌ NEVER: Unprotected mutation
@Post()
async create(@Body() dto: CreateDto) { }  // NO GUARDS = SECURITY HOLE
```

## Quick Reference

### Environment

- **Code editing:** Windows machine (this repo)
- **Runtime:** Ubuntu VM at `192.168.1.40`
- **Production:** https://api.lazygolfing.com

### VM Folder Structure (IMPORTANT)

The folder names differ between local development and the VM:

| Local (Windows) | VM Path | Contents |
|-----------------|---------|----------|
| `lzg.backend/` | `~/lazygolfing-backend/api/` | NestJS API source code |
| `lzg.home/` | `~/lazygolfing-frontend/` | Next.js frontend (if deployed) |

**Full VM structure:**
```
/home/lzg/
├── lazygolfing-backend/
│   ├── api/                    # ← THIS IS WHERE BACKEND CODE LIVES
│   │   ├── src/               # NestJS source
│   │   ├── dist/              # Compiled output
│   │   ├── prisma/            # Database schema
│   │   ├── ecosystem.config.js # PM2 configuration
│   │   └── package.json
│   ├── bin/                   # Helper scripts
│   └── lg/                    # Additional resources
└── lazygolfing-frontend/      # Frontend deployment (if any)
```

**SSH to VM:**
```bash
ssh lzg@192.168.1.40
cd ~/lazygolfing-backend/api   # Navigate to backend
```

### Essential Commands (Run on VM)

```bash
# Development
npm run start:dev               # Hot-reload server
npm run start:debug             # Debug mode (port 9229)
npm run test                    # Unit tests
npm run lint                    # ESLint

# Database
npx prisma generate             # REQUIRED after schema changes
npx prisma migrate dev --name <name>  # Create migration
npx prisma studio               # GUI at 192.168.1.40:5555

# Production
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 100     # View logs
npm run build && pm2 restart lg-api  # MUST build before restart!
```

---

## Task Completion Workflow

**MANDATORY:** After completing any task or feature, follow this workflow.

### 1. Verify Build & Tests
```bash
npm run build
npm run test
```

### 2. Commit Changes
```bash
git status
git add -A
git commit -m "<type>(<scope>): <description>"
```

**Commit types:** `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `style`, `refactor`, `perf`, `test`, `chore`

### 3. Push to Main
```bash
git push origin main
```

### 4. Build AND Restart API
```bash
npm run build && pm2 restart lg-api
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 20
```

**⚠️ CRITICAL:** You MUST run `npm run build` before `pm2 restart`:
- PM2 runs compiled JavaScript from `dist/` folder
- Without building, PM2 restarts with OLD compiled code
- Your source changes will NOT take effect without building first

**Also check for changes in:**
- `/home/lzg/lazygolfing-frontend/lzg.home/` - Frontend repository
- `/home/lzg/` - Root docs and CLAUDE.md files

---

## API Endpoints

### Authentication (Public)

| GET | `/auth/session` | None | Check login status |
| POST | `/auth/logout` | CSRF | Clear session |

### Profile (Requires JWT + CSRF)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v1/me` | Get user + player profile |
| PUT | `/v1/me` | Update player profile |
| GET | `/v1/me/stats` | Get user statistics |
| GET | `/v1/me/playing-partners` | Get aggregated partners |
| GET | `/v1/me/export` | GDPR data export |
| DELETE | `/v1/me` | Delete account (cascade) |

### Rounds (Requires JWT + CSRF)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v1/rounds` | List player's rounds (paginated) |
| POST | `/v1/rounds` | Create round |
| PUT | `/v1/rounds/:id` | Update round |
| DELETE | `/v1/rounds/:id` | Soft delete round |
| POST | `/v1/rounds/import-csv` | Bulk import rounds |

| POST | `/v1/rounds/:id/link/:targetId` | Link two rounds |
| GET | `/v1/rounds/:id/linked` | Get linked rounds |

### Courses

| Method | Path | Guard | Description |
|--------|------|-------|-------------|
| GET | `/v1/courses/search` | None | Search by name (+ API) |
| GET | `/v1/courses/nearby` | None | GPS-based search |
| GET | `/v1/courses` | None | List all courses |
| GET | `/v1/courses/:id` | None | Course details + stats |
| POST | `/v1/courses` | JWT+CSRF | Create course |
| POST | `/v1/courses/import` | JWT+CSRF | Import from Golf API |
| PUT | `/v1/courses/:id` | JWT+CSRF+Admin | Update course |
| DELETE | `/v1/courses/:id` | JWT+CSRF+Admin | Delete course |

### Friends (Requires JWT + CSRF)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v1/friends` | Get accepted friends |
| GET | `/v1/friends/requests` | Get pending requests received |
| GET | `/v1/friends/requests/sent` | Get pending requests sent |

| POST | `/v1/challenges/:id/decline` | Decline challenge |
| DELETE | `/v1/challenges/:id` | Cancel challenge |

### Notifications (Requires JWT + CSRF)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|

| GET | `/v1/notifications/count` | Get unread count + breakdown |

### Group Invites (Requires JWT + CSRF)

---

## Database Schema

### Model Relationships

```
User (OAuth identity)
  ├── 1:1 Player (golf profile)
  │        └── 1:N Round
  │                  └── N:1 Course (optional)
  ├── 1:N Notification (user notifications)
  ├── 1:N GroupInvite (sent/received)
  ├── 1:N SideGameInvite (sent/received)
  ├── 1:N Friendship (requester/addressee)
  └── 1:N Challenge (creator/receiver)

Role ←─ N:N ─→ User (via UserRole)
AuditLog (standalone)
```

### Key Fields

| Model | Key Fields |
|-------|------------|
| User | email, provider, providerId, avatarUrl, lastLoginAt |
| Player | firstName, lastName, ghinNumber, handicap |
| Round | strokes, front9, back9, girPct, firPct, totalPutts, birdies, pars, bogeys |
| Course | name, city, state, totalPar, courseRating, slopeRating, apiCourseId |
| Notification | type, title, userId, isRead, actionUrl, resourceId |
| GroupInvite | groupId, inviteeId, inviterId, status, message |
| SideGameInvite | gameId, inviteeId, inviterId, status, message |
| Friendship | requesterId, addresseeId, status |
| Challenge | challengerId, challengedId, status, startDate, endDate |

---

## Authentication Flow

```
1. Frontend → GET /auth/google
2. Redirect to Google consent
3. Google → /auth/google/redirect with code
4. GoogleStrategy exchanges code for user info
5. auth.service.findOrCreateUser() → User + Player
6. Sign JWT, set lg_jwt cookie (HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=None, 7 days)
7. Redirect to frontend success page
```

### Cookie Config

```typescript
{
  httpOnly: true,        // XSS protection
  secure: true,          // HTTPS only (production)
  sameSite: 'none',      // Cross-origin allowed
  maxAge: 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000,  // 7 days
}
```

### Protecting Routes

```typescript
// Basic JWT protection
@Get('protected-route')
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)
async myRoute(@CurrentUser() user: any) {
  // user contains JWT payload: { sub: userId, email }
}

// Admin-only route
@Put('admin-route')
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)
async adminOnly(@CurrentUser() user: any) {
  // Only users with 'admin' role can access
}
```

---

## CSRF Protection

### Double-Submit Cookie Pattern

Protected endpoints require the `X-CSRF-Token` header to match the `csrf_token` cookie.

**Cookie:** Set at login alongside `lg_jwt`
**Header:** `X-CSRF-Token: <token-value>`
**Validation:** Constant-time comparison (prevents timing attacks)

```typescript
// Frontend request example
fetch('/v1/me', {
  credentials: 'include',  // Include cookies
  headers: {
    'X-CSRF-Token': getCookie('csrf_token')
  }
});
```

**Skip for:** GET, HEAD, OPTIONS requests

---

## Rate Limiting

Global rate limiting via `@nestjs/throttler`:

| Category | Limit | Window |
|----------|-------|--------|
| Default | 120 requests | 1 minute |
| Auth endpoints | 20 requests | 1 minute |
| Session check | 20 requests | 1 minute |

**Response when exceeded:** `429 Too Many Requests`

---

  @IsString()

  1259→*This documentation covers the NestJS 11 backend for the Lazy Golfing platform.*
  1260→# CLAUDE.md - Auth Module
  1261→
  1262→> Context for working in `lzg.backend/src/auth/`
  1263→
  1264→---
  1265→
  1266→## Module Overview
  1267→
  1268→The auth module handles Google OAuth 2.0 authentication and JWT session management.
  1269→
  1270→```
  1271→auth/
  1272→├── auth.controller.ts      # /auth/google, /auth/session, /auth/logout
  1273→├── auth.service.ts         # findOrCreateUser, validateUser, ensureRoles
  1274→├── google.strategy.ts      # Passport Google OAuth strategy
  1275→├── jwt.strategy.ts         # Passport JWT validation strategy
  1276→├── jwt-auth.guard.ts       # @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)
  1277→├── admin.guard.ts          # @UseGuards(AdminGuard) - requires 'admin' role
  1278→├── csrf.guard.ts           # @UseGuards(CsrfGuard) - double-submit pattern
  1279→├── csrf.util.ts            # CSRF token generation utilities
  1280→└── current-user.decorator.ts  # @CurrentUser() parameter decorator
  1281→```
  1282→
  1283→---
  1284→
  1285→## Authentication Flow
  1286→
  1287→```
  1288→1. Frontend → GET /auth/google
  1289→2. Redirect to Google consent screen
  1290→3. Google → /auth/google/redirect with authorization code
  1291→4. GoogleStrategy exchanges code for user info
  1292→5. auth.service.findOrCreateUser() creates/updates User + Player
  1293→6. Sign JWT, set lg_jwt cookie (HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=None)
  1294→7. Set csrf_token cookie (readable by frontend)
  1295→8. Redirect to frontend success page
  1296→```
  1297→
  1298→---
  1299→
  1300→## Security Patterns
  1301→
  1302→### Guard Stack (Order Matters)
  1303→
  1304→```typescript
  1305→// Standard protected endpoint
  1306→@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)
  1307→async protectedRoute(@CurrentUser() user: User) { }
  1308→
  1309→// Admin-only endpoint
  1310→@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)
  1311→async adminRoute(@CurrentUser() user: User) { }
  1312→```
  1313→
  1314→**Guard execution order:**
  1315→1. `JwtAuthGuard` - Validates JWT from `lg_jwt` cookie
  1316→2. `CsrfGuard` - Validates `X-CSRF-Token` header matches `csrf_token` cookie
  1317→3. `AdminGuard` - Checks user has 'admin' role
  1318→
  1319→### Cookie Configuration
  1320→
  1321→```typescript
  1322→// JWT Cookie (HttpOnly - not accessible via JavaScript)
  1323→{
  1324→  httpOnly: true,
  1325→  secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production',
  1326→  sameSite: 'none',  // Required for cross-origin
  1327→  maxAge: 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000,  // 7 days
  1328→  path: '/',
  1329→}
  1330→
  1331→// CSRF Cookie (Readable by frontend JavaScript)
  1332→{
  1333→  httpOnly: false,  // Must be readable for header
  1334→  secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production',
  1335→  sameSite: 'none',
  1336→  maxAge: 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000,
  1337→  path: '/',
  1338→}
  1339→```
  1340→
  1341→### CSRF Protection
  1342→
  1343→**Double-Submit Cookie Pattern:**
  1344→
  1345→1. Server sets `csrf_token` cookie at login
  1346→2. Frontend reads cookie and sends as `X-CSRF-Token` header
  1347→3. Server compares header to cookie using constant-time comparison
  1348→4. Requests without matching token are rejected with 403
  1349→
  1350→```typescript
  1351→// Frontend request pattern
  1352→fetch('/v1/me', {
  1353→  method: 'PUT',
  1354→  credentials: 'include',  // Send cookies
  1355→  headers: {
  1356→    'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  1357→    'X-CSRF-Token': getCookie('csrf_token'),  // Must match cookie
  1358→  },
  1359→  body: JSON.stringify(data),
  1360→});
  1361→```
  1362→
  1363→**Skip CSRF for:** GET, HEAD, OPTIONS (safe methods)
  1364→
  1365→---
  1366→
  1367→## Adding New Protected Endpoints
  1368→
  1369→```typescript
  1370→import { UseGuards, Get, Controller } from '@nestjs/common';
  1371→import { JwtAuthGuard } from '../auth/jwt-auth.guard';
  1372→import { CsrfGuard } from '../auth/csrf.guard';
  1373→import { CurrentUser } from '../auth/current-user.decorator';
  1374→
  1375→@Controller('v1/my-feature')
  1376→export class MyFeatureController {
  1377→
  1378→  // Public endpoint (no guards)
  1379→  @Get('public')
  1380→  async publicEndpoint() {
  1381→    return { message: 'Anyone can access' };
  1382→  }
  1383→
  1384→  // Protected endpoint (logged-in users)
  1385→  @Get('protected')
  1386→  @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)
  1387→  async protectedEndpoint(@CurrentUser() user: User) {
  1388→    // user.id, user.email available
  1389→    return { userId: user.id };
  1390→  }
  1391→
  1392→  // Admin endpoint
  1393→  @Get('admin')
  1394→  @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)
  1395→  async adminEndpoint(@CurrentUser() user: User) {
  1396→    return { message: 'Admin only' };
  1397→  }
  1398→}
  1399→```
  1400→
  1401→---
  1402→
  1403→## Common Mistakes
  1404→
  1405→### Missing Guards
  1406→
  1407→```typescript
  1408→// ❌ WRONG: No guards on mutation
  1409→@Post()
  1410→async create(@Body() dto: CreateDto) { }
  1411→
  1412→// ✅ CORRECT: Always guard mutations
  1413→@Post()
  1414→@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)
  1415→async create(@Body() dto: CreateDto, @CurrentUser() user: User) { }
  1416→```
  1417→
  1418→### Wrong Guard Order
  1419→
  1420→```typescript
  1421→// ❌ WRONG: AdminGuard before JwtAuthGuard
  1422→@UseGuards(AdminGuard, JwtAuthGuard)
  1423→
  1424→// ✅ CORRECT: JWT first, then CSRF, then role-based
  1425→@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)
  1426→```
  1427→
  1428→### Exposing User Data
  1429→
  1430→```typescript
  1431→// ❌ WRONG: Returns all user fields including sensitive data
  1432→return user;
  1433→
  1434→// ✅ CORRECT: Select only needed fields
  1435→return {
  1436→  id: user.id,
  1437→  email: user.email,
  1438→  player: {
  1439→    firstName: user.player.firstName,
  1440→    lastName: user.player.lastName,
  1441→  },
  1442→};
  1443→```
  1444→
  1445→---
  1446→

  1448→
  1449→```bash
  1450→# Check session status (no auth needed)
  1451→curl https://api.lazygolfing.com/auth/session
  1452→
  1453→# Test with JWT cookie
  1454→curl -H "Cookie: lg_jwt=YOUR_TOKEN" \
  1455→     -H "X-CSRF-Token: YOUR_CSRF_TOKEN" \
  1456→     https://api.lazygolfing.com/v1/me
  1457→```

  1473→
  1474→## Before Committing Auth Changes
  1475→
  1476→- [ ] JWT secret is not hardcoded
  1477→- [ ] Cookies use secure settings in production
  1478→- [ ] CSRF validation uses constant-time comparison
  1479→- [ ] Error messages don't leak sensitive info
  1480→- [ ] Rate limiting applied to auth endpoints
  1481→- [ ] Tests cover happy path and auth failures

  name: string;

// 2. Add service method
async createThing(playerId: string, dto: CreateThingDto) {

    data: { ...dto, playerId },
  });
}

@Post()
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)
async create(
  @Body() dto: CreateThingDto,
  @CurrentUser() user: any,
) {

}

# CLAUDE.md - Auth Module

> Context for working in `lzg.backend/src/auth/`

## Module Overview

The auth module handles Google OAuth 2.0 authentication and JWT session management.

```
auth/
├── auth.controller.ts      # /auth/google, /auth/session, /auth/logout
├── auth.service.ts         # findOrCreateUser, validateUser, ensureRoles
├── google.strategy.ts      # Passport Google OAuth strategy
├── jwt.strategy.ts         # Passport JWT validation strategy
├── jwt-auth.guard.ts       # @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)
├── admin.guard.ts          # @UseGuards(AdminGuard) - requires 'admin' role
├── csrf.guard.ts           # @UseGuards(CsrfGuard) - double-submit pattern
├── csrf.util.ts            # CSRF token generation utilities
└── current-user.decorator.ts  # @CurrentUser() parameter decorator
```

---

## Authentication Flow

```
1. Frontend → GET /auth/google
2. Redirect to Google consent screen
3. Google → /auth/google/redirect with authorization code
4. GoogleStrategy exchanges code for user info
5. auth.service.findOrCreateUser() creates/updates User + Player
6. Sign JWT, set lg_jwt cookie (HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=None)
7. Set csrf_token cookie (readable by frontend)
8. Redirect to frontend success page
```

---

## Security Patterns

### Guard Stack (Order Matters)

```typescript
// Standard protected endpoint
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)
async protectedRoute(@CurrentUser() user: User) { }

// Admin-only endpoint
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)
async adminRoute(@CurrentUser() user: User) { }
```

**Guard execution order:**
1. `JwtAuthGuard` - Validates JWT from `lg_jwt` cookie
2. `CsrfGuard` - Validates `X-CSRF-Token` header matches `csrf_token` cookie
3. `AdminGuard` - Checks user has 'admin' role

### Cookie Configuration

```typescript
// JWT Cookie (HttpOnly - not accessible via JavaScript)
{
  httpOnly: true,
  secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production',
  sameSite: 'none',  // Required for cross-origin
  maxAge: 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000,  // 7 days
  path: '/',
}

// CSRF Cookie (Readable by frontend JavaScript)
{
  httpOnly: false,  // Must be readable for header
  secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production',
  sameSite: 'none',
  maxAge: 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000,
  path: '/',
}
```

### CSRF Protection

**Double-Submit Cookie Pattern:**

1. Server sets `csrf_token` cookie at login
2. Frontend reads cookie and sends as `X-CSRF-Token` header
3. Server compares header to cookie using constant-time comparison
4. Requests without matching token are rejected with 403

```typescript
// Frontend request pattern
fetch('/v1/me', {
  method: 'PUT',
  credentials: 'include',  // Send cookies
  headers: {
    'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    'X-CSRF-Token': getCookie('csrf_token'),  // Must match cookie
  },
  body: JSON.stringify(data),
});
```

**Skip CSRF for:** GET, HEAD, OPTIONS (safe methods)

---

## Adding New Protected Endpoints

```typescript
import { UseGuards, Get, Controller } from '@nestjs/common';
import { JwtAuthGuard } from '../auth/jwt-auth.guard';
import { CsrfGuard } from '../auth/csrf.guard';
import { CurrentUser } from '../auth/current-user.decorator';

@Controller('v1/my-feature')
export class MyFeatureController {

  // Public endpoint (no guards)
  @Get('public')
  async publicEndpoint() {
    return { message: 'Anyone can access' };
  }

  // Protected endpoint (logged-in users)
  @Get('protected')
  @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)
  async protectedEndpoint(@CurrentUser() user: User) {
    // user.id, user.email available
    return { userId: user.id };
  }

  // Admin endpoint
  @Get('admin')
  @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)
  async adminEndpoint(@CurrentUser() user: User) {
    return { message: 'Admin only' };
  }
}
```

---

## Common Mistakes

### Missing Guards

```typescript
// ❌ WRONG: No guards on mutation
@Post()
async create(@Body() dto: CreateDto) { }

// ✅ CORRECT: Always guard mutations
@Post()
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)
async create(@Body() dto: CreateDto, @CurrentUser() user: User) { }
```

### Wrong Guard Order

```typescript
// ❌ WRONG: AdminGuard before JwtAuthGuard
@UseGuards(AdminGuard, JwtAuthGuard)

// ✅ CORRECT: JWT first, then CSRF, then role-based
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)
```

### Exposing User Data

```typescript
// ❌ WRONG: Returns all user fields including sensitive data
return user;

// ✅ CORRECT: Select only needed fields
return {
  id: user.id,
  email: user.email,
  player: {
    firstName: user.player.firstName,
    lastName: user.player.lastName,
  },
};
```

---

## Testing Auth

```bash
# Check session status (no auth needed)
curl https://api.lazygolfing.com/auth/session

# Test with JWT cookie
curl -H "Cookie: lg_jwt=YOUR_TOKEN" \
     -H "X-CSRF-Token: YOUR_CSRF_TOKEN" \
     https://api.lazygolfing.com/v1/me
```

---

## Environment Variables

Required for this module:

```ini
JWT_SECRET=<openssl rand -base64 32>
GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=<from-google-console>.apps.googleusercontent.com
GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=GOCSPX-<from-google-console>
GOOGLE_CALLBACK_URL=http://localhost:3000/auth/google/redirect
```

---

## Before Committing Auth Changes

- [ ] JWT secret is not hardcoded
- [ ] Cookies use secure settings in production
- [ ] CSRF validation uses constant-time comparison
- [ ] Error messages don't leak sensitive info
- [ ] Rate limiting applied to auth endpoints
- [ ] Tests cover happy path and auth failures

---

*For full backend guidelines, see [lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md](../../CLAUDE.md)*

[API Integration](#api-integration) - Backend communication 10. [Routing](#routing) - App Router structure 11. [Deployment](#cloudflare-pages-deployment) - Build settings 12. [Development Workflow](#development-workflow) - Creating pages/components 13. [Styling](#styling-guidelines) - Tailwind patterns 14. [Legacy Site](#legacy-site-alpha) - /alpha/ preservation --- ## Quick Reference ### Tech Stack - **Framework:** Next.js 15 with React 19 - **Language:** TypeScript (strict mode) - **Styling:** Tailwind CSS 3.4 - **Components:** shadcn/ui (New York style) - **State:** Zustand (when needed) - **Theming:** next-themes (light/dark) ### Environment - **Code editing:** Windows machine - **Backend API (dev):** Ubuntu VM at `192.168.1.40:3000` - **Backend API (prod):** https://api.lazygolfing.com - **Production site:** https://lazygolfing.com (Cloudflare Pages) ### Essential Commands ```bash # Development npm install # Install dependencies npm run dev # Start dev server (localhost:3000) npm run build # Build for production (static export) npm run start # Serve production build locally npm run lint # ESLint checks # Legacy data pipeline (for /alpha site) npm run build:data # CSV → JSON conversion npm run check:data # Verify JSON is current ``` --- ## Task Completion Workflow **MANDATORY:** After completing any task or feature, follow this workflow. ### 1. Verify Build ```bash npm run build ``` ### 2. Commit Changes ```bash git status git add -A git commit -m "(): " ``` **Commit types:** `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `style`, `refactor`, `perf`, `test`, `chore` ### 3. Push to Main ```bash git push origin main ``` ### 4. Verify Deployment - Cloudflare Pages auto-deploys on push to main - Check https://lazygolfing.com to verify changes are live **Also check for changes in:** - `/home/lzg/lazygolfing-backend/api/` - Backend repository - `/home/lzg/` - Root docs and CLAUDE.md files --- ## Project Structure ``` lzg.home/ ├── src/ │ ├── app/ # Next.js App Router │ │ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout (Header, Footer, Schema) │ │ ├── page.tsx # Home page (content only) │ │ ├── globals.css # Global styles + CSS variables │ │ ├── about/page.tsx # About page (uses main layout) │ │ ├── (auth)/ # Auth route group (minimal layout) │ │ │ ├── layout.tsx # Auth-specific layout (no full nav) │ │ │ └── login/page.tsx # Login page │ │ └── [feature]/ # Feature routes │ ├── components/ │ │ ├── layout/ # Layout components (SINGLE SOURCE) │ │ │ ├── Header.tsx # Site header with nav + NotificationBell │ │ │ ├── Footer.tsx # Site footer │ │ │ └── index.ts # Barrel export │ │ ├── seo/ # SEO components │ │ │ ├── JsonLd.tsx # Schema.org structured data │ │ │ └── index.ts # Barrel export │ │ ├── ui/ # shadcn/ui components │ │ ├── notification-bell.tsx # Notification bell with popover dropdown │ │ ├── login-modal.tsx # Login modal component + context │ │ ├── theme-provider.tsx # next-themes wrapper │ │ └── theme-toggle.tsx # Dark/light toggle │ ├── lib/ │ │ ├── utils.ts # cn() utility for class merging │ │ ├── metadata.ts # Centralized SEO metadata config │ │ ├── theme.ts # Brand colors configuration │ │ ├── api.ts # API client (auto-detects env) │ │ └── auth.ts # Authentication utilities │ ├── hooks/ # Custom React hooks │ │ ├── index.ts # Barrel export │ │ ├── useAuth.ts # Auth state management │ │ ├── useNotifications.ts # Notification polling + state (30s interval) │ │ ├── useGeolocation.ts # Geolocation hook │ │ └── use-*.ts # Other utility hooks │ └── types/ # TypeScript types │ └── notification.ts # Notification types + NotificationCount ├── public/ │ ├── alpha/ # Legacy static site (v1) │ ├── favicons/ # All favicon files (organized) │ ├── images/ # Logo, OG image, photos │ ├── patterns/ # SVG patterns (grid, etc.) │ └── site.webmanifest # PWA manifest ├── next.config.ts # Next.js config (static export) ├── tailwind.config.ts # Tailwind + theme colors ├── tsconfig.json # TypeScript config ├── components.json # shadcn/ui config └── package.json # Dependencies & scripts ``` --- ## Layout Architecture (Critical Patterns) ### Single Source of Truth for Header/Footer **RULE:** Header and Footer live ONLY in `layout.tsx` - NEVER duplicate in pages. ```tsx // src/app/layout.tsx - THE pattern import { Header, Footer } from '@/components/layout'; export default function RootLayout({ children }) { return (
{/* Persists across navigation */}
{children}
{/* Persists across navigation */} ); } ``` **Why this matters:** - Header/Footer don't re-render on page navigation (SPA behavior) - Single place to update nav links, branding, footer content - Consistent experience across all pages ### Pages are Content-Only ```tsx // src/app/page.tsx - CORRECT export default function Home() { return ( <>
Hero content...
Features content...
); } // WRONG - Don't do this: export default function Home() { return (
{/* NO! This is in layout.tsx */}
Content
{/* NO! This is in layout.tsx */}
); } ``` ### Route Groups for Different Layouts Use parentheses folders `(group-name)` to create route groups with different layouts: ``` src/app/ ├── layout.tsx # Main layout (full header/footer) ├── page.tsx # / - uses main layout ├── about/page.tsx # /about - uses main layout ├── (auth)/ # Auth group - different layout │ ├── layout.tsx # Minimal layout (no full nav) │ └── login/page.tsx # /login - uses auth layout └── (dashboard)/ # Future: Dashboard group ├── layout.tsx # Dashboard layout (sidebar) └── rounds/page.tsx # /rounds - uses dashboard layout ``` **Key insight:** The `(auth)` folder name doesn't appear in URLs. `/login` not `/(auth)/login`. --- ## SEO & Metadata (Single Source of Truth) ### Centralized Configuration All SEO metadata is defined in `src/lib/metadata.ts`: ```typescript import { createMetadata } from '@/lib/metadata'; // In any page: export const metadata = createMetadata({ title: 'Rounds', description: 'Track and view your golf rounds', path: '/rounds', }); ``` ### What's Included (Automatic) | Feature | Implementation | Location | |---------|---------------|----------| | **OpenGraph** | og:title, og:description, og:image | `metadata.ts` | | **Twitter Cards** | summary_large_image | `metadata.ts` | | **Schema.org** | WebSite, Organization schemas | `JsonLd` component | | **Favicon** | All sizes, apple-touch-icon | `metadata.ts` | | **Robots** | index, follow, googlebot | `metadata.ts` | | **Canonical URLs** | Per-page via path | `createMetadata()` | ### Schema.org Structured Data ```tsx // In layout.tsx or pages: import { JsonLd, websiteSchema, organizationSchema } from '@/components/seo'; // For dynamic pages (e.g., course details): import { createGolfCourseSchema } from '@/components/seo'; ``` ### Updating SEO Across the Platform | To Change | Edit This File | |-----------|---------------| | Site name, tagline, description | `src/lib/metadata.ts` → `siteConfig` | | OG image | Replace `/public/images/og-image.jpg` (1200x630) | | Favicons | Replace files in `/public/favicons/`, update `metadata.ts` icons config | | PWA manifest | Edit `/public/site.webmanifest` | | Twitter handle | `src/lib/metadata.ts` → `siteConfig.twitterHandle` | | Schema.org data | `src/components/seo/JsonLd.tsx` | | Page-specific meta | Export `metadata` from the page file | --- ## Public Folder Organization (Critical Rules) **RULE:** All static assets MUST be organized in appropriate subfolders. ### Folder Structure ``` public/ ├── favicons/ # All favicon-related files │ ├── favicon.ico # Classic favicon │ ├── favicon.svg # Scalable SVG favicon │ ├── favicon-96x96.png # High-res PNG favicon │ ├── favicon-32x32.png # Standard PNG favicon │ ├── favicon-16x16.png # Small PNG favicon │ ├── apple-touch-icon.png # iOS home screen (180x180) │ ├── web-app-manifest-192x192.png # PWA icon │ └── web-app-manifest-512x512.png # PWA icon large ├── images/ # Photos, logos, OG images │ ├── logo.png │ └── og-image.jpg # 1200x630 for social sharing ├── patterns/ # SVG patterns and textures │ └── grid.svg ├── alpha/ # Legacy site (don't modify) └── site.webmanifest # PWA manifest (references /favicons/) ``` ### Asset Path Rules | Asset Type | Folder | Example Path | |-----------|--------|--------------| | Favicons | `/favicons/` | `/favicons/favicon-32x32.png` | | Logos/Brand | `/images/` | `/images/logo.png` | | OG/Social Images | `/images/` | `/images/og-image.jpg` | | Photos/Screenshots | `/images/` | `/images/screenshot-1.png` | | SVG Patterns | `/patterns/` | `/patterns/grid.svg` | ### Social Sharing Image Specs | Platform | Optimal Size | Notes | |----------|-------------|-------| | OpenGraph (FB, LinkedIn, Discord) | 1200x630 | 1.91:1 ratio | | Twitter Cards | 1200x600 | 2:1 ratio (1200x630 works) | | Keep safe zone | 800x400 center | For cropping variations | ### Never Do This ```tsx // ❌ WRONG - Files loose in public/ src="/logo.png" src="/og-image.jpg" src="/grid.svg" // ✅ CORRECT - Files in organized folders src="/images/logo.png" src="/images/og-image.jpg" src="/patterns/grid.svg" ``` --- ## Miami Vice Theming System ### Centralized Color Configuration All colors are defined in `src/lib/theme.ts`: ```typescript // Full brand palette export const brandColors = { pink: { hex: '#ff0080', hsl: '330 100% 50%' }, cyan: { hex: '#00f0ff', hsl: '184 100% 50%' }, purple: { hex: '#9b59ff', hsl: '270 70% 60%' }, orange: { hex: '#ff7033', hsl: '25 100% 55%' }, yellow: { hex: '#ffbf00', hsl: '45 100% 55%' }, coral: { hex: '#ff6b6b', hsl: '10 100% 65%' }, }; ``` ### Theme-Adaptive Colors (globals.css) ```css :root { /* Light theme uses warmer colors */ --brand-primary: 25 100% 55%; /* Orange */ --brand-secondary: 45 100% 55%; /* Yellow */ --brand-accent: 10 100% 65%; /* Coral */ } .dark { /* Dark theme uses neon colors */ --brand-primary: 330 100% 55%; /* Hot Pink */ --brand-secondary: 184 100% 50%; /* Cyan */ --brand-accent: 270 70% 60%; /* Purple */ } ``` ### Using Theme Colors ```tsx // Theme-adaptive (changes with light/dark):
// Uses --brand-primary → --brand-secondary // Static brand colors (always same): // #ff0080 // #00f0ff
// Pink → Cyan
// Orange → Coral → Pink // Utility classes:
// Text glow effect
// Glass morphism
// Background glow effects ``` ### Theme Toggle ```tsx import { ThemeToggle } from '@/components/theme-toggle'; // In header: // Renders sun/moon icon button ``` --- ## Notification System ### NotificationBell Component The notification bell icon appears in the header next to the theme toggle (for authenticated users only). ```tsx import { NotificationBell } from '@/components/notification-bell'; // In Header.tsx: ``` **Features:** - Bell icon with unread count badge (bg-destructive) - Popover dropdown with recent notifications - Summary badges by category (friends, challenges, messages, groups, games) - "Mark all read" button - "See all" link to /notifications page - Automatically refreshes when popover opens ### useNotifications Hook ```tsx import { useNotifications } from '@/hooks/useNotifications'; // Usage: const { notifications, // Recent notifications array count, // NotificationCount with breakdown totalUnread, // Total unread count isLoading, markAsRead, // (id: string) => Promise markAllAsRead, // () => Promise refresh, // () => Promise } = useNotifications(isAuthenticated); ``` **Polling:** - Fetches `/v1/notifications/count` every 30 seconds when authenticated - Pauses polling when tab is hidden (visibilitychange) - Refetches immediately when tab becomes visible ### Notification Types ```typescript // src/types/notification.ts export type NotificationType = | 'FRIEND_REQUEST' | 'FRIEND_ACCEPTED' | 'CHALLENGE_RECEIVED' | 'CHALLENGE_ACCEPTED' | 'CHALLENGE_DECLINED' | 'CHALLENGE_COMPLETED' | 'MESSAGE' | 'GROUP_INVITE' | 'GAME_INVITE' | 'ACHIEVEMENT_EARNED' | 'ROUND_LINKED'; export interface Notification { id: string; type: NotificationType; title: string; message?: string; actionUrl?: string; isRead: boolean; createdAt: string; data?: Record; } export interface NotificationCount { unreadCount: number; breakdown: { friendRequests: number; challenges: number; messages: number; groupInvites: number; gameInvites: number; }; } ``` ### Notifications Page

Full notifications page at `/notifications` with: - Filter tabs: All, Friends, Challenges, Messages, Groups, Games - Paginated list with "Load more" button - Individual notification cards with read/delete actions - Bulk "Mark all read" action - Relative timestamps (just now, 5m ago, 2h ago, 3d ago) --- ## Adding shadcn/ui Components ```bash # Add a component npx shadcn-ui@latest add button npx shadcn-ui@latest add card npx shadcn-ui@latest add dialog # Components go to src/components/ui/ ``` ### Available Components (pre-installed) - `Button` - Primary UI button - Additional components added as needed ### Usage Pattern ```tsx import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'; ``` --- ## API Integration ### API Client (`src/lib/api.ts`) ```typescript import { apiRequest, API_BASE, IS_DEV } from '@/lib/api'; // GET request const { ok, data } = await apiRequest('/v1/endpoint'); // POST with body const result = await apiRequest('/v1/rounds', { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify({ courseName: 'Test', strokes: 85 }), }); // Response type interface ApiResponse { ok: boolean; status: number; data: T | null; } ``` **Features:** - Auto-detects localhost vs production - Includes credentials (for JWT cookies) - CSRF token extraction from cookies - Handles 204 No Content responses ### Authentication (`src/lib/auth.ts`) ```typescript import { getSession, getMe, logout, getGoogleLoginUrl } from '@/lib/auth'; // Check auth status const session = await getSession(); if (session.authenticated) { console.log(session.user.email); } // Get user profile const { ok, data: profile } = await getMe(); // Redirect to Google OAuth window.location.href = getGoogleLoginUrl(); ``` ### Environment Variables | Variable | Purpose | Default | |----------|---------|---------| | `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` | Backend API URL | Auto-detects based on hostname | **Auto-detection logic:** - If `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` is set → use that - If hostname is `localhost` or `127.0.0.1` → use `http://localhost:3000` - Otherwise → use `https://api.lazygolfing.com` **Development `.env.local`:** ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://192.168.1.40:3000 ``` --- ## Routing ### App Router Structure ``` src/app/ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout (Header, Footer, Schema) ├── page.tsx # / (home) - content only ├── about/page.tsx # /about ├── (auth)/ # Auth route group (minimal header) │ ├── layout.tsx # Auth-specific layout │ └── login/page.tsx # /login ├── (main)/ # Future: Pages with full nav │ ├── rounds/ │ │ ├── page.tsx # /rounds (list) │ │ └── [id]/page.tsx # /rounds/123 (detail) │ ├── leagues/ │ │ └── page.tsx # /leagues │ ├── tournaments/ │ │ └── page.tsx # /tournaments │ └── profile/ │ └── page.tsx # /profile ``` ### Route Groups Explained | Group | Layout | Use Case | |-------|--------|----------| | `(auth)` | Minimal header, no nav | Login, signup, forgot password | | `(main)` | Full header/footer | Most public pages | | `(dashboard)` | Sidebar + header | Authenticated user pages | ### Link to Legacy Site ```tsx // Link to old pages during migration: Sign In (Legacy) ``` --- ## Cloudflare Pages Deployment ### Build Settings - **Build command:** `npm run build` - **Build output directory:** `out` - **Root directory:** `lzg.home` ### Static Export The `next.config.ts` is configured for static export: ```typescript const nextConfig = { output: 'export', // Static HTML export trailingSlash: true, // /about/ instead of /about images: { unoptimized: true }, // Required for static }; ``` ### Output Structure ``` out/ ├── index.html # New home page ├── rounds/index.html # New rounds page ├── alpha/ # Legacy site (copied from public/) │ ├── index.html │ └── ... └── _next/ # JS/CSS bundles ``` --- ## Development Workflow ### Starting Development ```bash cd lzg.home npm install npm run dev ``` ### Creating a New Page ```tsx // src/app/courses/page.tsx import { createMetadata } from '@/lib/metadata'; // 1. Add page-specific metadata export const metadata = createMetadata({ title: 'Courses', description: 'Browse golf courses and view ratings, slopes, and reviews.', path: '/courses', }); // 2. Page is content-only (Header/Footer come from layout.tsx) export default function CoursesPage() { return (

Courses

{/* Content here */}
); } ``` **Checklist for new pages:** - [ ] Export `metadata` using `createMetadata()` for SEO - [ ] Don't include Header/Footer (they're in layout.tsx) - [ ] Use the standard content wrapper pattern - [ ] Add to navigation in `components/layout/Header.tsx` if needed ### Creating a Component ```tsx // src/components/course-card.tsx interface CourseCardProps { name: string; location: string; par: number; } export function CourseCard({ name, location, par }: CourseCardProps) { return (

{name}

{location}

Par {par}

); } ``` --- ## Styling Guidelines ### Use Tailwind Classes ```tsx // Good - Tailwind utility classes
// Avoid - inline styles
``` ### CSS Variables for Theming ```tsx // Good - uses theme variables
// Avoid - hardcoded colors
``` ### Responsive Design ```tsx // Mobile-first approach
``` --- ## Legacy Site (Alpha) The previous static site is preserved at `/alpha/`: - **URL:** https://lazygolfing.com/alpha/ - **Location:** `public/alpha/` - **Purpose:** Fallback during migration ### Legacy Files ``` public/alpha/ ├── index.html, profile.html, rounds.html, ... ├── js/auth.js # Authentication client ├── js/profile.js, rounds.js # Page logic ├── css/style.css # Custom styles ├── data/ # Generated JSON ├── golfers/, leagues/, tours/ # CSV + scripts └── scripts/convert-csv.js # Data pipeline ``` ### Maintaining Legacy Data The CSV → JSON pipeline still works: ```bash npm run build:data # Converts CSVs in public/alpha/ npm run check:data # Verifies JSON is current ``` --- ## File Naming Conventions | Type | Convention | Example | |------|------------|---------| | Components | PascalCase | `CourseCard.tsx` | | Pages | kebab-case folders | `src/app/my-rounds/page.tsx` | | Utilities | camelCase | `utils.ts`, `apiClient.ts` | | Hooks | use prefix | `useAuth.ts`, `useRounds.ts` | | Types | PascalCase | `types.ts` → `export type Round` | --- ## Common Patterns ### Client Components ```tsx 'use client'; // Add at top for interactivity import { useState } from 'react'; export function Counter() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); return ; } ``` ### Server Components (Default) ```tsx // No 'use client' - runs on server export default async function Page() { const data = await fetch('https://api.example.com/data'); return
{/* render data */}
; } ``` ### Loading States ```tsx // src/app/rounds/loading.tsx export default function Loading() { return
Loading...
; } ``` --- ## Debugging ### Check Theme ```javascript // Browser console document.documentElement.classList.contains('dark') // true if dark mode ``` ### Check API Base ```javascript // Environment variable in browser console.log(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL); ``` ### Common Issues | Symptom | Cause | Solution | |---------|-------|----------| | Hydration mismatch | Theme flash | `suppressHydrationWarning` on html | | 404 on routes | Static export | Check `trailingSlash: true` | | Styles not loading | Tailwind purge | Check `content` in tailwind.config | | API CORS errors | Wrong origin | Add localhost to backend CORS | | Build fails | Type errors | Run `npm run lint` first | --- ## Related Documentation | Document | Description | |----------|-------------| | [/CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md) | Root monorepo overview | | [/lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md](../lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md) | Backend API documentation | | [/docs/architecture/API_REFERENCE.md](../docs/architecture/API_REFERENCE.md) | Complete API endpoint reference | | [/docs/architecture/DATABASE_SCHEMA.md](../docs/architecture/DATABASE_SCHEMA.md) | Prisma schema & ER diagram | | [/docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md](../docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md) | High-level architecture | | [/docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md](../docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md) | Miami Vice design system | | [/docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md](../docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md) | Comprehensive feature list | --- *This file was updated during the Next.js v2.0 + Miami Vice migration (2025-12-06).* *Architecture patterns (Layout, SEO, Route Groups) added 2025-12-06.* # CLAUDE.md - Components Directory > Context for working in `lzg.home/src/components/` --- ## Directory Structure ``` components/ ├── layout/ # SINGLE SOURCE: Header, Footer │ ├── Header.tsx # Site header with navigation │ ├── Footer.tsx # Site footer │ └── index.ts # Barrel export ├── seo/ # SEO components │ ├── JsonLd.tsx # Schema.org structured data │ └── index.ts # Barrel export ├── ui/ # shadcn/ui components (auto-generated) │ ├── button.tsx │ ├── card.tsx │ └── [other shadcn components] ├── theme-provider.tsx # next-themes wrapper └── theme-toggle.tsx # Dark/light mode toggle ``` --- ## Critical Rules ### Layout Components (NEVER DUPLICATE) **Header and Footer exist ONLY in `layout/` directory.** ```tsx // CORRECT: Import from layout/ import { Header, Footer } from '@/components/layout'; // WRONG: Never create Header/Footer elsewhere // components/home/Header.tsx ❌ FORBIDDEN ``` ### shadcn/ui Components **Never manually edit files in `ui/` directory.** ```bash # To add a component: npx shadcn-ui@latest add button # To update a component: npx shadcn-ui@latest add button --overwrite ``` If you need to customize a shadcn component: 1. Create a wrapper in `components/` (not in `ui/`) 2. Import and extend the base component ```tsx // components/branded-button.tsx - CORRECT import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'; export function BrandedButton({ children, ...props }) { return ( ); } ``` --- ## Component Patterns ### File Structure ```tsx // components/course-card.tsx // 1. Imports (external, then internal, then types) import { useState } from 'react'; import { cn } from '@/lib/utils'; import type { Course } from '@/types'; // 2. Props interface interface CourseCardProps { course: Course; onSelect?: (course: Course) => void; className?: string; } // 3. Component (named export preferred) export function CourseCard({ course, onSelect, className }: CourseCardProps) { return (
{/* Content */}
); } ``` ### Styling Rules ```tsx // CORRECT: Theme variables
// CORRECT: Brand utilities
// WRONG: Hardcoded colors
// ❌
// ❌ ``` ### Size Limits - **Maximum 200 lines per component** - If larger, split into smaller components - Extract hooks for complex logic ---
