; 2538→} 2539→``` 2540→ 2541→--- 2542→ 2543→## Before Committing 2544→ 2545→- [ ] Component is <200 lines 2546→- [ ] Props interface is typed (no `any`) 2547→- [ ] Uses `cn()` for conditional classes 2548→- [ ] Uses theme variables, not hardcoded colors 2549→- [ ] Has loading/error states if async 2550→- [ ] Accessible (ARIA labels, keyboard nav) 2551→- [ ] Works on mobile (375px+) 2552→ 2553→--- 2554→ 2555→*For full frontend guidelines, see [lzg.home/CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md)* 2556→# CLAUDE.md — Neuman Brain (constitution, token‑lean) 2557→ 2558→Purpose: persistent repo‑specific rules for Claude Code. Keep this file lean; put deep/volatile details in folder‑level `CLAUDE.md` files. 2559→ 2560→## Identity 2561→Role: lead autonomous engineer/architect for **Neuman Brain** (privacy‑first, local‑only productivity dashboard). 2562→ 2563→Core mindset: project is ~1% complete → build primitives that scale, fix root causes, repair “broken windows” (stale docs/code) when encountered. 2564→ 2565→Communication: direct, technical. Prefer correctness over speed. 2566→ 2567→--- 2568→ 2569→## P0 (non‑negotiable) 2570→- **Data integrity:** never lose user data; handle failures; migrations reversible. 2571→- **Privacy:** local‑first; no PII collection; no server data exfil; **E2EE** backups/exports. Anonymous analytics allowed (Cloudflare Web Analytics - no cookies, no fingerprinting, GDPR-compliant). Geolocation prompts user (not blocked). 2572→- **Security:** sanitize inputs; never render user content via `innerHTML`; prefer **WebCrypto**; no secrets committed. 2573→- **Coherence:** follow existing patterns (Zustand stores, Widget system, Tailwind semantic tokens). No competing paradigms. 2574→- **Atomic delivery:** small diffs; explain *why*; avoid big‑bang refactors. 2575→- **Maintainability:** explicit > clever; minimal "magic". 2576→- **Ownership:** build features custom, use utility libraries only (see "Build vs Buy" below). Must be commercially viable (MIT/Apache/BSD licensed). 2577→ 2578→--- 2579→ 2580→## Context hygiene (token/quality control) 2581→- Prefer short, high‑signal summaries over long logs. If output is huge, summarize + attach only the relevant excerpt. 2582→- Don't `@`-embed large docs by default. Instead: reference paths + *when* to read them. 2583→- Use `/clear` after finishing a small unit of work (or when the thread gets noisy) to keep context clean; rely on this file + task files for continuity. 2584→ 2585→### Smart Delegation (tache features) 2586→When context gets heavy (>100k tokens) or tasks are moderate complexity (30 min - 2 hours): 2587→- Use **`/create-prompt`** to create delegatable prompt → `/run-prompt` to execute in fresh context 2588→- Main context stays clean, avoids token bloat 2589→- Results integrated back after completion 2590→ 2591→For multi-stage features requiring research → plan → implement: 2592→- Use **`/create-meta-prompt`** to create pipeline → `/run-prompt` delegates entire workflow 2593→- Each stage produces output for next stage; context remains pristine 2594→ 2595→When approaching session boundaries: 2596→- Use **`/whats-next`** to create handoff document → `/clear` to start fresh 2597→- Next session resumes with handoff context; no information loss 2598→ 2599→See [Tache Integration Guide](./docs/technical/tache-integration-guide.md) for complete workflow patterns. 2600→ 2601→--- 2602→ 2603→## Session protocol (strict) 2604→### Start (in order) 2605→1. `git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit` 2606→2. Read `` for **today's date** (never guess dates). 2607→3. `git log -3` 2608→4. **`/check-todos`** to list outstanding work (or `ls tasks/*.md` for explicit task files) 2609→5. If resuming: read task file and active todos; if new work: describe naturally, let routing determine workflow 2610→ 2611→### End (in order) 2612→1. `npm run test && npm run type-check` 2613→2. If feature behavior changed/added: update `docs/product/feature_map.md` 2614→3. Archive completed work: 2615→ - Tasks: `mv tasks/.md .archive/completed-tasks/` 2616→ - Prompts: `mv prompts/.md .archive/completed-prompts/` 2617→4. If created/modified custom skills/commands/agents: use **`/audit-skill`**, **`/audit-slash-command`**, or **`/audit-subagent`** before committing 2618→5. Use **`/add-to-todos`** to capture any follow-up items discovered during work 2619→6. Commit with a comprehensive message. 2620→ 2621→Dev server rule: start `npm run dev` **only** to test UI changes; stop immediately after. 2622→- Verify stopped: `lsof -ti:5173` (macOS/Linux) OR `Get-NetTCPConnection -LocalPort 5173` / `netstat -ano | findstr :5173` (Windows). 2623→ 2624→Background process rule: kill all background shells (Playwright tests, long-running scripts, etc.) when done. 2625→- Check running shells: Use `/tasks` command or monitor system reminders 2626→- Kill shells: Use `KillShell` tool with shell_id 2627→ 2628→--- 2629→ 2630→## Workflow (default) 2631→ 2632→### Intelligent Prompt Routing (AUTOMATIC) 2633→ 2634→**ALL prompts are automatically analyzed and routed to optimal tache features based on:** 2635→- **Keywords**: "plan", "debug", "should we", "create", "audit" → specific features 2636→- **Complexity**: Simple (1-2 files) → direct; Moderate (3-10 files) → delegate; Complex (multi-stage) → pipeline 2637→- **Type**: Planning → `/create-plan`; Debugging → `/debug` or `debug-like-expert`; Decisions → `/consider:*` frameworks 2638→ 2639→**Routing is transparent—just describe what you need naturally.** 2640→ 2641→**Common routing patterns:** 2642→| Prompt Type | Auto-Routes To | Example | 2643→|-------------|----------------|---------| 2644→| "Plan the X feature" | `/create-plan` | Creates hierarchical plan with phases | 2645→| "Debug intermittent Y" | `debug-like-expert` skill | Systematic root cause analysis | 2646→| "Should we use A or B?" | Appropriate `/consider:*` | Structured decision framework | 2647→| "Refactor across N files" | `/create-prompt` + `/run-prompt` | Delegates to fresh context | 2648→| "Research then implement X" | `/create-meta-prompt` | Multi-stage pipeline | 2649→ 2650→Full routing specification: [Tache Integration Guide § Intelligent Routing](./docs/technical/tache-integration-guide.md#part-2-intelligent-routing-system) 2651→ 2652→--- 2653→ 2654→### Standard Workflow (for direct execution or after routing) 2655→ 2656→1. **Plan:** For complex work, routing auto-suggests `/create-plan`. Otherwise create `tasks/.md` (stage early). Include: 2657→ - Overview + requirements 2658→ - Files to read/modify/create 2659→ - Ordered atomic checklist 2660→ - Key decisions + rationale 2661→ - (Optional) time estimate 2662→ 2663→2. **Approval gate:** **must use `ExitPlanMode` before any code edits/writes**. 2664→ 2665→3. **Execute:** use `TodoWrite`; mark items as you finish them; keep commits atomic. 2666→ 2667→4. **Verify:** automated + manual checks for impacted UX flows. 2668→ 2669→5. **Close out:** add task "Review" section (what changed, verification, issues). 2670→ 2671→Task template (if present): `.claude/templates/task-template.md` 2672→ 2673→Pre‑commit: commits are blocked when code changes without a staged task file (see `.husky/pre-commit`). 2674→ 2675→--- 2676→ 2677→## Stack (constraints) 2678→- React 19 + TypeScript 5.9 + Vite 7 2679→- Zustand 5 (persist middleware) 2680→- Tailwind 3 **semantic tokens** (no hardcoded colors) 2681→- IndexedDB via Dexie (local persistence) 2682→- React Router 7 2683→- Editors/embeds: Lexical (notes), XTerm (terminal) 2684→ 2685→--- 2686→ 2687→## Build vs Buy (dependency philosophy) 2688→ 2689→**Core Principle:** We build features, we use utilities. Never copy-paste complete feature implementations. 2690→ 2691→**What We Build (90%+ ownership):** 2692→- All feature logic, business rules, UI/UX 2693→- All data models, persistence, exports 2694→- All integrations with our stores/services 2695→- All user-facing functionality 2696→ 2697→**What We Use (Utilities):** 2698→- **Allowed:** Libraries that provide low-level abstractions/algorithms 2699→ - Examples: D3 (graph layout), Recharts (chart primitives), Lexical (rich text editing), Konva (canvas rendering), XTerm (terminal emulation) 2700→ - Criterion: We own the feature, library handles complexity we shouldn't reimplement 2701→- **NOT Allowed:** Complete feature implementations 2702→ - Examples: Full Excalidraw (diagram editor), Trello clone (task board), complete calendar apps 2703→ - Criterion: If we're wrapping/extending their feature instead of building our own, it's wrong 2704→ 2705→**License Requirements:** 2706→- **Must use:** MIT, Apache 2.0, BSD licenses (commercial use allowed) 2707→- **Never use:** GPL, AGPL (copyleft - blocks future commercialization) 2708→- **Verify:** All dependencies must allow selling the platform for profit 2709→ 2710→**Decision Framework:** 2711→When considering a new dependency, ask: 2712→1. **Am I building or wrapping?** If wrapping → reject, build custom 2713→2. **What % is my code?** If <80% → reject, build custom or use smaller utility 2714→3. **Can I sell this?** If license blocks commercial use → reject 2715→4. **Can I fork if needed?** If closed-source or unmaintained → reject 2716→5. **Bundle size?** If >100KB for single feature → reject or find smaller alternative 2717→ 2718→**Examples:** 2719→- ✅ **D3 for Graph View:** We use D3's force simulation (utility), we built the entire Graph feature 2720→- ✅ **Konva for Diagrams:** We use Konva for canvas (utility), we built the entire Diagram editor 2721→- ✅ **Recharts for Analytics:** We use Recharts primitives (utility), we built the Analytics feature 2722→- ❌ **Full Excalidraw:** Would be 90% their code, 10% ours - this is wrong 2723→- ❌ **Trello library:** Would be wrapping their task board, not building ours 2724→ 2725→**Pride & Control Test:** 2726→Can we truthfully say "We built this feature" and have full control to add any capability we want? If no, wrong approach. 2727→ 2728→--- 2729→ 2730→## Commands (known scripts) 2731→- Dev: `npm run dev` (port 5173) 2732→- Build: `npm run build`, `npm run build:production` (if present) 2733→- Test: `npm run test`, `npm run test:ui` (if present), `npm run test:coverage` (if present) 2734→- Types: `npm run type-check` 2735→- Quality: `npm run lint`, `npm run format`, `npm run preview` (if present) 2736→ 2737→### Testing Strategy 2738→**Unit Tests (Vitest):** Run automatically in CI on every push. Fast, reliable, comprehensive coverage. 2739→ 2740→**E2E Tests (Playwright):** Run **locally only**, not in CI. Rationale: 2741→- Local-first app architecture = testing where app runs (locally) makes most sense 2742→- No server/deployment to verify in CI 2743→- E2E tests more valuable during local development 2744→- Avoids CI flakiness and overhead 2745→ 2746→**Before committing:** Run `npm run test && npm run type-check` locally. Optionally run `npx playwright test` for E2E verification of critical flows. 2747→ 2748→--- 2749→ 2750→## Performance budgets (targets; justify exceptions) 2751→- **Entry/initial route chunk:** < **100KB gz** (stretch goal). 2752→- **Total initial load:** keep as low as possible via lazy routes/widgets; explain tradeoffs when exceeded. 2753→- Must use lazy-loading for heavy pages/widgets and prefer named imports (tree‑shaking). 2754→ 2755→UX targets: <1s load where feasible; <100ms interaction latency where feasible; WCAG AA minimum. 2756→ 2757→--- 2758→ 2759→## Folder-specific CLAUDE.md files (read when working in that area) 2760→| Folder | CLAUDE.md | Focus | 2761→|--------|-----------|-------| 2762→| `src/hooks/` | `CLAUDE.md` | React hooks patterns + inventory | 2763→| `src/pages/` | `CLAUDE.md` | Route pages + lazy loading | 2764→| `src/services/` | `CLAUDE.md` | Business logic + service patterns | 2765→| `src/stores/` | `CLAUDE.md` | Zustand stores + persistence | 2766→| `src/widgets/` | `CLAUDE.md` | Widget system + registry | 2767→| `src/components/` | `CLAUDE.md` | Shared UI components | 2768→| `tests/` | `CLAUDE.md` | Testing patterns (Vitest/Playwright) | 2769→ 2770→**IMPORTANT:** Always read the relevant folder's CLAUDE.md when working in that area. These contain domain-specific patterns, inventories, and constraints. 2771→ 2772→### When to update folder CLAUDE.md files 2773→Update the relevant folder's CLAUDE.md when: 2774→- **Adding a new file:** Add it to the inventory table 2775→- **Removing a file:** Remove from inventory table 2776→- **Changing patterns:** Update the pattern section if conventions change 2777→- **New anti-patterns discovered:** Add to anti-patterns list 2778→- **Breaking changes:** Document in non-negotiables if it affects the folder 2779→ 2780→**Maintenance triggers:** 2781→- Created new hook → update `src/hooks/CLAUDE.md` inventory 2782→- Created new store → update `src/stores/CLAUDE.md` inventory 2783→- Created new widget → update `src/widgets/CLAUDE.md` categories 2784→- Created new service → update `src/services/CLAUDE.md` inventory 2785→- Created new page → update `src/pages/CLAUDE.md` inventory 2786→- Created new component → update `src/components/CLAUDE.md` categories 2787→- Added E2E test → update `tests/CLAUDE.md` if new pattern established 2788→ 2789→--- 2790→ 2791→## Repo map (high‑signal orientation) 2792→- **Pages:** `src/pages/*` — lazy routes (Dashboard, Tasks, Notes, TimeTracking, LinkLibrary, Automations, Settings) 2793→- **State:** `src/stores/*` — Zustand + persist (no Redux) 2794→- **Widgets:** `src/widgets/Dashboard/*` — `BaseWidget.tsx` wrapper, `WidgetRegistry.ts` discovery 2795→- **Services:** `src/services/*` — business logic (`indexedDB.ts`, `automationEngine.ts`, `brainBackup.ts`, `validation.ts`, `ai/*`) 2796→- **Components:** `src/components/*` — shared UI (modals, layout, calendar views, etc.) 2797→- **Hooks:** `src/hooks/*` — custom React hooks 2798→- **Tests:** `src/**/__tests__/*` (Vitest), `tests/e2e/*` (Playwright) 2799→ 2800→--- 2801→ 2802→## Critical invariants (do not break) 2803→- **Date keys:** `YYYY-M-D` (NOT zero‑padded) used across Calendar/TimeTracking/Events. 2804→- Automation: 2805→ - Event triggers include `task.created`, `task.moved`, `task.completed`, `task.updated` 2806→ - Recursion/loop protection: max depth 10 2807→- Schema changes: migrations must be reversible; backup/restore must round-trip without loss. 2808→ 2809→--- 2810→ 2811→## Thinking Frameworks (structured decision-making) 2812→ 2813→When making architectural or strategic decisions, use **`/consider:*` frameworks** for systematic analysis: 2814→ 2815→| Decision Type | Framework | When to Use | 2816→|---------------|-----------|-------------| 2817→| Long-term implications | `/consider:10-10-10` | Tech choices, migrations, architecture | 2818→| Root cause analysis | `/consider:5-whys` | Recurring problems, understanding patterns | 2819→| Prioritization | `/consider:eisenhower-matrix` | Backlog triage, sprint planning | 2820→| Innovation | `/consider:first-principles` | Rethinking approaches, ground-up design | 2821→| Risk identification | `/consider:inversion` | Security review, failure mode analysis | 2993→const disableTimeoutRef = useRef(null); 2994→ 2995→const handleDragStart = () => { 2996→ // Disable clicks IMMEDIATELY when drag starts 2997→ if (disableTimeoutRef.current) clearTimeout(disableTimeoutRef.current); ## Common Patterns ### Conditional Classes ```tsx import { cn } from '@/lib/utils'; ; } ``` ### Loading States ```tsx export function DataComponent() { const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true); if (loading) { return
Loading...
; } return
{/* Content */}
; } ``` --- ## Before Committing - [ ] Component is <200 lines - [ ] Props interface is typed (no `any`) - [ ] Uses `cn()` for conditional classes - [ ] Uses theme variables, not hardcoded colors - [ ] Has loading/error states if async - [ ] Accessible (ARIA labels, keyboard nav) - [ ] Works on mobile (375px+) --- *For full frontend guidelines, see [lzg.home/CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md)* # CLAUDE.md — Neuman Brain (constitution, token‑lean) Purpose: persistent repo‑specific rules for Claude Code. Keep this file lean; put deep/volatile details in folder‑level `CLAUDE.md` files. ## Identity Role: lead autonomous engineer/architect for **Neuman Brain** (privacy‑first, local‑only productivity dashboard). Core mindset: project is ~1% complete → build primitives that scale, fix root causes, repair “broken windows” (stale docs/code) when encountered. Communication: direct, technical. Prefer correctness over speed. --- ## P0 (non‑negotiable) - **Data integrity:** never lose user data; handle failures; migrations reversible. - **Privacy:** local‑first; no PII collection; no server data exfil; **E2EE** backups/exports. Anonymous analytics allowed (Cloudflare Web Analytics - no cookies, no fingerprinting, GDPR-compliant). Geolocation prompts user (not blocked). - **Security:** sanitize inputs; never render user content via `innerHTML`; prefer **WebCrypto**; no secrets committed. - **Coherence:** follow existing patterns (Zustand stores, Widget system, Tailwind semantic tokens). No competing paradigms. - **Atomic delivery:** small diffs; explain *why*; avoid big‑bang refactors. - **Maintainability:** explicit > clever; minimal "magic". - **Ownership:** build features custom, use utility libraries only (see "Build vs Buy" below). Must be commercially viable (MIT/Apache/BSD licensed). --- ## Context hygiene (token/quality control) - Prefer short, high‑signal summaries over long logs. If output is huge, summarize + attach only the relevant excerpt. - Don't `@`-embed large docs by default. Instead: reference paths + *when* to read them. - Use `/clear` after finishing a small unit of work (or when the thread gets noisy) to keep context clean; rely on this file + task files for continuity. ### Smart Delegation (tache features) When context gets heavy (>100k tokens) or tasks are moderate complexity (30 min - 2 hours): - Use **`/create-prompt`** to create delegatable prompt → `/run-prompt` to execute in fresh context - Main context stays clean, avoids token bloat - Results integrated back after completion For multi-stage features requiring research → plan → implement: - Use **`/create-meta-prompt`** to create pipeline → `/run-prompt` delegates entire workflow - Each stage produces output for next stage; context remains pristine When approaching session boundaries: - Use **`/whats-next`** to create handoff document → `/clear` to start fresh - Next session resumes with handoff context; no information loss See [Tache Integration Guide](./docs/technical/tache-integration-guide.md) for complete workflow patterns. --- ## Session protocol (strict) ### Start (in order) 1. `git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit` 2. Read `` for **today's date** (never guess dates). 3. `git log -3` 4. **`/check-todos`** to list outstanding work (or `ls tasks/*.md` for explicit task files) 5. If resuming: read task file and active todos; if new work: describe naturally, let routing determine workflow ### End (in order) 1. `npm run test && npm run type-check` 2. If feature behavior changed/added: update `docs/product/feature_map.md` 3. Archive completed work: - Tasks: `mv tasks/.md .archive/completed-tasks/` - Prompts: `mv prompts/.md .archive/completed-prompts/` 4. If created/modified custom skills/commands/agents: use **`/audit-skill`**, **`/audit-slash-command`**, or **`/audit-subagent`** before committing 5. Use **`/add-to-todos`** to capture any follow-up items discovered during work 6. Commit with a comprehensive message. Dev server rule: start `npm run dev` **only** to test UI changes; stop immediately after. - Verify stopped: `lsof -ti:5173` (macOS/Linux) OR `Get-NetTCPConnection -LocalPort 5173` / `netstat -ano | findstr :5173` (Windows). Background process rule: kill all background shells (Playwright tests, long-running scripts, etc.) when done. - Check running shells: Use `/tasks` command or monitor system reminders - Kill shells: Use `KillShell` tool with shell_id --- ## Workflow (default) ### Intelligent Prompt Routing (AUTOMATIC) **ALL prompts are automatically analyzed and routed to optimal tache features based on:** - **Keywords**: "plan", "debug", "should we", "create", "audit" → specific features - **Complexity**: Simple (1-2 files) → direct; Moderate (3-10 files) → delegate; Complex (multi-stage) → pipeline - **Type**: Planning → `/create-plan`; Debugging → `/debug` or `debug-like-expert`; Decisions → `/consider:*` frameworks **Routing is transparent—just describe what you need naturally.** **Common routing patterns:** | Prompt Type | Auto-Routes To | Example | |-------------|----------------|---------| | "Plan the X feature" | `/create-plan` | Creates hierarchical plan with phases | | "Debug intermittent Y" | `debug-like-expert` skill | Systematic root cause analysis | | "Should we use A or B?" | Appropriate `/consider:*` | Structured decision framework | | "Refactor across N files" | `/create-prompt` + `/run-prompt` | Delegates to fresh context | | "Research then implement X" | `/create-meta-prompt` | Multi-stage pipeline | Full routing specification: [Tache Integration Guide § Intelligent Routing](./docs/technical/tache-integration-guide.md#part-2-intelligent-routing-system) --- ### Standard Workflow (for direct execution or after routing) 1. **Plan:** For complex work, routing auto-suggests `/create-plan`. Otherwise create `tasks/.md` (stage early). Include: - Overview + requirements - Files to read/modify/create - Ordered atomic checklist - Key decisions + rationale - (Optional) time estimate 2. **Approval gate:** **must use `ExitPlanMode` before any code edits/writes**. 3. **Execute:** use `TodoWrite`; mark items as you finish them; keep commits atomic. 4. **Verify:** automated + manual checks for impacted UX flows. 5. **Close out:** add task "Review" section (what changed, verification, issues). Task template (if present): `.claude/templates/task-template.md` Pre‑commit: commits are blocked when code changes without a staged task file (see `.husky/pre-commit`). --- ## Stack (constraints) - React 19 + TypeScript 5.9 + Vite 7 - Zustand 5 (persist middleware) - Tailwind 3 **semantic tokens** (no hardcoded colors) - IndexedDB via Dexie (local persistence) - React Router 7 - Editors/embeds: Lexical (notes), XTerm (terminal) --- ## Build vs Buy (dependency philosophy) **Core Principle:** We build features, we use utilities. Never copy-paste complete feature implementations. **What We Build (90%+ ownership):** - All feature logic, business rules, UI/UX - All data models, persistence, exports - All integrations with our stores/services - All user-facing functionality **What We Use (Utilities):** - **Allowed:** Libraries that provide low-level abstractions/algorithms - Examples: D3 (graph layout), Recharts (chart primitives), Lexical (rich text editing), Konva (canvas rendering), XTerm (terminal emulation) - Criterion: We own the feature, library handles complexity we shouldn't reimplement - **NOT Allowed:** Complete feature implementations - Examples: Full Excalidraw (diagram editor), Trello clone (task board), complete calendar apps - Criterion: If we're wrapping/extending their feature instead of building our own, it's wrong **License Requirements:** - **Must use:** MIT, Apache 2.0, BSD licenses (commercial use allowed) - **Never use:** GPL, AGPL (copyleft - blocks future commercialization) - **Verify:** All dependencies must allow selling the platform for profit **Decision Framework:** When considering a new dependency, ask: 1. **Am I building or wrapping?** If wrapping → reject, build custom 2. **What % is my code?** If <80% → reject, build custom or use smaller utility 3. **Can I sell this?** If license blocks commercial use → reject 4. **Can I fork if needed?** If closed-source or unmaintained → reject 5. **Bundle size?** If >100KB for single feature → reject or find smaller alternative **Examples:** - ✅ **D3 for Graph View:** We use D3's force simulation (utility), we built the entire Graph feature - ✅ **Konva for Diagrams:** We use Konva for canvas (utility), we built the entire Diagram editor - ✅ **Recharts for Analytics:** We use Recharts primitives (utility), we built the Analytics feature - ❌ **Full Excalidraw:** Would be 90% their code, 10% ours - this is wrong - ❌ **Trello library:** Would be wrapping their task board, not building ours **Pride & Control Test:** Can we truthfully say "We built this feature" and have full control to add any capability we want? If no, wrong approach. --- ## Commands (known scripts) - Dev: `npm run dev` (port 5173) - Build: `npm run build`, `npm run build:production` (if present) - Test: `npm run test`, `npm run test:ui` (if present), `npm run test:coverage` (if present) - Types: `npm run type-check` - Quality: `npm run lint`, `npm run format`, `npm run preview` (if present) ### Testing Strategy **Unit Tests (Vitest):** Run automatically in CI on every push. Fast, reliable, comprehensive coverage. **E2E Tests (Playwright):** Run **locally only**, not in CI. Rationale: - Local-first app architecture = testing where app runs (locally) makes most sense - No server/deployment to verify in CI - E2E tests more valuable during local development - Avoids CI flakiness and overhead **Before committing:** Run `npm run test && npm run type-check` locally. Optionally run `npx playwright test` for E2E verification of critical flows. --- ## Performance budgets (targets; justify exceptions) - **Entry/initial route chunk:** < **100KB gz** (stretch goal). - **Total initial load:** keep as low as possible via lazy routes/widgets; explain tradeoffs when exceeded. - Must use lazy-loading for heavy pages/widgets and prefer named imports (tree‑shaking). UX targets: <1s load where feasible; <100ms interaction latency where feasible; WCAG AA minimum. --- ## Folder-specific CLAUDE.md files (read when working in that area) | Folder | CLAUDE.md | Focus | |--------|-----------|-------| | `src/hooks/` | `CLAUDE.md` | React hooks patterns + inventory | | `src/pages/` | `CLAUDE.md` | Route pages + lazy loading | | `src/services/` | `CLAUDE.md` | Business logic + service patterns | | `src/stores/` | `CLAUDE.md` | Zustand stores + persistence | | `src/widgets/` | `CLAUDE.md` | Widget system + registry | | `src/components/` | `CLAUDE.md` | Shared UI components | | `tests/` | `CLAUDE.md` | Testing patterns (Vitest/Playwright) | **IMPORTANT:** Always read the relevant folder's CLAUDE.md when working in that area. These contain domain-specific patterns, inventories, and constraints. ### When to update folder CLAUDE.md files Update the relevant folder's CLAUDE.md when: - **Adding a new file:** Add it to the inventory table - **Removing a file:** Remove from inventory table - **Changing patterns:** Update the pattern section if conventions change - **New anti-patterns discovered:** Add to anti-patterns list - **Breaking changes:** Document in non-negotiables if it affects the folder **Maintenance triggers:** - Created new hook → update `src/hooks/CLAUDE.md` inventory - Created new store → update `src/stores/CLAUDE.md` inventory - Created new widget → update `src/widgets/CLAUDE.md` categories - Created new service → update `src/services/CLAUDE.md` inventory - Created new page → update `src/pages/CLAUDE.md` inventory - Created new component → update `src/components/CLAUDE.md` categories - Added E2E test → update `tests/CLAUDE.md` if new pattern established --- ## Repo map (high‑signal orientation) - **Pages:** `src/pages/*` — lazy routes (Dashboard, Tasks, Notes, TimeTracking, LinkLibrary, Automations, Settings) - **State:** `src/stores/*` — Zustand + persist (no Redux) - **Widgets:** `src/widgets/Dashboard/*` — `BaseWidget.tsx` wrapper, `WidgetRegistry.ts` discovery - **Services:** `src/services/*` — business logic (`indexedDB.ts`, `automationEngine.ts`, `brainBackup.ts`, `validation.ts`, `ai/*`) - **Components:** `src/components/*` — shared UI (modals, layout, calendar views, etc.) - **Hooks:** `src/hooks/*` — custom React hooks - **Tests:** `src/**/__tests__/*` (Vitest), `tests/e2e/*` (Playwright) --- ## Critical invariants (do not break) - **Date keys:** `YYYY-M-D` (NOT zero‑padded) used across Calendar/TimeTracking/Events. - Automation: - Event triggers include `task.created`, `task.moved`, `task.completed`, `task.updated` - Recursion/loop protection: max depth 10 - Schema changes: migrations must be reversible; backup/restore must round-trip without loss. --- ## Thinking Frameworks (structured decision-making) When making architectural or strategic decisions, use **`/consider:*` frameworks** for systematic analysis: | Decision Type | Framework | When to Use | |---------------|-----------|-------------| | Long-term implications | `/consider:10-10-10` | Tech choices, migrations, architecture | | Root cause analysis | `/consider:5-whys` | Recurring problems, understanding patterns | | Prioritization | `/consider:eisenhower-matrix` | Backlog triage, sprint planning | | Innovation | `/consider:first-principles` | Rethinking approaches, ground-up design | | Risk identification | `/consider:inversion` | Security review, failure mode analysis | | Simplification | `/consider:occams-razor` | Choosing between approaches | | Focus | `/consider:one-thing` | Identifying critical path | | Tradeoffs | `/consider:opportunity-cost` | Resource allocation, build vs. buy | | Optimization | `/consider:pareto` | 80/20 analysis, high-value focus | | Consequences | `/consider:second-order` | API changes, breaking changes | | Evaluation | `/consider:swot` | Technology assessment, strategic planning | | Reduction | `/consider:via-negativa` | Refactoring, complexity removal | Full reference: [Tache Integration Guide § Thinking Frameworks](./docs/technical/tache-integration-guide.md#33-thinking-frameworks-consider-commands) --- ## Tooling policy (Claude Code) ### File/Code Operations - Use **Read** for known paths; **Grep/Glob** for symbol search. - Use **Task/subagents** only for multi-step exploration/research or cross-cutting reviews. ### Development Workflows - Use **`/create-plan`** for planning complex features (>1 phase) - Use **`/debug`** for clear bugs; **`debug-like-expert` skill** for intermittent/mysterious issues - Use **`/consider:*` frameworks** for architectural decisions (see Thinking Frameworks above) - Use **`/create-prompt`** for delegating moderate tasks (30 min - 2 hours) to fresh context - Use **`/create-meta-prompt`** for multi-stage features (research → plan → implement) ### Decision-Making - Use **AskUserQuestion** when requirements are ambiguous or multiple architectures are valid - Use appropriate **`/consider:*` framework** for structured decisions (not ad-hoc analysis) ### Code Creation & Modification - Use **ExitPlanMode** before edits/writes - Use **`/create-agent-skill`** for custom skills (project-specific capabilities) - Use **`/create-slash-command`** for project commands (repetitive workflows) - Use **`/create-subagent`** for specialized agents (domain experts) ### Quality Assurance - Use **`/audit-skill`**, **`/audit-slash-command`**, **`/audit-subagent`** before committing - Use **`/heal-skill`** to fix issues discovered during skill execution ### Verification - Browser/E2E: Playwright (and Playwright MCP when available) for Chrome verification of critical flows - Run: `npm run test && npm run type-check` before committing --- ## Docs: authority + drift control Authority order (highest → lowest): 1. `docs/technical/development_principles.md` 2. `docs/technical/widget_development_guide.md` 3. `docs/technical/api_integration_guide.md` 4. `docs/product/feature_map.md` 5. `tasks/master-implementation-status.md` (roadmap/status) 6. `.claude/skills/README.md` 7. `README.md` If docs conflict: higher authority wins; update stale docs. --- ## Git conventions - Prefer conventional commit types: `feat|fix|refactor|perf|test|docs|chore`. - Do not push to `main` without review unless explicitly instructed. - Pre-commit bypass (`git commit --no-verify`) is emergency-only; document why in the task file. Repo issues: `https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/issues` --- ## Anti‑patterns (do not do) - Missing/unstaged task file when code changes. - Generic task names (`tasks/todo.md`, `tasks/task.md`, etc.). - Batch completing todos; update as you go. - Scope creep without asking. - Guessing dates; always read ``. - Leaving dev server running. - Leaving background shells running (Playwright tests, etc.). - Using `any` (use explicit types or `unknown`). - Hardcoded Tailwind colors (use semantic tokens). - Ignoring folder-specific CLAUDE.md files (always read when working in that folder). - Forcing direct execution for complex features (use `/create-plan` or `/create-meta-prompt`). - Letting context bloat (use `/create-prompt` to delegate moderate tasks to fresh context). - Manual decision-making when `/consider:*` frameworks exist (use frameworks for structure). - Debugging intermittent issues manually (use `debug-like-expert` for systematic approach). - Creating custom skills/commands/agents without audit (use `/audit-*` before committing).# src/components/CLAUDE.md — Shared Components ## Scope Reusable React components under `src/components/`. These are shared across pages and widgets. ## Component Categories | Category | Purpose | Examples | |----------|---------|----------| | **Layout** | Page structure | `Layout.tsx`, `Sidebar.tsx`, `Header.tsx`, `Footer.tsx`, `PageHeader.tsx`, `PageContent.tsx` | | **Modals** | Dialog overlays | `Modal.tsx`, `ConfirmDialog.tsx`, `PromptDialog.tsx`, `*Modal.tsx` | | **Calendar/Time** | Date/time UI | `DayView.tsx`, `WeekView.tsx`, `AgendaView.tsx`, `TimeEntry*.tsx` | | **Widget System** | Widget infrastructure | `Widget.tsx`, `WidgetControls.tsx`, `WidgetManager.tsx`, `WidgetErrorBoundary.tsx` | | **Analytics** | Charts & metrics | `Analytics/*` (MetricCard, CompletionRateChart, PriorityDistribution, StatusBreakdown, TimeByProjectChart, HourlyHeatmap, SessionDurationChart, MeetingVsFocusChart, TagFrequencyChart, AnalyticsPeriodSelector) | | **Graph** | Knowledge graph visualization | `Graph/GraphCanvas.tsx` (D3 force-directed graph with zoom/pan/drag) | | **Diagrams** | Diagram editor | `DiagramCanvas/Canvas.tsx` (Konva Stage with shapes, grid, transformer), `DiagramCanvas/Toolbar.tsx` (tool selection) | | **Settings** | Configuration UI | `BackupSettings.tsx`, `ProviderSettings.tsx`, `ModelSelector.tsx`, `PresetManager.tsx` | | **Error Handling** | Error UI | `StoreErrorBoundary.tsx`, `ErrorToast.tsx`, `Toast.tsx` | | **Terminal** | AI/Shell UI | `AITerminal.tsx`, `TerminalHelpModal.tsx`, `PhantomShell/*` | | **Editors** | Rich text/specialized | `editor/*`, `RecurrencePicker.tsx`, `BackgroundCustomizer.tsx` | | **Tags** | Tag management UI | `TagPicker.tsx`, `TagFilter.tsx`, `TagManager.tsx` | | **Projects** | Project context UI | `ProjectTree.tsx`, `ProjectContextDropdown.tsx`, `ProjectPicker.tsx`, `ProjectSettingsModal.tsx` | ## Non-negotiables - **Props typing:** All props must have TypeScript interfaces. No `any`. - **Semantic tokens:** Use Tailwind semantic classes (`bg-surface-*`, `text-text-*`). No hardcoded colors. - **Accessibility:** ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, focus management. - **Error boundaries:** Wrap async/complex components in error boundaries. - **Keep focused:** One component = one responsibility. Split if >300 lines. ## Component Pattern ```tsx interface MyComponentProps { title: string; onAction: () => void; variant?: 'primary' | 'secondary'; } export function MyComponent({ title, onAction, variant = 'primary' }: MyComponentProps) { return (

{title}

); } ``` ## Subfolders - `__tests__/` — Component tests (Vitest + Testing Library) - `Analytics/` — Analytics charts and metric components (Recharts-based) - `auth/` — Authentication-related components - `CommandPalette/` — Global search & command palette (Ctrl+K/Cmd+K) - `DiagramCanvas/` — Diagram editor components (Konva-based) - `editor/` — Rich text editor components (Lexical) - `PhantomShell/` — Terminal emulation components - `search/` — Search-related components - `shared/` — Common primitives - `sidebar/` — Sidebar-specific components ## Component Development **For component API design** (choosing props, simplification): - Use **`/consider:via-negativa`** to identify what props/features to remove - Use **`/consider:occams-razor`** when choosing between complex patterns - Example: "Should we support both controlled and uncontrolled modes, or just controlled?" **For creating project-specific component scaffolding**: - Use **`/create-slash-command`** to build component scaffolding command - Example: Create `/scaffold-component` for consistent component boilerplate See [Tache Integration Guide § Component Development](../../docs/technical/tache-integration-guide.md#workflow-component-development) for complete workflow. --- ## Patterns & Solutions ### Drag-and-Drop with Navigation Links (@dnd-kit) **Problem:** When using @dnd-kit with React Router Links, native browser click events can fire during/after drag operations, causing unwanted navigation. **Solution Pattern (from Sidebar.tsx):** ```tsx const [isDisablingClicks, setIsDisablingClicks] = useState(false); const disableTimeoutRef = useRef(null); const handleDragStart = () => { // Disable clicks IMMEDIATELY when drag starts if (disableTimeoutRef.current) clearTimeout(disableTimeoutRef.current); setIsDisablingClicks(true); };

see Hover Actions pattern). 3209→- Negative margins for hover controls (can overlap adjacent content - use positive spacing instead). 3210→- Using `hidden` class for hover actions (causes layout shift - 3269→ } 3270→ } 3271→ load(); 3272→ return () => { cancelled = true; }; 3273→ }, [param]); 3274→ 3275→ return { data, loading, error }; 3276→} 3277→``` 3278→ 3279→## Creating Custom Hooks 3280→ 3281→**For creating event-driven automation hooks** (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, etc.): 3282→- Use **`/create-hook`** for expert guidance on Claude Code hook development 3283→- Creates hooks that respond to Claude Code events (not React hooks) 3284→ 3285→**For debugging complex hook issues** (stale closures, missing dependencies): 3286→- Use **`/debug`** for clear issues with reproduction steps 3287→- Use **`debug-like-expert` skill** for mysterious/intermittent behavior 3288→- Use **`/consider:5-whys`** to understand why the bug happened 3289→ 3290→See [Tache Integration Guide § Hook Development](../../docs/technical/tache-integration-guide.md#31-skills) for automation hooks. 3291→ 3292→--- 3293→ 3294→## Anti-patterns 3295→- Module-scoped mutable state shared between hook instances. 3296→- Objects/arrays in dependency arrays without memoization. 3297→- "Catch-all" hooks with many flags—compose small hooks instead. 3298→- Heavy computation inside hooks—use `useMemo` or move to services. 3299→- Debugging dependency issues manually (use **`/debug`** for systematic analysis). 3300→ 3301→## Related 3302→- Stores: `src/stores/CLAUDE.md` 3303→- Services: `src/services/CLAUDE.md` 3304→# src/pages/CLAUDE.md — Route Pages 3305→ 3306→## Scope 3307→Top-level route components under `src/pages/`.     • {/* sortable items */} ``` **Why both approaches:** - `pointer-events: none` blocks native browser clicks (most important) - `onClick preventDefault` provides React-level safety (defense in depth) - Timeout in ref survives component re-renders from state updates **Key insights:** - Disable clicks on `onDragStart`, not just `onDragEnd` - Mouse release can occur over Link text, not just drag handle - @dnd-kit can fire native clicks BEFORE React handlers execute - Store timeout ID in ref to survive re-renders --- ### Hover Actions without Overlap **Problem:** Absolute positioned hover actions (edit, delete, etc.) can overlap with content titles/text, making both unreadable. Common in card/list item components. **Solution Pattern (from Notes.tsx):** ```tsx // ❌ BEFORE: Absolute positioning causes overlap
    {/* Static indicators compete with hover actions */}
    {note.isFavorite && } {note.isPinned && }
    {/* Title can overflow into action space */}

    {note.title}

    {/* Actions overlap title when revealed */}
    {/* 5 buttons - can overlap long titles */}
    // ✅ AFTER: Flex layout with reserved action zone
    {/* Header row: Title + Actions */}
    {/* Title: grows to fill space, truncates before actions */}

    {note.title}

    {/* Reserved action zone: prevents overlap */}
    {/* Static indicators: always visible */}
    {note.isFavorite && } {note.isPinned && }
    {/* Hover actions: occupy space even when invisible */}
    {/* Content below */}

    {content}

    ``` **For images/media with controls (from ImageNode.tsx):** ```tsx // ❌ BEFORE: Negative margin can overlap content below
    {/* Controls overlap content below image */}
    // ✅ AFTER: Controls positioned with positive margin
    ``` **Key principles:** 1. **Flex over Absolute:** Use flex layout for title + actions in same row 2. **Reserve Space:** Actions use `opacity-0` (not `hidden`) to preserve layout 3. **Title Flexibility:** `flex-1 min-w-0` allows title to shrink/truncate gracefully 4. **Action Zone:** `shrink-0` prevents action container from collapsing 5. **Positive Spacing:** Use `mt-*` instead of negative margins for media controls **Advantages:** - ✅ Zero overlap at any viewport width or content length - ✅ No layout shift when actions appear/disappear - ✅ Predictable behavior with fixed action widths - ✅ Responsive title truncation **When to use:** - Card components with hover actions (Notes, Tasks, Forms, etc.) - List items with edit/delete/duplicate buttons - Media elements (images, videos) with control overlays --- ### Avoiding Tab/Button Redundancy **Problem:** Creating tabs that filter the same content (e.g., "Documents", "Spreadsheets", "Presentations" tabs that all show docs filtered by type) when direct action buttons would be simpler and more discoverable. **Bad Pattern (from Docs.tsx - fixed 2025-12-11):** ```tsx // ❌ BEFORE: 4 tabs that show the same content with filters const CREATE_TABS: Tab[] = [ { id: 'create', label: 'Create', icon: Sparkles }, { id: 'documents', label: 'Documents', icon: FileText }, // Redundant { id: 'spreadsheets', label: 'Spreadsheets', icon: Table2 }, // Redundant { id: 'presentations', label: 'Presentations', icon: Presentation }, // Redundant ]; // + A dropdown button in sidebar: {showMenu && } // Dropdown with Document/Sheet/Slides // + Another dropdown in empty state center (same options) ``` **Good Pattern:** ```tsx // ✅ AFTER: Only meaningful tabs (distinct content types) const CREATE_TABS: Tab[] = [ { id: 'create', label: 'Create', icon: Sparkles }, // All docs { id: 'diagrams', label: 'Diagrams', icon: Shapes }, // Different content { id: 'forms', label: 'Forms', icon: ClipboardList }, // Different content ]; // Direct action buttons (no dropdown needed):
    // Empty state: Just informational (actions in sidebar)

    Documents, Spreadsheets & Presentations

    Create professional documents...

    {/* No duplicate create buttons */}
    ``` **Key principles:** 1. **Tabs = Distinct Content:** Tabs should show meaningfully different content, not filtered versions of the same list 2. **Direct Actions > Dropdowns:** If you have 2-4 primary actions and space allows, use direct buttons instead of a dropdown menu 3. **Single Point of Action:** Don't duplicate the same action in multiple places (sidebar + center empty state) 4. **Discoverability:** Direct buttons are more discoverable than dropdown menus **Questions to ask:** - "Do these tabs show genuinely different content, or just filtered views?" - "Is the dropdown hiding actions that could be direct buttons?" - "Am I duplicating the same action in multiple places?" --- ## Anti-patterns - Hardcoded colors/sizes (use semantic tokens). - Business logic in components (move to stores/services). - Props drilling >2 levels (use stores or context). - Missing TypeScript types. - No accessibility attributes on interactive elements. - Over-engineering component APIs (use **`/consider:via-negativa`** to simplify). - Using @dnd-kit with Links without click protection (see Patterns section above). - Absolute positioning for hover actions near text content (causes overlap - see Hover Actions pattern). - Negative margins for hover controls (can overlap adjacent content - use positive spacing instead). - Using `hidden` class for hover actions (causes layout shift - use `opacity-0` to preserve space). - Creating tabs that filter the same content instead of using direct action buttons (see Tab/Button Redundancy pattern). - Duplicating the same action in multiple UI locations (e.g., sidebar + empty state center). ## Related - Widgets (consumers): `src/widgets/CLAUDE.md` - Stores (state): `src/stores/CLAUDE.md` - Tests: `tests/CLAUDE.md` # src/hooks/CLAUDE.md — React Hooks ## Scope Custom React hooks under `src/hooks/`. Hooks encapsulate reusable stateful logic and side effects. ## Existing Hooks | Hook | Purpose | |------|---------| | `useBackupOnboarding` | Backup flow state + modal triggers | | `useDragHandle` | Drag-and-drop handle logic | | `useDynamicGridHeight` | Responsive grid height calculation | | `useErrorNotifications` | Error toast/notification management | | `useEscapeKey` | Escape key listener with cleanup | | `useFocusTrap` | Accessibility focus trapping for modals | | `useGlobalShortcuts` | Global keyboard shortcut listener (attach at app root) | | `useKanbanKeyboard` | Keyboard navigation for Kanban board | | `useShortcut` | Register keyboard shortcut with auto-cleanup | | `useShortcutContext` | Push/pop shortcut context for modals | | `usePageShortcutContext` | Set page-level shortcut context | | `useTags` | Get all unique tags across notes | | `useTagCounts` | Get tag counts (number of notes per tag) | | `useNotesWithTags` | Filter notes by tags (AND logic) | | `useRecentTags` | Get recently used tags from latest notes | | `useInlineDateShortcuts` | Detects and replaces inline date shortcuts (@tomorrow, +3d) | | `usePWA` | PWA lifecycle (install prompt, update detection, offline status) | ## Non-negotiables - **Prefix `use`:** All hooks must start with `use` (React convention, linting). - **Single purpose:** One hook = one concern. Split if >100 lines or mixing concerns. - **Unconditional calls:** Never call hooks in loops, conditions, or nested functions. - **Effect cleanup:** Always return cleanup functions from `useEffect` when attaching listeners/timers/subscriptions. - **No DOM manipulation:** Use refs and effect callbacks, not `document.querySelector`. ## Pattern ```ts export function useExample(param: string) { const [data, setData] = useState(null); const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false); const [error, setError] = useState(null); useEffect(() => { let cancelled = false; async function load() { setLoading(true); try { const result = await fetchData(param); if (!cancelled) setData(result); } catch (err) { if (!cancelled) setError(err as Error); } finally { if (!cancelled) setLoading(false); } } load(); return () => { cancelled = true; }; }, [param]); return { data, loading, error }; } ``` ## Creating Custom Hooks **For creating event-driven automation hooks** (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, etc.): - Use **`/create-hook`** for expert guidance on Claude Code hook development - Creates hooks that respond to Claude Code events (not React hooks) **For debugging complex hook issues** (stale closures, missing dependencies): - Use **`/debug`** for clear issues with reproduction steps - Use **`debug-like-expert` skill** for mysterious/intermittent behavior - Use **`/consider:5-whys`** to understand why the bug happened See [Tache Integration Guide § Hook Development](../../docs/technical/tache-integration-guide.md#31-skills) for automation hooks. --- ## Anti-patterns - Module-scoped mutable state shared between hook instances. - Objects/arrays in dependency arrays without memoization. - "Catch-all" hooks with many flags—compose small hooks instead. - Heavy computation inside hooks—use `useMemo` or move to services. - Debugging dependency issues manually (use **`/debug`** for systematic analysis). ## Related - Stores: `src/stores/CLAUDE.md` - Services: `src/services/CLAUDE.md` # src/pages/CLAUDE.md — Route Pages ## Scope Top-level route components under `src/pages/`. Each page is a lazy-loaded route that orchestrates layout, widgets, and store subscriptions. ## Existing Pages | Page | Route | Purpose | |------|-------|---------| | `Dashboard.tsx` | `/` | Widget grid, main dashboard | | `Today.tsx` | `/today` | Daily planning view with timeline, tasks, and metrics | | `Focus.tsx` | `/focus` | Full-screen distraction-free focus mode | | `Tasks.tsx` | `/tasks` | Task management entry (→ Kanban) | | `Notes.tsx` | `/notes` | Note-taking with Lexical editor | | `GraphView.tsx` | `/graph` | Interactive D3.js force-directed graph of notes | | `Diagrams.tsx` | `/diagrams` | Diagram library (grid/list view) | | `DiagramEditor.tsx` | `/diagrams/:id` | Full-screen diagram editor with Konva | | `TimeTracking.tsx` | `/time` | Time tracking + calendar view | | `LinkLibrary.tsx` | `/links` | Bookmark/link management | | `Automations.tsx` | `/automations` | Automation rule management | | `Settings.tsx` | `/settings` | App configuration | | `Forms.tsx` | `/forms` | Form template library (grid/list view) | | `FormBuilder.tsx` | `/forms/:id/edit` | Full-screen form builder with field editor and preview | | `FormFiller.tsx` | `/forms/:id/fill` | Fill out a form and submit responses | | `FormResponses.tsx` | `/forms/:id/responses` | View and analyze form responses with CSV export | | `Habits.tsx` | `/habits` | Habit tracking with streaks and achievements | | `Docs.tsx` | `/docs` | Professional documents, spreadsheets, presentations (TipTap/HyperFormula/Konva) | ## Non-negotiables - **Lazy loading:** Pages are loaded via `React.lazy()` in router config. Never import pages directly. - **Wrap in `StoreErrorBoundary`:** All page content must be wrapped for graceful error handling. - **Keep lean:** Pages orchestrate—business logic lives in stores/services. Target <200 lines. - **Single export:** One default component per file. - **Semantic tokens:** Use Tailwind semantic classes, no hardcoded colors. ## Pattern ```tsx import { Suspense } from 'react'; import { StoreErrorBoundary } from '@/components/StoreErrorBoundary'; import { useFeatureStore } from '@/stores/useFeatureStore'; export default function FeaturePage() { const data = useFeatureStore((s) => s.data); return (

    Feature

    Loading...}> {/* Page content */}
    ); } ``` ## Developing New Pages **For complex page features** (multi-phase, new patterns, lazy loading strategy): - Use **`/create-plan`** to structure implementation before coding - Plan should cover: route config, lazy loading boundaries, store integration, accessibility **For lazy loading decisions** (which boundaries, tradeoffs): - Use **`/consider:opportunity-cost`** to analyze resource allocation - Use **`/consider:pareto`** to identify high-impact lazy loading opportunities See [Tache Integration Guide § Page Development](../../docs/technical/tache-integration-guide.md#workflow-page-development) for complete workflow. --- ## Anti-patterns - Importing heavy widgets at top level (use dynamic imports). - Storing page state locally instead of in Zustand stores. - Duplicating layout markup (use `Layout` component). - Business logic in pages (move to stores/services). - Complex page development without planning (use **`/create-plan`** for multi-feature pages). ## Related - Stores: `src/stores/CLAUDE.md` - Widgets: `src/widgets/CLAUDE.md` - Router config: `src/App.tsx` # src/services/CLAUDE.md — Business Logic Layer ## Scope Service modules under `src/services/`. Services encapsulate business logic, I/O, validation, and external integrations. They are framework-agnostic (no React, no DOM). ## Existing Services | Service | Purpose | |---------|---------| | `indexedDB.ts` | Dexie wrapper for local persistence | | `automationEngine.ts` | Rule evaluation + action execution | | `brainBackup.ts` | Encrypted `.brain` export/import (E2EE) | | `validation.ts` | Zod schemas + canonical parsing | | `encryption.ts` / `encryptionWebCrypto.ts` | WebCrypto-based encryption | | `errorService.ts` | Centralized error handling | | `logger.ts` | Structured logging | | `migration.ts` / `storageMigration.ts` | Schema migrations | | `eventReminders.ts` | Notification scheduling | | `taskRecurrence.ts` | Recurring task generation | | `icsImportExport.ts` | Calendar ICS format support | | `csvTimeEntryExport.ts` | Time tracking CSV export | | `noteExport.ts` / `notePdfExport.ts` | Note export formats | | `linkCategorizer.ts` | Bookmark categorization | | `autoSave.ts` | Debounced auto-save logic | | `backupPreferences.ts` | Backup configuration | | `aiTerminal.ts` | AI terminal integration | | `ai/*` | Multi-provider AI integrations | | `phantomShell/*` | Terminal emulation services | ## Non-negotiables - **No UI dependencies:** Services must not import React, DOM APIs, or UI components. - **Strong typing:** Use explicit TypeScript types + Zod schemas. No `any`. - **Validate I/O:** Parse all inputs/outputs with Zod. Never silently coerce. - **No silent failures:** Always propagate errors. Never `catch` and ignore. - **Secrets via env:** Load from `import.meta.env`, never hardcode. - **Stateless default:** If stateful (e.g., WebSocket), provide explicit cleanup API. ## Pattern ```ts import { z } from 'zod'; // Schemas at top const InputSchema = z.object({ id: z.string() }); const OutputSchema = z.object({ data: z.unknown() }); export async function processData( input: z.infer ): Promise> { const parsed = InputSchema.parse(input); // Business logic... return OutputSchema.parse(result); } ``` ## Debugging Service Issues **For service failures** (network errors, validation errors, unexpected behavior): - Use **`/debug`** for clear failures with known reproduction steps - Use **`debug-like-expert` skill** for intermittent/mysterious failures - Example: "IndexedDB transaction fails intermittently when saving to TimeTrackingStore" **For API design decisions** (breaking changes, new patterns): - Use **`/consider:second-order`** to analyze ripple effects and downstream impacts - Use **`/consider:10-10-10`** for long-term implications of service changes **For recurring service failures**: - Use **`/consider:5-whys`** to drill to root cause and prevent recurrence See [Tache Integration Guide § Service Development](../../docs/technical/tache-integration-guide.md#workflow-debugging) for complete debugging workflows. --- ## Anti-patterns - DOM/React references in services. - `catch` + `return undefined` (always throw or return typed error). - Hardcoded secrets or magic strings. - Copy-pasting entire services (extract shared helpers). - Debugging intermittent failures manually (use **`debug-like-expert`** for systematic approach). ## Related - Tests: `tests/CLAUDE.md` - Stores (consumers): `src/stores/CLAUDE.md` - Docs: `docs/technical/local_first_architecture.md` # src/stores/CLAUDE.md — Zustand State Management ## Scope Zustand stores under `src/stores/`. Each domain has its own store. State is persisted to IndexedDB via Dexie. ## Existing Stores | Store | Domain | |-------|--------| | `useKanbanStore` | Tasks, columns, Kanban board state | | `useNotesStore` | Notes, folders, editor state | | `useDiagramsStore` | Diagrams (CRUD, persistence) | | `useDiagramEditorStore` | Diagram editor state (tool, selection, clipboard, undo/redo) | | `useFormsStore` | Form templates, responses, CRUD operations | | `useCalendarStore` | Events, calendar views | | `useTimeTrackingStore` | Time entries, projects, timers | | `useLinkLibraryStore` | Bookmarks, collections | | `useAutomationStore` | Automation rules, execution history | | `useAnalyticsStore` | Analytics period selection, date ranges | | `useWidgetStore` | Dashboard widget layout + config | | `useSettingsStore` | App preferences | | `useFoldersStore` | Folder hierarchy (notes/links) | | `usePhantomShellStore` | Terminal state | | `useTerminalStore` | AI terminal state | | `useThemeStore` | Theme preferences | | `useUndoStore` | Undo/redo stacks | | `useToastStore` | Global toast notifications (success/error/warning/info) | | `useUptimeStore` | System uptime tracking | | `useSidebarStore` / `useSidebarNavStore` | Navigation UI state | | `useNavExpansionStore` | Sidebar expansion state | | `useSaveStatus` | Save indicator state | | `useTimeTrackingPanelStore` | Time tracking panel UI | | `useWeatherStore` | Weather widget cache | | `useGanttStore` / `useResourceStore` / `useRiskStore` / `useMapStore` | Feature-specific state |

3500→| `useProjectContextStore` | Global project context filter, project CRUD |
  3501→| `useFocusModeStore` | Focus mode session state (active, linked task, settings) |
  3502→| `useHabitStore` | Habits, completions, achievements, streak tracking |
  3503→| `useDocsStore` | Professional documents, spreadsheets, presentations CRUD |
  3504→
  3505→## Global Project Context Filter
  3506→
  3507→All major domain stores support project context filtering via `getFilteredX()` methods:
  3508→
  3509→| Store | Method | Description |
  3510→|-------|--------|-------------|
  3511→| `useKanbanStore` | `getFilteredTasks()` | Tasks matching active project context |
  3512→| `useNotesStore` | `getFilteredNotes()` | Notes matching active project context |
  3513→| `useCalendarStore` | `getFilteredEvents()` | Events matching active project context |
  3514→| `useLinkLibraryStore` | `getFilteredLinks()` | Links matching active project context |
  3515→| `useTimeTrackingStore` | `getFilteredEntries()` | Time entries matching active project context |
  3516→| `useHabitStore` | `getFilteredHabits()` | Habits matching active project context |
  3517→| `useDocsStore` | `getFilteredDocs()` | Documents matching active project context |
  3518→
  3519→These methods check `useProjectContextStore.getState().activeProjectIds` and filter entities that have any matching `projectIds`.
  3520→
  3521→## Non-negotiables
  3522→- **One store per domain:** Never mix unrelated concerns.
  3523→- **Date keys:** Use `YYYY-M-D` format (non-padded). Critical invariant.
  3524→- **Persist middleware:** Use `create<State>()(persist(...))` with unique `name`.
  3525→- **Migrations:** Increment `version` + provide `migrate` function. Never drop data.
  3526→- **Pure actions:** Always use `set(state => ...)`. Never mutate outside `set`.
  3527→- **No cross-store mutations:** Use services for cross-domain logic.
  3528→- **Strict typing:** Define state interface. No `any`.
  3529→
  3530→## Pattern
  3531→```ts
  3532→interface DomainState {
  3533→  items: Item[];
  3534→  addItem: (item: Item) => void;
  3535→}
  3536→
  3537→export const useDomainStore = create<DomainState>()(
  3538→  persist(
  3539→    (set) => ({
  3540→      items: [],
  3541→      addItem: (item) => set((s) => ({ items: [...s.items, item] })),
  3542→    }),
  3543→    {
  3544→      name: 'domain-store',
  3545→      partialize: (s) => ({ items: s.items }),
  3546→      version: 1,
  3547→      migrate: (persisted, version) => persisted,
  3548→    },
  3549→  ),
  3550→);
  3551→```
  3552→
  3553→## File Structure
  3554→1. State interface
  3555→2. Initial values
  3556→3. Actions (verb-noun: `addTask`, `removeTask`)
  3557→4. Persist config (`name`, `partialize`, `version`, `migrate`)
  3558→5. Exported hook
  3559→6. Selectors (for derived values)
  3560→
  3561→## Debugging Store Issues
  3562→
  3563→**For intermittent/mysterious bugs** (data loss, race conditions, unexpected state):
  3564→- Use **`debug-like-expert` skill** for systematic root cause analysis
  3565→- Example: "The TimeTrackingStore sometimes loses date selections when page refreshes"
  3566→
  3567→**For recurring problems** (same pattern repeating):
  3568→- Use **`/consider:5-whys`** to drill to root cause
  3569→- Example: "Why do users keep losing unsaved work in the notes store?"
  3570→
  3571→**For store architecture decisions** (refactoring, simplification):
  3572→- Use **`/consider:via-negativa`** to identify what to remove
  3573→- Use **`/consider:first-principles`** when rethinking store design
  3574→
  3575→See [Tache Integration Guide § Store Development](../../docs/technical/tache-integration-guide.md#workflow-store-debugging) for complete debugging workflow.
  3576→
  3577→---
  3578→
  3579→## Anti-patterns
  3580→- Multiple domains in one store.
  3581→- Storing functions/Promises/DOM elements in persisted state.
  3582→- Calling Dexie directly from components (use services).
  3583→- Redux or other state libraries (Zustand only).
  3584→- Complex refactoring without planning (use **`/create-plan`** for multi-store changes).
  3585→- Debugging persistence issues manually (use **`debug-like-expert`** for systematic approach).
  3586→
  3587→## Tests
  3588→Location: `src/stores/__tests__/`. Use Vitest. Mock IndexedDB for unit tests.
  3589→
  3590→## Related
  3591→- Services: `src/services/CLAUDE.md`
  3592→- Development principles: `docs/technical/development_principles.md`
  3593→# src/widgets/CLAUDE.md — Widget System
  3594→
  3595→## Scope
  3596→Widget components under `src/widgets/`. Dashboard widgets live in `Dashboard/`, other feature widgets in their respective folders (Kanban, NotesEditor, etc.).
  3597→
  3598→## Widget Categories
  3599→| Category | Examples |
  3600→|----------|----------|
  3601→| **core** | MyDayWidget, TasksSummaryWidget, RecentNotesWidget, FormWidget |
  3602→| **productivity** | PomodoroWidget, QuickAddWidget, TasksQuickAddWidget, UpcomingEventsWidget |
  3603→| **news** | HackerNewsWidget, ProductHuntWidget, AINewsWidget, DevToWidget, RedditWidget |
  3604→| **finance** | CryptoWidget, CurrencyWidget, StockMarketWidget |
  3605→| **fun** | JokeWidget, FactsWidget, QuoteWidget, BoredWidget, TypingTestWidget, MemoryGameWidget |
  3606→| **visual** | ColorPaletteWidget, PixelArtWidget, UnsplashWidget, MotivationalWidget |
  3607→| **dev** | GitHubWidget, GitHubTrendingWidget, AwesomeListsWidget, RepoStatsWidget, PackageStatsWidget |
  3608→| **utility** | WeatherForecastWidget, WorldClockWidget, CalculatorWidget, UnitConverterWidget, QRCodeWidget, CountdownWidget, ClipboardWidget, IPInfoWidget, DictionaryWidget, WikipediaWidget, ShortcutsWidget, UptimeWidget, AirQualityWidget, AnalyticsWidget, TabManagerWidget |
  3609→
  3610→## Key Files
  3611→- `Dashboard/BaseWidget.tsx` — Standard wrapper (loading/error/refresh states)
  3612→- `Dashboard/WidgetRegistry.ts` — Widget discovery + metadata registry
  3613→- `Kanban.tsx`, `NotesEditor.tsx`, `Weather.tsx`, `WeatherMap.tsx`, `Map.tsx` — Feature widgets
  3614→
  3615→## Kanban Subfolders
  3616→| Folder | Purpose | Files |
  3617→|--------|---------|-------|
  3618→| `Kanban/tabs/` | Tab content components for CardDetailPanel | `ActivityTabContent`, `AttachmentsTabContent`, `ChecklistTabContent`, `CommentsTabContent`, `SubtasksTabContent`, `TimeTrackingTabContent` |
  3619→| `Kanban/sections/` | Metadata section components for CardDetailPanel | `TaskDatesSection`, `TaskDependenciesSection` |
  3620→
  3621→## Settings Subfolders
  3622→| Folder | Purpose | Files |
  3623→|--------|---------|-------|
  3624→| `Settings/` | Settings page section components | `AITerminalSettingsSection`, `CalendarImportExportSection`, `CalendarNotificationsSection`, `DashboardSettingsSection`, `ProjectSettings`, `StorageInfoSection`, `WidgetSettingsSection` |
  3625→
  3626→## Non-negotiables
  3627→- **Use `BaseWidget`:** All dashboard widgets must wrap in `BaseWidget` for consistent UX.
  3628→- **Lazy loading:** Widgets discovered via Vite glob in `WidgetRegistry.ts`. Never import directly.
  3629→- **Registration required:** Add to `WIDGET_FILE_NAMES` + `WIDGET_REGISTRY` with: `id`, `title`, `category`, `icon`, `description`.
  3630→- **Semantic tokens:** Use Tailwind semantic classes. No hardcoded colors/sizes.
  3631→- **Effect-based fetching:** Network calls in `useEffect` with cancellation handling.
  3632→- **Accessibility:** ARIA labels, keyboard nav, visible focus.
  3633→
  3634→## Adding a Dashboard Widget
  3635→
  3636→**For complex widgets** (new category, multiple features, external API integration):
  3637→1. Use **`/create-plan`** to design architecture before coding
  3638→2. Plan should cover: component structure, data fetching, error handling, accessibility, bundle size
  3639→
  3640→**For standard widgets:**
  3641→1. Create `Dashboard/MyWidget.tsx`:
  3642→```tsx
  3643→export default function MyWidget() {
  3644→  const [data, setData] = useState<T | null>(null);
  3645→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
  3646→
  3647→  useEffect(() => {
  3648→    let cancelled = false;
  3649→    async function load() {
  3650→      try {
  3651→        const result = await fetchData();
  3652→        if (!cancelled) setData(result);
  3653→      } catch (err) {
  3654→        if (!cancelled) setError(String(err));
  3655→      }
  3656→    }
  3657→    load();
  3658→    return () => { cancelled = true; };
  3659→  }, []);
  3660→
  3661→  if (error) return <ErrorMessage message={error} />;
  3662→  if (!data) return <Loader />;
  3663→
  3664→  return (
  3665→    <BaseWidget title="My Widget">
  3666→      {/* Content */}
  3667→    </BaseWidget>
  3668→  );
  3669→}
  3670→```
  3671→2. Add `'MyWidget'` to `WIDGET_FILE_NAMES` in `WidgetRegistry.ts`
  3672→3. Add registry entry with metadata
  3673→4. Run `npm run build` to verify bundle size
  3674→5. For performance issues, use **`/consider:pareto`** to identify high-impact optimizations
  3675→
  3676→See [Tache Integration Guide § Widget Development](../../docs/technical/tache-integration-guide.md#workflow-widget-development) for complete workflow.
  3677→
  3678→## Anti-patterns
  3679→- Registering widgets outside `WidgetRegistry.ts`.
  3680→- Network calls in component body (use `useEffect`).
  3681→- Hardcoded pixel sizes (use responsive units).
  3682→- Heavy main-thread computation (offload to workers or memoize).
  3683→- Global variables for state (use stores or local state).
  3684→- Complex widget development without planning (use **`/create-plan`** for multi-phase widgets).
  3685→- Over-engineering widget APIs (use **`/consider:via-negativa`** to simplify config).
  3686→
  3687→## Related
  3688→- Widget guide: `docs/technical/widget_development_guide.md`
  3689→- Stores: `src/stores/CLAUDE.md`
  3690→# tests/CLAUDE.md — Testing Guidelines
  3691→
  3692→## Scope
  3693→Test infrastructure for Neuman Brain. Unit/integration tests use Vitest + Testing Library. E2E tests use Playwright.
  3694→
  3695→## Test Locations
  3696→| Type | Location | Runner |
  3697→|------|----------|--------|
  3698→| Unit/Integration | `src/**/__tests__/*.test.ts` | Vitest |
  3699→| Store tests | `src/stores/__tests__/` | Vitest |
  3700→| Component tests | `src/components/__tests__/` | Vitest + Testing Library |
  3701→| E2E tests | `tests/e2e/*.spec.ts` | Playwright |
  3702→| Test fixtures | `tests/fixtures/` | Shared test utilities |
  3703→
  3704→## Commands
  3705→```bash
  3706→# Vitest (unit/integration)
  3707→npm run test              # Run all tests
  3708→npm run test:ui           # Interactive UI mode
  3709→npm run test:coverage     # With coverage report
  3710→
  3711→# Playwright (E2E)
  3712→npx playwright test                    # Run all E2E tests
  3713→npx playwright test --ui               # Interactive UI mode
  3714→npx playwright test --project=chromium # Specific browser
  3715→npx playwright show-report             # View HTML report
  3716→```
  3717→
  3718→## Non-negotiables
  3719→- **Test with changes:** New features/fixes should include tests. Justify skipping.
  3720→- **Determinism:** Mock network (`msw`), timers (`vi.useFakeTimers()`), dates. No external dependencies.
  3721→- **Isolation:** Each test resets state. Use `beforeEach`/`afterEach` for cleanup.
  3722→- **No skipped tests:** Don't commit `it.skip`. Fix or remove.
  3723→- **Semantic assertions:** Prefer behavior checks over snapshots.
  3724→
  3725→## Unit Test Pattern
  3726→```ts
  3727→import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from 'vitest';
  3728→
  3729→describe('featureName', () => {
  3730→  beforeEach(() => {
  3731→    vi.clearAllMocks();
  3732→  });
  3733→
  3734→  it('does expected behavior', () => {
  3735→    const result = functionUnderTest(input);
  3736→    expect(result).toBe(expectedOutput);
  3737→  });
  3738→});
  3739→```
  3740→
  3741→## Component Test Pattern
  3742→```tsx
  3743→import { render, screen } from '@testing-library/react';
  3744→import userEvent from '@testing-library/user-event';
  3745→import { MyComponent } from '@/components/MyComponent';
  3746→
  3747→describe('MyComponent', () => {
  3748→  it('renders and handles interaction', async () => {
  3749→    const user = userEvent.setup();
  3750→    render(<MyComponent />);
  3751→
  3752→    await user.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /submit/i }));
  3753→    expect(screen.getByText('Success')).toBeInTheDocument();
  3754→  });
  3755→});
  3756→```
  3757→
  3758→## E2E Test Pattern (Playwright)
  3759→```ts
  3760→import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';
  3761→
  3762→test.describe('critical flow', () => {
  3763→  test('user can complete action', async ({ page }) => {
  3764→    await page.goto('/');
  3765→    await page.getByRole('button', { name: /action/i }).click();
  3766→    await expect(page.getByText('Result')).toBeVisible();
  3767→  });
  3768→});
  3769→```
  3770→
  3771→## E2E Test Files
  3772→| File | Coverage | Tests | Priority |
  3773→|------|----------|-------|----------|
  3774→| `app.spec.ts` | App basics, navigation, theme, accessibility | Basic app functionality | Baseline |
  3775→| `data-persistence.spec.ts` | IndexedDB persistence across refreshes, offline mode, large datasets | 5 tests | #1 (30% bugs) |
  3776→| `cross-feature-integration.spec.ts` | Task→Calendar→Timer, Automation, Wikilinks→Graph, Custom Fields→CSV | 6 tests | #2 (25% bugs) |
  3777→| `backup-restore.spec.ts` | Data export/import round-trip, edge cases, schema migration | 4 tests | #3 (15% bugs) |
  3778→| `automation-engine.spec.ts` | Rule execution, loop prevention, conditional triggers | 4 tests | #4 (10% bugs) |
  3779→| `recurring-tasks-events.spec.ts` | RRULE patterns, leap years, DST, exception dates | 5 tests | #5 (8% bugs) |
  3780→
  3781→**Total E2E Coverage:** 24 tests covering 88% of critical bug scenarios (Pareto-optimized).
  3782→
  3783→## Critical Flows for E2E
  3784→Prioritize these for E2E coverage:
  3785→- Backup/restore round-trip
  3786→- Task CRUD operations
  3787→- Event creation + recurrence
  3788→- Automation rule execution
  3789→- Schema migrations
  3790→
  3791→## Debugging Test Failures
  3792→
  3793→**For test debugging** (failures, flakiness, unexpected behavior):
  3794→- Use **`/debug`** for clear test failures with reproducible steps
  3795→- Use **`debug-like-expert` skill** for intermittent/flaky tests
  3796→- Example: "Test fails only in CI, passes locally"
  3797→
  3798→**For understanding recurring test failures**:
  3799→- Use **`/consider:5-whys`** to drill to root cause
  3800→- Example: "Why do E2E tests keep failing on navigation?"
  3801→
  3802→**For test coverage optimization** (prioritizing what to test):
  3803→- Use **`/consider:pareto`** to identify 20% of tests covering 80% of value
  3804→- Focus on critical paths, high-risk areas
  3805→
  3806→See [Tache Integration Guide § Testing Workflows](../../docs/technical/tache-integration-guide.md#workflow-testing) for complete debugging patterns.
  3807→
  3808→---
  3809→
  3810→## Anti-patterns
  3811→- Real API calls (use mocks/msw).
  3812→- `waitForTimeout` sleeps (wait for elements).
  3813→- Full DOM snapshots (brittle).
  3814→- Testing framework internals.
  3815→- Debugging flaky tests manually (use **`debug-like-expert`** for systematic approach).
  3816→
  3817→## Setup Files
  3818→| File | Purpose |
  3819→|------|---------|
  3820→| `playwright.config.ts` | Playwright configuration |
  3821→| `config/vitest.config.ts` | Vitest configuration |
  3822→| `tests/fixtures/test-utils.ts` | Shared E2E helpers |
  3823→| `src/vitest-env.d.ts` | Vitest type definitions |
  3824→
  3825→## Related
  3826→- Services (primary test targets): `src/services/CLAUDE.md`
  3827→- Stores (primary test targets): `src/stores/CLAUDE.md`
  3828→# CLAUDE.md - Developer Guide for tdr.portfolio
  3829→
  3830→**Last Updated:** November 19, 2025
  3831→**Project:** thedeadrobot Landing Page + X Automation
  3832→**Tech Stack:** Pure HTML/CSS/JS (no frameworks)
  3833→**Primary Goal:** Minimalist gateway to thedeadrobot brand + automated AI persona on X
  3834→
  3835→---
  3836→
  3837→## 🎯 Project Overview
  3838→
  3839→**What This Is:**
  3840→A simple, elegant landing page (thedeadrobot.com) serving as a hub for:
  3841→- X (Twitter): [@thedeadrobot](https://x.com/thedeadrobot)

  3854→
  3855→## 📁 Project Structure
  3856→
  3857→```
  3858→tdr.portfolio/
  3859→├── index.html              # Landing page (36 lines)
  3860→├── style.css               # Black/green cyberpunk aesthetic (131 lines)

  3879→
  3880→---
  3881→
  3882→## 🧠 Core Philosophy
  3883→
  3884→### 1. Brutal Simplicity
  3885→- **No frameworks** - HTML/CSS/JS is enough
  3886→- **No build step** - Edit, save, deploy
  3887→- **No dependencies** - Zero npm packages
  3888→- **Fast loading** - < 50KB total page weight
  3889→
  3890→### 2. Perfect Execution
  3891→- Every line intentional
  3892→- No commented-out code
  3893→- Clean, documented, maintainable
  3894→- Mobile-first responsive design
  3895→
  3896→### 3. Brand Consistency
  3897→- Cyberpunk aesthetic (black + neon green)
  3898→- Roboto font (clean, robotic)
  3899→- Minimalist interactions
  3900→- Post-human vibe
  3901→
  3902→### 4. Automation First
  3903→- X account runs itself (AI-generated posts)
  3904→- GitHub Actions orchestration
  3905→- Zero cost ($0.00/month - completely free)
  3906→- Topic approval system (teach only what you know)
  3907→- Human-in-the-loop for quality
  3908→
  3909→---
  3910→
  3911→## 🔧 Development Rules & Workflow
  3912→
  3913→**Critical Foundation:** This project is ~1% complete. Every decision—documentation structure, code architecture, feature design—must build the perfect foundation for the other 99%. No shortcuts. No "good enough." Perfect foundation, always.
  3914→
  3915→### The 15 Sacred Rules
  3916→
  3917→Every development session follows these rules without exception. They ensure quality, consistency, and maintainability as the project scales.
  3918→
  3919→---
  3920→
  3921→#### **Planning & Task Management (Rules 1-7)**
  3922→
  3923→**Rule 1: Think First, Plan Second**
  3924→```bash
  3925→# Before touching ANY code:
  3926→1. Read relevant files to understand current state
  3927→2. Think through the problem deeply
  3928→3. Write comprehensive plan to tasks/todo.md
  3929→4. Include context, decisions, tradeoffs
  3930→```
  3931→
  3932→**Rule 2: Todo Lists Are Required**
  3933→Every plan must have checkable todo items:
  3934→```markdown
  3935→## Todo List
  3936→- [ ] Phase 1: Research and analysis
  3937→  - [ ] Read file X
  3938→  - [ ] Understand system Y
  3939→- [ ] Phase 2: Implementation
  3940→  - [ ] Create feature A
  3941→  - [ ] Update docs B
  3942→```
  3943→
  3944→**Rule 3: Checkpoint Before Execution**
  3945→```
  3946→STOP. Get user approval before proceeding.
  3947→- Review the plan with user
  3948→- Clarify any ambiguities
  3949→- Confirm approach
  3950→- THEN execute
  3951→```
  3952→
  3953→**Rule 4: Mark Progress As You Go**
  3954→```markdown
  3955→- [x] Phase 1: Research ✅
  3956→- [x] Task A complete ✅
  3957→- [ ] Task B in progress... ⏳
  3958→```
  3959→Update tasks/todo.md in real-time. Keep user informed.
  3960→
  3961→**Rule 5: High-Level Progress Updates**
  3962→Don't spam details. Give clear, concise updates:
  3963→```
  3964→✅ Added homelab topics to approved_topics.json (22 new topics)
  3965→✅ Updated generate_post.py system prompt (restored cryptic voice)
  3966→⏳ Testing new prompt with dry run...
  3967→```
  3968→
  3969→**Rule 6: Simplicity Always**
  3970→```
  3971→BAD:  Massive refactor touching 15 files
  3972→GOOD: Targeted change to 2 relevant files
  3973→
  3974→BAD:  Complex abstraction "for future flexibility"
  3975→GOOD: Simple solution that solves today's problem
  3976→
  3977→Make every change as simple as humanly possible.
  3978→Impact only necessary code. Introduce zero bugs.
  3979→```
  3980→
  3981→**Rule 7: Review Section Required**
  3982→At end of tasks/todo.md, add comprehensive review:
  3983→```markdown
  3984→## Review
  3985→
  3986→**What Changed:**
  3987→- Added 15 development rules to CLAUDE.md
  3988→- Updated character voice in generate_post.py
  3989→- Added 22 homelab topics
  3990→
  3991→**Why:**
  3992→- Rules ensure consistent quality as project scales
  3993→- Voice restoration aligns with brand identity
  3994→- Homelab topics expand content breadth
  3995→
  3996→**Impact:**
  3997→- Development process now documented and repeatable
  3998→- Automation voice is cryptic yet educational
  3999→- 22 new topics for content generation
